## Older (Cycle 305 вАФ adult build ¬Ј DEEPEN, Mirror-Canon cycle 2)

- **[/dream/302-mirror-canon-round](/dream/302-mirror-canon-round)** вАФ Mirror Canon (Round вЗД Phase). `demoable`
  **Cycle 2 of the lab's Mirror-Canon thread (a deepening of the standout `287-mirror-choir`).** The piece is otherwise unchanged вАФ conduct a four-voice round sung by past versions of yourself, in a matte wooden mirror вАФ but it now has a **Round вЗД Phase mode toggle** that folds in the banked sibling `301-mirror-canon-phase`'s engine rather than shipping a separate piece. In **Round** mode every committed past-you enters offset on one locked bar grid (a true canon, no drift). Flip to **Phase** and each voice's loop is stretched by `(1 + n¬Ј0.012)`√Ч the bar and clocked from its own commit time вАФ **Steve Reich's *Piano Phase*** вАФ so the past-yous gradually slip in and out of phase with one another and **the round literally never repeats** (genuinely different at minute 2 than at second 5). A live **drift HUD** shows each voice's loop position as a tinted marker: in Round they hold their fixed offsets, in Phase they slide apart. Both modes share the same record/commit, conduct (mute/solo), ghost-fallback (auto-commits two voices so it demos itself with no camera), and Rozin wooden-mirror render. This advances the lab's **2nd genuine multi-cycle thread** to its planned cycle 2 вАФ the direct answer to the JURY's #1 standing provocation ("stop shipping orphans; pick ONE thing and deepen it over 2вАУ3 cycles"). **Ambition 2/5**: multi-cycle-commitment(#4) + named-reference(#3: Steve Reich *Piano Phase*, atop 287's Rozin/round/Frippertronics lineage). **Diversity**: a deliberate **continue-a-multi-cycle-build** (the mandate's explicit carve-out); the NEW axis carrying the gate is the phasing TECHNIQUE, and INPUT (camera/body) ¬Ј VIBE (adult/embodied) still dodge the standing banned touch/three.js/pentatonic-poke. Adult ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API route ¬Ј no guard ¬Ј 7.51 kB (was 6.89). Born from cyc303's banked sibling + RESEARCH ¬І303. **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вАФ unverified: the *feel* of the drift rate (`PHASE_DRIFT` 0.012) on real hardware, whether the phase cloud reads as "my selves slipping apart" vs. a wash, and (carried) MediaPipe CDN reliability + formant intelligibility at 4 stacked voices.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/302-mirror-canon-round/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 304 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE, 3 explorers)

- **[/dream/303-kids-wind-harp](/dream/303-kids-wind-harp)** вАФ Kids' Wind-Harp. `demoable`
  **Tip the world.** Hit one big button, then **tilt your phone or iPad** вАФ gravity swings a row of seven glowing strings like a wind-harp, and any string that swings far enough **plucks itself and sings**. The child plays music by *tipping the world*, hands-free, no tapping, no reading, no way to lose. The lab's **first tilt-input piece since the loved `83-kids-tilt-rain`**, and a deliberate pull on three of Karel's loved prototypes (`83-kids-tilt-rain`вЭ§пЄП tilt, `105-pluck-field`вЭ§пЄП Karplus pluck, `140-kids-string-bridge`вЭ§пЄП playable strings). Two genuinely **fresh-for-this-lab techniques** coupled into one toy: (1) **Verlet-integration string physics** вАФ each of the 7 strings is a 14-node mass-chain solved with Verlet + distance-constraint relaxation (3 passes/frame) on a **fixed 120 Hz step**; the device-tilt `deviceorientation` ќ≤/ќ≥ becomes a **gravity vector**, so tipping the tablet literally changes which way "down" is and the hanging strings swing and settle with believable physical motion; (2) **Karplus-Strong plucked-string synthesis** (Karplus & Strong, 1983) вАФ when a string's midpoint swing crosses a threshold it plucks: a noise burst rendered offline through a tuned, lowpass-fed delay line into an `AudioBuffer`, bigger swing вЖТ louder + brighter + longer, per-string refractory so it never machine-guns, stereo-panned lowвЖТhigh across the row. Three subsystems вЖТ one pipeline (physics + synth + a hand-written **raw WebGL2** ribbon renderer вАФ matte alpha-over string curves expanded along their normals on a soft breathing dark gradient, a glow flash on each pluck, **no additive bloom, no three.js**). The 7 strings are tuned to a warm **D-Dorian** scale (D E F G A B C вАФ explicitly **NOT** C-major-pentatonic), with an ambient D+A drone underneath and a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter so a fistful of simultaneous plucks can never blast. Degrades at every step so it's **fully playable on a desktop with no sensors**: iOS 13+ `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` is called inside the Start gesture (denial вЖТ readable `text-rose-300` note + auto-fallback); **pointer-drag** tilts the gravity vector on desktop; and a **2 s auto-sway** kicks in when no tilt events arrive so the harp **plays itself** and a 4-year-old (or Karel at 06:30) always sees causeвЖТeffect. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; the two siblings вАФ `299-kids-clap-band` (clap вЖТ HFC-onset-detection layered groove, Steve Reich *Clapping Music*) and `300-kids-blow-sail` (blow вЖТ Wiener-entropy breath-envelope sailing) вАФ are build-reviewed and re-banked in IDEAS.md. Chains from RESEARCH ¬І304 (*Rhythm in the Air*, arXiv:2511.00793, Nov 2025 вАФ embodied motion вЖТ scale-constrained musical output). **Ambition 2/5**: never-used-technique(#1: Verlet rope physics вАФ 0 prior INDEX/README hits) + named-reference(#3: Aeolian harp; Karplus-Strong 1983). **Diversity** (dodges the standing banned touch/three.js/matte-Canvas2D/pentatonic-poke): INPUT=device tilt ¬Ј OUTPUT=raw-WebGL2 ribbons ¬Ј TECHNIQUE=Verlet physics + Karplus-Strong ¬Ј VIBE=kids/calm/modal. For kids 4+ ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API route ¬Ј no guard. **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вАФ unverified surface: the deviceorientationвЖТgravity *feel* and pluck threshold on a real tilting hand, the iOS permission branches, and the rendered look of the ribbon strips (no sensors/GPU in the sandbox).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/303-kids-wind-harp/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 300 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE, 3 explorers)

- **[/dream/295-kids-shadow-dance](/dream/295-kids-shadow-dance)** вАФ Kids' Shadow Dance. `demoable`
  **Stand back, hit one big button, and *dance with your whole body* вАФ wherever you move, a dusk meadow blooms, leaves glowing light-trails, and sings. Nothing to tap, no way to be wrong; the only instruction is *move*.** The lab's **first kids whole-body / camera piece** вАФ it answers the 2026-06-03 jury's #2 provocation head-on ("ban touch for kids; make the child *move* or *sing*, not poke") and escapes the C-pentatonic rut Karel flagged (it's tuned to **G-Lydian**, not pentatonic). On **Start dancing** it requests the camera and runs **frame-difference optical-flow** with **zero dependencies, no MediaPipe, no skeleton**: each frame is drawn to a tiny **32√Ч24** offscreen canvas, per-cell Rec.601 luminance is differenced against the previous frame (noise-gated + lightly smoothed) вЖТ a **motion field** robust to a constantly-wiggling 4-year-old. That field is summarised to three numbers вАФ `energy` (total motion), `heightY` (centre-of-motion height), `spawn` (hot-cell count) вАФ and packed into an **RG8 texture** (R=motion, G=silhouette luma). Three subsystems feed one pipeline: (1) the motion-field analyser; (2) a **raw WebGL2 `#version 300 es`** two-pass renderer вАФ a **ping-pong RGBA8 framebuffer trail-accumulation** pass (`newTrail = max(oldTrail¬Јdecay, freshMotion)`) so motion leaves glowing light-trails, then a dusk skyвЖТmeadow scene shader with blooms from hot cells, swaying grass, quiet stars, and a faint low-res **self-silhouette composite** (a cool glowing presence, never a hard cutout, mirror-flipped so it reads like a mirror); (3) a fully-synthesised **Web Audio** ensemble вАФ a breathing pad always underneath plus triangle+octave bloom voices on a **three-octave G-Lydian** scale (Lydian's raised 4th = floaty dusk, every note consonant), with `energy` вЖТ pad swell + lowpass opening + denser blooms + occasional stacked harmonies, `heightY` вЖТ register, `spawn` вЖТ bloom count, all through a soft lowpass + `DynamicsCompressor` limiter so it can **never** get harsh however wildly the child dances. Camera is **analysis-only** вАФ frames are reduced to motion numbers and discarded, never recorded or sent (no network, no API route, no guard). Degrades at every step: **no camera / denied** вЖТ a hand-authored **ghost dancer** (a soft blob that figure-eights, jumps, and flails) drives the *identical* motion-fieldвЖТAV pipeline so it's fully demoable and never silent (amber notice); **no WebGL2** вЖТ readable rose notice and the music keeps playing. WIDE winner over two banked siblings вАФ `294-kids-voice-garden` (sing a garden into bloom, autocorrelation pitch-detection + sing-back memory) and `296-kids-firefly-tilt` (tilt a firefly to wake stars; the sky remembers your path and replays it as a lullaby) вАФ both build-verified, full specs in IDEAS.md. Chains from RESEARCH ¬І300 (CHI 2026 movement-sonification workshop) + recent-research IDEAS seed. **Ambition 3/5**: вЙ•3-subsystems + named-reference + recent-research. **Diversity** (dodges the jury's banned touch/three.js/matte-Canvas2D + the pentatonic rut): INPUT=camera/whole-body ¬Ј OUTPUT=raw-WebGL2 trail-meadow ¬Ј TECHNIQUE=frame-difference optical-flow motion-field ¬Ј VIBE=kids/dusk/embodied. References: **√Йmile Jaques-Dalcroze, *Eurhythmics*** (music through whole-body movement); **Myron Krueger, *Videoplace*** (1985, foundational full-body video interaction); **Frid & Bresin**, interactive sonification of children's movement (*Frontiers in Neuroscience*, 2016). For kids 4+ ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API route ¬Ј no guard ¬Ј 7.08 kB. Build-verified, not browser-verified.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/295-kids-shadow-dance/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 298 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE, 3 explorers)

- **[/dream/293-kids-sky-band](/dream/293-kids-sky-band)** вАФ Kids' Sky Band. `demoable`
  **A tiny band that plays the *real sky outside your window right now* вАФ so it sounds a little different every day, because the weather is real.** The lab's **FIRST kids real-world-data piece** and **first weather source** (prior real-world pieces вАФ 279 earthquakes, 288 aurora вАФ were adult/cosmic). On Start it fetches live conditions from keyless, CORS-open **Open-Meteo** via `navigator.geolocation` (3s timeout вЖТ San Francisco fallback вЖТ bundled `SAMPLE_WEATHER` if offline), then drives a four-voice **C-major-pentatonic** ensemble (no wrong notes) through a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter: **Sun** = warm bell (higher/brighter by day, from `temperature_2m`+`is_day`), **Cloud** = soft sine pad whose level+lowpass tracks `cloud_cover`, **Wind** = filtered-noise whoosh on an LFO from `wind_speed_10m`, **Rain** = gentle pentatonic plinks whose density follows `precipitation`; tempo nudged by temperature; a 12-min lullaby fade. The sky itself is a **single raw WebGL2 `#version 300 es` fragment shader** (one full-screen quad) fed the *same* live numbers: gradient dawn/day/dusk/night palette from `is_day`+temp, fbm clouds at `cloud_cover`, a sun/moon disc with a distance-field smiley face, rain streaks, wind-driven drift, twinkling night stars. **Plays fully hands-free** вАФ the jury banned touch input for this kids cycle, so there's nothing to poke (tapping a sky-friend to solo it is an optional bonus). Starts instantly on the bundled sample, swaps to live data when it resolves, and degrades at every step (no geo вЖТ SF ¬Ј no network вЖТ sample + amber notice ¬Ј no WebGL2 вЖТ audio-only + readable rose notice). WIDE winner over `294-kids-voice-garden` (singвЖТbloom) + `295-kids-shadow-dance` (danceвЖТbloom), both banked in IDEAS. Born from RESEARCH ¬І298 (DATASONICA 2026 award; RIT Weather Chimes Apr 2026). Ref: **John Luther Adams, *The Place Where You Go to Listen*** (Museum of the North, Fairbanks); Open-Meteo. For kids 4+ ¬Ј real-world-data input (hands-free) ¬Ј raw-WebGL2 fragment-shader output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API route ¬Ј no guard ¬Ј 7.73 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/293-kids-sky-band/README.md`

- **[/dream/286-kids-jelly-choir](/dream/286-kids-jelly-choir)** вАФ Jelly Choir. `demoable`
  **Five wobbly candy-colored jellies with googly eyes sit in a row вАФ poke one (or drag it) and it stretches, springs back, and *sings its own wobble*; poke two at once and they harmonize.** The lab's **first mass-spring / Verlet soft-body вЖТ audio** instrument. Each jelly is a ring of 14 point-masses held in a circle by **radial** springs (each point вЖТ center) and **structural** springs (each point вЖТ its neighbors); poke a point and **Verlet integration** carries your velocity, so on release the blob overshoots and wobbles back вАФ real soft-body physics, not a canned animation. **The sound *is* the wobble**: every frame we measure the jelly's *deformation energy* (mean stray of each surface point from its rest circle) and that one number drives the voice вАФ **energy¬≤ вЖТ loudness** (silent at rest), **energy вЖТ low-pass brightness**, **energy вЖТ vibrato depth + rate** вАФ so the shimmer tracks the jiggle in real time. **Tuning = just intonation** (1/1, 9/8, 5/4, 3/2, 2/1 over a 196 Hz G3 root): consonant *pure* intervals, deliberately **not** C-major pentatonic, so poking two jellies rings out a clean justly-tuned interval with a soft glowing thread drawn between them. **Output = inline animated SVG** (Catmull-Rom blob bodies вЖТ cubic B√©ziers, Gaussian-blur candy glow, eyes as an HTML overlay, a mouth path that opens with energy) вАФ **no `<canvas>` anywhere**, so it dodges the canvas2d 5√Ч ban and is the lowest-risk render path for a 06:30 demo on any device. Multi-touch (two kids, two hands); always-on root+fifth drone so it's never silent; `AudioContext` created on first poke (mobile autoplay), and the jellies still wobble silently if audio can't start. **Resurrected from the cycle-292 WIDE physical-modeling fire** (it was the `kids-jelly-choir` sibling, build-verified then banked); born from RESEARCH ¬І292. References: **nlm: Real-Time Non-linear Modal Synthesis in Max, arXiv 2603.10240 (2026)**; **Provot, *Deformation Constraints in a Mass-Spring Model* (1995)**; **M√Љller et al., *Position-Based Dynamics* (2007)**. For kids 4+ ¬Ј touch (poke/drag) input ¬Ј inline-SVG output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј no guard ¬Ј 3.57 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/286-kids-jelly-choir/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 293 вАФ adult build ¬Ј solo)

- **[/dream/285-mosaic-listener](/dream/285-mosaic-listener)** вАФ Mosaic Listener. `demoable`
  **Press Begin, then drag your finger across a glowing map of sounds вАФ the piece is re-assembled in real time out of whichever grains sit nearest your finger, music made of shards and never the original recording.** The lab's **first concatenative-synthesis / audio-mosaicing piece** in 280+ prototypes: where everything else reacts to or synthesizes sound, this one takes an existing recording, **shatters it into hundreds of ~165 ms grains**, tags each with three descriptors вАФ **loudness** (RMS), **brightness** (spectral centroid via a 256-pt Hann DFT), and a rough **pitch** (zero-crossing, for hue) вАФ and lays them out as a 2-D **descriptor atlas** (x = brightness, y = loudness, color = pitch class). A target wanders the atlas; you **drag** to place it (CataRT's "play the cloud by hand"), let it **auto-drift** along a slow Lissajous (the self-playing demo), or **hum into the mic** so your voice's live brightness+loudness becomes the target. A Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler fires ~6вАУ9 grains/sec, finding the **kвЙИ6 nearest grains** and picking one at random (organic, not robotic), windowed (12 ms attack / 60 ms release) through lowpass вЖТ feedback-delay wash вЖТ limiter; the chosen grain lights up and a trail draws your path. **Corpus = Karel's real piano**: type a **Welcome Home** track ID and *Use Karel's piano as the corpus* вЖТ read-only `fetch("/api/audio/:id")` (the loved `163`/`227` pattern), decode, re-slice вАФ so the shards are literally his playing (non-pentatonic by source). **Output is matte WebGL2** (Canvas2D was banned this cycle at 5√Ч; the 2026-06-02 jury hard-banned additive/glowing point clouds) вАФ raw `GL_POINTS` with **normal premultiplied-alpha blending**, soft matte dots on near-black, recently-played grains brighten modestly with **no additive glow**. Degrades gracefully: a **procedural piano corpus** is synthesized on Begin so it always runs with no network/no key/no permission; track-load failure falls back with an amber notice; mic-block вЖТ drag/drift with a rose notice; no-WebGL keeps the audio mosaic playing. **Born from this cycle's research dive** (RESEARCH ¬І293). References: **Diemo Schwarz вАФ CataRT** (interactive corpus-based concatenative synthesis, IRCAM); **"The Concatenator," arXiv 2411.04366 (2024)**; **Lee & Pasquier, "Musical Agent Systems: MACAT and MACataRT," arXiv 2502.00023 (2025)**; **FluCoMa** (live web audio mosaicing). Adult ¬Ј drag / mic / auto-drift input ¬Ј matte WebGL2 output ¬Ј reads `/api/audio` only ¬Ј no guard ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј 6.28 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/285-mosaic-listener/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 292 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/284-kids-thunder-drum](/dream/284-kids-thunder-drum)** вАФ Thunder Drum. `demoable`
  **Strike a big drum *skin* anywhere with a finger вАФ hit the center for a deep round boom, the rim for a bright slap, and hit it *hard* to hear the head's tension bend the pitch up before it settles (the "bwooOOWww" of a thunder-drum / tympani).** This is the lab's **first playable physical-modeling / non-linear modal-synthesis instrument** in 280+ prototypes. Every other kids toy plays a Karplus pluck, a triangle bell, or an additive sine locked to **C-major pentatonic**; here the pitches are **circular-membrane eigenmodes** вАФ the Bessel-zero ratios `[1.0, 1.594, 2.136, 2.296, 2.653, 2.918, 3.156, 3.5] √Ч f0`, inharmonic and **non-pentatonic by physics**, with higher modes decaying faster so it rings like a struck skin, not a glockenspiel. **The signature is the tension pitch-glide**: a hard/fast strike starts all partials up to **+6% sharp** then exponentially relaxes them to rest pitch over 120вАУ260 ms via `setTargetAtTime` вАФ the non-linear behavior straight from this cycle's research anchor. **Strike position is timbre**: a tap radius (0=center вЖТ 1=rim) reweights the partial bank вАФ center hits dark and round, edge hits bright and slappy. Four drum zones sit on a just-intonation spread (1/1¬Ј4/3¬Ј3/2¬Ј2/1 over a 110 Hz root) so zones are consonant yet unmistakably not a pentatonic toy. **Visual = three.js** (hard non-canvas2d constraint this cycle): a tilted `CircleGeometry` skin whose vertices are displaced by a **travelling, rim-pinned radial standing-wave ripple** from each contact point (multi-strike = overlapping ripples), bold saturated color per zone, expanding glow rings, a soft always-on drone so it's never silent, multi-touch via Pointer Events; no WebGL вЖТ a readable `text-rose-300` notice. **Born from this cycle's research dive** (RESEARCH ¬І292) вАФ real-time physical-modeling synthesis вАФ and the deepest answer yet to the standing JURY "audit the SOUND" mandate (it diversifies the *synthesis method*, not just the scale, going beyond the recent tuning-only breaks at `272`/`276`/`280`). References: **nlm: Real-Time Non-linear Modal Synthesis in Max, arXiv 2603.10240 (2026)**; **Lord Rayleigh, *Theory of Sound* (1877)** (circular-membrane Bessel modes); **Morrison & Rossing** (tympani tension nonlinearity). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-builder kids fire (three physical objects √Ч three never-used synthesis methods √Ч three non-canvas2d outputs, all non-pentatonic); siblings `285-kids-singing-bowl` (rub the rim вЖТ **friction/stick-slip** sustain of inharmonic bell partials, three.js metallic bowl + cymatic water) + `286-kids-jelly-choir` (squish a **mass-spring/Verlet soft-body** blob вЖТ it sings its own wobble in just intonation, inline SVG) are **build-verified** (all-three build, exit 0) and banked in IDEAS.md. For kids 4+ ¬Ј touch input ¬Ј three.js output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј no guard ¬Ј 3.69 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/284-kids-thunder-drum/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 291 вАФ adult build ¬Ј WIDE orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/283-piano-isosurface](/dream/283-piano-isosurface)** вАФ Piano Isosurface. `demoable`
  **Tap Start and a glowing 3D form begins breathing to the music вАФ drop an audio file, or paste a Welcome Home track id, and your real piano sculpts a living volume you can drag to orbit.** This is the lab's **first marching-cubes / volumetric isosurface** in 280+ prototypes: where every prior visual drew meshes, particles, shaders, or 2D canvas, this one **reconstructs a surface from a scalar field**. Audio вЖТ one `AnalyserNode` (fftSize 2048) вЖТ 5 frequency bands + RMS + a normalized spectral centroid вЖТ a field of ~8 **metaballs** inside a three.js `MarchingCubes(48)` volume that re-polygonizes a single connected isosurface every frame: **bass** swells a central core, **mids** orbit it at mid radius, **highs** flick small fast flecks at the edges, overall **RMS** makes the whole form breathe (global scale/isolation), and the **spectral centroid** drives material hue (cool/deep when the sound is dark вЖТ warm/bright when brilliant). Rendered with a `MeshStandardMaterial` emissive glow, two directional lights + ambient, fog, slow auto-rotation, and a hand-rolled pointer-drag orbit. **Four audio sources, never silent:** file-drop/picker (`decodeAudioData` вЖТ looping buffer) ¬Ј **mic** (analysis-only, never routed to output, never recorded) ¬Ј **track id** вЖТ read-only `fetch("/api/audio/<id>")` handling both JSON `{url}` and raw bytes (the loved `163-paths-visualizer` вЭ§пЄП pattern вАФ Karel's path to feed his real Welcome Home piano) ¬Ј an always-on generative **D-dorian** synth pad (non-pentatonic) so the surface is alive from the first gesture even with no input. Degrades gracefully: no WebGL вЖТ `text-rose-300` notice; mic denied / decode failure вЖТ rose notice + the pad keeps sculpting. **Born from this cycle's research dive** (RESEARCH ¬І291): GPU isosurface extraction is now *real-time in the browser*, which is exactly what makes a sound-driven, per-frame marching-cubes surface feasible live. References: **William Lorensen & Harvey Cline, "Marching Cubes: A High Resolution 3D Surface Construction Algorithm," SIGGRAPH 1987**; the 2024 WebGPU-marching-cubes lineage (Will Usher / Twinklebear); **Refik Anadol** (sound вЖТ volumetric living form). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-builder adult fire (three orthogonal directions, none using the cycle's banned canvas2d output); siblings `281-midi-harmonograph` (a held chord draws itself as a Victorian harmonograph in raw WebGL2; JI-lock makes consonance a clean closed figure) + `282-ensemble-tabs` (open 2вАУ3 tabs вЖТ one serverless, tempo-locked ensemble on a `Date.now()` shared clock вАФ the lab's first networked piece) are **build-verified** and banked in IDEAS.md. Adult ¬Ј audio-file/mic/track-id input ¬Ј three.js Marching Cubes output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј reads `/api/audio` only ¬Ј no guard ¬Ј 9.97 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/283-piano-isosurface/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 288 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/276-kids-balloon-tritave](/dream/276-kids-balloon-tritave)** вАФ Balloon Sky (Tritave). `demoable`
  **Tilt your tablet to drift a little paper cloud-bird through a dusk sky of cut-paper hot-air balloons вАФ and every balloon it brushes sings in a tuning that has no octave.** This is the lab's **first non-octave / non-pentatonic tuning** in 280+ prototypes: instead of the major-pentatonic-in-12-TET that every "kid-safe" music toy leans on, the balloons are tuned to the **BohlenвАУPierce scale**, whose repeat interval is the **tritave (3:1)** вАФ a perfect twelfth вАФ not the 2:1 octave. It's **equal-tempered BohlenвАУPierce (13 equal divisions of the tritave)**: step ratio `3^(1/13) вЙИ 1.0882` (~146¬Ґ each), `freq(k)=220¬Ј3^(k/13)`. BP was engineered around **odd-harmonic** chords, so three balloons are deliberately seated on the signature **3:5:7 triad** (13-EDT degrees 0/6/10) вАФ brush them together and the chord sounds *rooted*, not random; extra balloons (degrees 3/4/7 + the tritave) give melodic range. The voice is a **clarinet-like additive tone** (mostly ODD partials 1¬Ј3¬Ј5¬Ј7¬Ј9 вАФ the clarinet's chalumeau spectrum is the natural BP timbre, and it overblows at the twelfth, not the octave) over a soft root+tritave **drone bed**, so it's consonant but genuinely *otherworldly* вАФ "nothing sounds wrong" coming from a completely different harmonic universe. **Input = device TILT** (`DeviceOrientationEvent`; iOS permission requested on the first tap) with a mouse/drag fallback so it's fully playable on a laptop at review вАФ a gravity-drift you steer with your hands, not taps. **Output = inline SVG** cut-paper (matte, `feDropShadow` lift + `feTurbulence` grain, animated via rAF through refs вАФ **no canvas, no WebGL, no three.js, no glow**), continuing the loved `268-kids-shadow-theater` cut-paper register. Brushing balloons in a row plays a melody; brushing two at once plays a chord; each brush leaves a paper-confetti puff. Always-on drone (never silent), no score, no "wrong", no fail, ~14-min lullaby fade. **Born from this cycle's research dive** into non-octave tuning (RESEARCH ¬І288). Reference: the **BohlenвАУPierce scale** вАФ independently discovered by **Heinz Bohlen (1972)** and **Max Mathews & John R. Pierce** (Bell Labs, ~1984); composer **Elaine Walker** (ZIA). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-builder kids fire (three unrelated non-pentatonic *sound worlds*, each dodging the cycle's banned tags вАФ touch-input, canvas2d-output, three.js, C-pentatonic); siblings `277-kids-overtone-cave` (hum вЖТ a raw-WebGL crystal grotto sings your **harmonic-series overtones** back, khoomei/Lucier) + `278-kids-dream-flock` (wave at the camera вЖТ frame-diff motion steers a boids flock singing a **whole-tone / Debussy** dream cloud) are **build-verified** and banked in IDEAS.md. For kids 4+ ¬Ј tilt input ¬Ј SVG output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј no guard ¬Ј 4.07 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/276-kids-balloon-tritave/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 287 вАФ adult build ¬Ј DEEP orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/275-memory-loom](/dream/275-memory-loom)** вАФ Memory Loom. `demoable`
  **Play into your mic and the piece quietly records the phrases you play, then weaves them back as an endless, ever-changing Brian-Eno / Robert-Fripp tape-loop room built entirely from YOUR own sound вАФ audibly different at minute 10 than at minute 1.** This is the **listen вЖТ remember вЖТ rewrite** piece the JURY asked for: it extends the loved `259-paths-generative` вЭ§пЄП (its exact incommensurate-loop engine) but instead of playing back a file, it **listens**. A muted mic tap fills an 8-second rolling ring buffer; a two-threshold RMS onset/release detector marks phrase boundaries and, on phrase-end, slices the most-recent audio out of the ring, peak-normalizes it, fades the ends for loopability, and banks it as a **verbatim tape loop** вАФ keeping the *raw audio*, not extracted features, so the room is literally made of the sound you made. Up to **7 captured phrases** loop at mutually **incommensurate lengths/playheads** (never phase-align) each with a slow incommensurate gain-LFO, a consonant `playbackRate` transposition (octave/5th/4th/M3/unison), and a stereo pan, summed through a **procedural convolution reverb** + shared lowpass + a `DynamicsCompressor` master limiter (never clips, never silent). A **generative state machine** enters a new *movement* every 60вАУ130 s that random-walks the active set (3вАУ5 of the bank, multi-second crossfades), the transpositions, density, filter cutoff, and reverb wetness вАФ and as new phrases arrive the **oldest memory decays out**, so the texture at minute 10 is genuinely unlike minute 1. It's **never silent**: a synthesized **D-dorian** pad (deliberately *not* C-major pentatonic, banned by the JURY) plays from the first instant and is gradually displaced by your own captured sound. Tri-modal source вАФ **mic** (primary) ¬Ј **file-drop** ¬Ј **Welcome Home track-by-ID** вЖТ read-only `/api/audio/:id` (JSON-`{url}`-or-raw-bytes, the loved `163` pattern, so Karel can feed it his real piano) ¬Ј the synth demo fallback. Canvas2D **loom** makes the memory legible: each phrase is a horizontal lane with its waveform drawn in, a playhead sweeping at its own incommensurate rate, brightness = current gain, warm hue (your captures) vs amber (demo seeds), a `captured 2m ago` label, a capture-flash as a new phrase enters, and a HUD of elapsed / movement / active-voice count. **First live audio-capture looping piece in the lab** (loops existed at `172` вЭ§пЄП and granular at `227` вЭ§пЄП, but never *recording you in real time into the loop bank*) and the **first self-listening / Frippertronics architecture** in 270+ prototypes. **Born from this cycle's research dive** into self-listening musical agents (RESEARCH ¬І287). References: **Brian Eno & Robert Fripp** вАФ *Frippertronics* / *(No Pussyfooting)* (1973) + Eno's *Music for Airports* (1978); **Pauline Oliveros** вАФ *Deep Listening*. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-builder fire (one concept вАФ "a piece that listens, remembers, and rewrites itself over 10 min" вАФ three *memory representations*); siblings `mosaic-listener` (CataRT / MACataRT concatenative grain-corpus mosaicing вАФ the lab's first concatenative synthesis) + `motif-memory` (autocorrelation pitch-transcription вЖТ symbolic Markov re-performance, the Lewis *Voyager* / Rowe *Cypher* lineage) are **build-verified** and banked in IDEAS.md. Mic/audio-file input ¬Ј canvas2d output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero server route (read-only `/api/audio` only) ¬Ј no guard ¬Ј 5.51 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/275-memory-loom/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 286 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/272-kids-tune-purr](/dream/272-kids-tune-purr)** вАФ Tune Purr. `demoable`
  **Slide a sleepy, wobbling creature up and down until its shivering stops вАФ and feel the exact moment two notes lock into tune.** A contemplative tuning toy a 4-year-old can run alone (touch-only, no reading): a warm **110 Hz drone** hums, and three round, matte "hummer" creatures each sing a tone that starts a few Hz *out of tune* with a pure interval against the drone вАФ so you literally **hear and see the beating**: a rhythmic wobble, and the creature trembles in sync with it. Drag a creature and its pitch glides; as you approach the pure **just-intonation** ratio (unison 1:1, major third 5:4, perfect fifth 3:2) the wobble slows from a fast shimmer вЖТ a slow sway вЖТ nothing, and within ¬±6 cents it **snaps into tune**: the shivering stops, its eyes open, it smiles, and it begins a soft contented **purr**. Lock all three at once and they form a pure **4:5:6 just-intonation major triad** with a gentle matte ring-wave celebration. Drag one back out and the wobble returns вАФ fully reversible, no score, no fail, infinite calm play. **The whole point is the SOUND**, and it is deliberately *not* the C-major pentatonic of every other kids-music toy: it's pure acoustic beating resolving into consonance вАФ the first time audible **interference beating is the instrument itself** in 270+ prototypes (the child resolves roughnessвЖТconsonance *by ear*, pre-verbally). The visual is fully **non-luminous / matte** вАФ deep-dusk brown background, dusty terracotta/sage/blue creatures with true `shadowBlur` drop-shadows, **zero additive blending, no glow, no three.js, no WebGL** вАФ built to answer the JURY's "ban the glow, ban the pentatonic, audit the sound" cycle on both axes at once. Drone + creatures are alive on first touch; AudioContext unlocks on gesture; everything via `setTargetAtTime` (click-free, toddler-safe, nothing above 350 Hz); 116 px drag targets, Pointer Events + `setPointerCapture` + `touch-none`; degrades gracefully (no Web Audio вЖТ readable `text-rose-300` notice). **Born from this cycle's research dive** into the psychoacoustics of consonance вАФ the three-term roughness model (harmonicity + dislike of fast beats + liking of slow beats). References: **Helmholtz**, *On the Sensations of Tone* (beats/roughness as the physical basis of dissonance) + **McBride 2025** roughness review (arXiv 2510.14159) + Parncutt & Hair. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-builder kids fire (three unrelated non-glow / non-pentatonic *sound worlds*); siblings `273-kids-raga-peacock` (tanpura drone + Raga Yaman in just intonation + meend glide, SVG cut-paper) + `274-kids-clay-clock` (interlocking 2:3:4 + 12/8 gankogui **polyrhythm** on a two-clock scheduler, matte clay, percussion-only) are build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md. For kids 3+ ¬Ј touch input ¬Ј canvas2d-matte output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј no permissions ¬Ј 3.93 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/272-kids-tune-purr/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 284 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/268-kids-shadow-theater](/dream/268-kids-shadow-theater)** вАФ Shadow Theater. `demoable`
  **Tap a paper animal and it walks onto a glowing lamp-lit stage and starts to sing вАФ in the shimmering, deliberately-not-Western tuning of a Javanese gamelan.** A wayang-kulit (Indonesian shadow-puppet) theater a 4-year-old can run alone: five cut-paper animal silhouettes (bird, elephant, deer, fish, monkey) wait on a rack; tap one and it strolls onto the amberвЖТindigo screen, sways/walks, and adds its voice; tap again and it ambles home, fading out. Several puppets at once = a tiny gamelan ensemble, all locked to a slow, calming **colotomic gong cycle** (a deep gong every 8 beats, a mid kempul every 4, footstep "kethuk" ticks) so the room is never silent and feels ceremonial. **The surprise is the sound.** The five pitches are NOT C-major pentatonic (the default of every kids-music toy) вАФ they come from a **slendro-like set of stretched, non-equal-tempered ratios** (вЙИ 1.00, 1.16, 1.35, 1.52, 1.78 over 220 Hz); each metallophone voice is two slightly-detuned sine fundamentals (a few Hz apart вЖТ the characteristic gamelan *beating*/shimmer) plus an inharmonic ~2.41√Ч partial, fast attack + long bell decay (saron/bonang character). The gong is a ~70 Hz sine with a downward glide + filtered-noise thump. **The whole visual is inline SVG** вАФ `<path>` cut-paper silhouettes, a radial-gradient lamp backdrop, a `feTurbulence` oil-lamp flicker вАФ animated by `requestAnimationFrame` updating SVG transforms. **No `<canvas>`, no WebGL, no three.js.** **First SVG-rendered prototype in the lab** (260+ prototypes; `svg` grepped clean) and the **first non-Western / microtonal tuning** anywhere in the lab вАФ built to directly answer the JURY's "ban the glow, ban the pentatonic" cycle: a non-luminous output in a non-pentatonic mode. Every combination sounds good (no wrong note, no fail state); always-on `DynamicsCompressor` limiter keeps it soft + toddler-safe; degrades gracefully (no Web Audio вЖТ readable `text-rose-300` notice, SVG still animates). References: Javanese **wayang kulit** + **gamelan slendro** (Colin McPhee) and **Lotte Reiniger**'s silhouette animation (*Prince Achmed*, 1926). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-builder kids fire (three unrelated non-glow / non-pentatonic directions); siblings `kids-paper-parade` (Eric-Carle torn-paper marching band вАФ a rhythm-layer **groove** builder) + `kids-paper-score` (a Cardew-style **graphic score** a playhead reads вАФ your drawing performs itself) are build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md. For kids 4+ ¬Ј touch input ¬Ј SVG output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј no permissions ¬Ј 4.6 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/268-kids-shadow-theater/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 283 вАФ adult build ¬Ј DEEP orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/267-spectral-drift](/dream/267-spectral-drift)** вАФ Spectral Drift. `demoable`
  **Drop your own piano recording (or load a Resonance track) and your music becomes a flowing river of light you drift through вАФ each frequency a stream of glowing particles that advects and braids forward through space as the song plays.** Not a frozen wall of spectrum bins: the spectral content *flows*. A single pool of **24,000 additive `THREE.Points`** (one `BufferGeometry` + custom soft-gaussian `ShaderMaterial`, allocated once) is emitted as a fresh "sheet" each frame at the far horizon вАФ **bin вЖТ lateral X + hue**, **bin energy вЖТ brightness + point size + emission probability** (quiet bins emit nearly nothing, so silence reads as genuinely empty space ahead). Then every particle **advects**: it drifts forward toward the camera *and* meanders laterally through a cheap curl-like sinusoidal flow field with light damping, so the frequency streams **swirl and braid** as they travel rather than running on rigid rails. Each particle keeps the hue/energy it was born with, so you fly through a gently-deforming record of the song. **Spectral centroid** вЖТ global hue temperature (bass-heavy = deep violet/indigo; bright = cyanвЖТrose) + flow turbulence; **spectral-flux onsets** (adaptive mean+1.5¬Јstd, 100 ms refractory) вЖТ a burst of extra-bright particles + a forward speed surge + subtle camera shake, with a median-IOI **BPM** readout; `FogExp2` into near-black for depth. **This is the flowing-particle reading of the JURY's flagged richest vein, "fly through YOUR music,"** extending the loved `243-spectral-cloud` вЭ§пЄП (the only 5/5 floor build вАФ a cloud you *orbit*; this is a river you fly *through*) in the loved glowing-points language of `130-tsl-particle-compute` вЭ§пЄП. **Born from this cycle's research dive**: it borrows the reframe from **NSTR вАФ "Neural Spectral Transport Representation for Space-Varying Frequency Fields"** (arXiv 2511.18384, Nov 2025), that a spectrum is not a static stack but a *frequency field that transports through space* вАФ embodied here aesthetically as the advection flow (honestly noted in the README as an artistic borrowing, not the neural model). Tri-modal source, never silent: **file drop** ¬Ј **"load a Resonance track by ID"** вЖТ `fetch('/api/audio/:id')` (handles direct-audio *or* JSON `{url}`, so Karel can paste a real Welcome Home piano track вАФ read-only, no route created) ¬Ј a synthesized pad+pentatonic **demo** that flows on Start with zero input. Degrades gracefully (no WebGL вЖТ notice, audio keeps playing; decode/track-load error вЖТ `text-rose-300`). **Input = audio-file ¬Ј output = three.js additive points** вАФ dodges the over-represented `canvas2d` (4√Ч) + `raw-WebGL` (4√Ч) of the last 10. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-builder fire (one concept вАФ "fly through YOUR music as a living 3D world" вАФ three render readings); siblings `spectral-tunnel` (radial wormhole, rings freeze-on-spawn + translate in Z вАФ the kinetic reading) + `spectral-canyon` (spectrogram-waterfall terrain with the Z-remap fix вАФ the legible reading) are **build-verified** and banked in IDEAS.md, queued to make this vein a 4-piece body of work with `243`. Audio-file input ¬Ј three.js output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero server route (read-only `/api/audio`) ¬Ј 4.9 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/267-spectral-drift/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 282 вАФ kids build ¬Ј DEEP orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/264-kids-lenia-pond](/dream/264-kids-lenia-pond)** вАФ Lenia Pond. `demoable`
  **Touch a dark pond and a glowing nebula creature blooms under your finger, then swims away on its own and drifts across the water, singing.** The creatures are **orbium gliders from Lenia** вАФ Bert Chan's 2019 continuous-state cellular automaton, where a soft jellyfish-blob of "matter" self-organizes into a stable, *self-propelling* form (the Lenia equivalent of a Game-of-Life glider). A scalar field `A(x,y)` lives on a 150√Ч150 toroidal grid (Float32 ping-pong, CPU): each step convolves with Chan's exact smooth `exp(4вИТ1/(r(1вИТr)))` ring kernel (R=13) вЖТ growth bell (mu=0.15, sigma=0.017) вЖТ `A вЖР clamp(A+dt¬ЈG,0,1)`, dt=0.1. **Tap** stamps **Bert Chan's canonical 20√Ч20 orbium matrix verbatim** (random quarter-turn rotation per tap so creatures head off in varied directions); one orbium is seeded dead-center on Start so the pond is alive immediately. The field packs to a WebGL2 **R8** texture (deliberately R8 вАФ it's `LINEAR`-filterable on every device, where an R32F-linear path renders black on tablets lacking `OES_texture_float_linear`) rendered through a violetвЖТcyanвЖТrose nebula fragment shader with a white-hot bloom in the dense cores вАФ luminous jellyfish-nebulae, no 2D canvas. **Audio**: 5 vertical bands вЖТ 5 pentatonic voices (C3 E3 G3 A3 C4, no wrong notes), band massвЖТgain + brightness, vertical centroidвЖТ¬±cents detune so the chord breathes as creatures drift; always-on C3+G3 pad; per-tap octave-up ping <50 ms; warm lowpass вЖТ limiter (never loud/harsh). **First Lenia / continuous-CA piece in the lab** and the **2nd emergent-self-organization kids piece** вАФ with Physarum `260-kids-slime-garden`, "emergent life that sings" is now a body of work, not a fluke (the JURY's standing ask). **The cycle-280 banked seed, finally resurrected and de-risked**: the one risk that lost it (does the orbium stay alive or dissolve/explode?) was settled by an **empirical 400-step Node simulation** вАФ mass held steady ~75вАУ76 while the centroid translated ~0.24 cells/step and wrapped toroidally вАФ so it's a verified glider, not a guess. **Touch input + raw-WebGL output**, dodging the over-represented `mic` (4√Ч) + `canvas2d` (4√Ч) of the last 10. Extends the loved emergence vein of `236-particle-life-song` вЭ§пЄП and the cosmic-nebula palette of `130-tsl-particle-compute` вЭ§пЄП. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-builder fire (one concept вАФ "breed glowing lifeforms that sing" вАФ three Lenia formulations); siblings `kids-glow-lifeforms` (energy-based **Particle-Lenia** glowing particle swarm) + `kids-flow-pond` (mass-conserving **Flow-Lenia** spill-proof goo) are build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md. For kids 4+ ¬Ј touch input ¬Ј raw-WebGL output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј 4.85 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/264-kids-lenia-pond/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 281 вАФ adult build ¬Ј DEEP orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/262-aurora-particle](/dream/262-aurora-particle)** вАФ Aurora Particle. `demoable`
  **The solar wind hitting Earth right now writes the score вАФ a generative drone and a flowing aurora built from thousands of glowing particles.** Press Start: three live NOAA SWPC feeds (solar-wind plasma, the interplanetary magnetic field, and the planetary Kp index) are fetched client-side and re-polled every 60s, then EMA-smoothed so sound and light *glide* on each update. The Sun drives both at once вАФ **wind speed** вЖТ drone root pitch + bell-arp tempo + aurora hue (slow=green вЖТ fast=violet); **plasma density** вЖТ number of detuned drone partials (2вАУ5); **Bz** (north-south IMF) вЖТ majorвЖФminor third *and* curtain height/agitation (negative Bz = storm coupling вЖТ minor + taller, wilder curtains); **Bt** вЖТ loudness + brightness; **Kp 0вАУ9** вЖТ the whole system steps from a couple of soft green sheets (CALM) to many towering red/violet curtains (STORM). The aurora is ~9,800 additive `THREE.Points` arranged into curtain sheets, each particle rising and swaying in a custom vertex shader, lit through `@react-three/postprocessing` Bloom over a deep night-sky + star field вАФ the glowing-points-in-3D language of the loved `130-tsl-particle-compute` вЭ§пЄП, aimed at the sky. An always-on drone (detuned saw/tri вЖТ Kp-driven lowpass вЖТ feedback delay вЖТ code-synthesized convolution reverb вЖТ limiter) means it's never silent and never clips. **The 2nd real-world-data sonification in the lab** (after `233-earth-pulse`) вАФ open it on two different days and it's a different piece because the Sun was. Degrades gracefully: any feed failure вЖТ a bounded random-walk **simulated** solar wind with a readable rose notice; no WebGL вЖТ drone-only. **Input = live external space-weather API ¬Ј output = three.js particle curtains + Bloom** вАФ dodges the over-represented `mic` (4√Ч) and `canvas2d` (4√Ч) of the last 10. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-builder fire (one concept вАФ "live solar wind = aurora + drone" вАФ three render strategies); siblings `aurora-drone-field` (raw-WebGL2 GLSL sum-of-sines curtains) + `aurora-raymarch` (volumetric raymarch with real O-green/O-red/N-violet altitudeвЖТemission-line physics) are build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md. Zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј 5.6 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/262-aurora-particle/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 280 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/260-kids-slime-garden](/dream/260-kids-slime-garden)** вАФ Slime Garden. `demoable`
  **Touch a dark screen and thousands of tiny glowing creatures crawl toward your finger, weaving living veins of light that sing as they connect.** A real *Physarum polycephalum* slime-mold simulation: ~3,500 typed-array agents on a 220√Ч220 trail grid each sense the trail at three points (front-left / front / front-right), steer toward the brightest, deposit, and the field diffuses + decays вАФ the canonical **Jones/Jenson transport-network algorithm** вАФ plus a gentle pull toward the "food" nodes you tap, so the swarm physically *connects the points you touch*. The trail grid uploads each frame as a WebGL2 `R8` texture rendered through a bioluminescent goldвЖТtealвЖТmagenta fragment shader over deep indigo (no 2D-canvas art). Up to **5 food nodes** each own a C-major-pentatonic voice (C3 E3 G3 A3 C4) whose gain + lowpass cutoff rise with the local glow вАФ a well-connected node sings louder and brighter; an always-on C3+G3 pad + master limiter keep it calm and never silent. **First Physarum / agent-transport-network piece in the lab**, and a kids prototype rendered entirely through GPU shaders вАФ **touch input + raw-WebGL output**, deliberately dodging the over-represented `three.js` + `canvas2d` of the last 10. Extends the loved emergence vein of `236-particle-life-song` вЭ§пЄП (music FROM self-organization). Winner of a WIDE 3-builder kids fire; siblings `kids-lenia-pond` (Bert Chan's **Lenia** continuous CA вАФ glowing lifeforms that glide & sing) and `kids-light-cloth` (Jakobsen **Verlet** mass-spring cloth you strum) are banked in IDEAS.md. For kids 4+ ¬Ј touch input ¬Ј raw-WebGL output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј 4.74 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/260-kids-slime-garden/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 279 вАФ adult build ¬Ј WIDE orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/259-paths-generative](/dream/259-paths-generative)** вАФ Paths Generative. `demoable`
  **Drop your own piano recording (or load a Resonance track) and it becomes an endless, never-repeating Brian-Eno ambient room вАФ a living tape-loop piece that's audibly different at minute 10 than at minute 1.** Implements the *Music for Airports* / *Discreet Music* trick directly: **six loops of mutually incommensurate length** (11.3 / 14.7 / 18.1 / 23.9 / 29.3 / 37.7 s вАФ product вЙИ **29 hours** before any exact repeat) play at once, each transposed to a consonant interval (octave / 5th / 4th / M3 via `playbackRate`), each on its own slow LFO-gain (0.023вАУ0.071 Hz, also incommensurate) + stereo pan, summed through a synthesized-noise **convolution reverb** + shared lowpass. A **generative state machine** enters a new "movement" every 90вАУ150 s (scaled by a *drift* slider) that random-walks the active-voice subset (3вАУ5 of 6, 6-s fades), filter cutoff (200вАУ4000 Hz), reverb wetness, transposition set, palette and density вАФ with memory, so the texture genuinely evolves (3 bright close voices at minute 1 вЖТ a deep 5-voice wash at minute 5 вЖТ a register shift at minute 10). Echoes **Steve Reich** phase music too: loops drifting out of alignment surface rhythms nobody programmed. Tri-modal source: **file-drop** ¬Ј **"load a Resonance track by ID"** вЖТ `fetch('/api/audio/:id')` (handles a direct-audio *or* JSON `{url}` response вАФ the loved `163-paths-visualizer` вЭ§пЄП pattern, so Karel can paste a real Welcome Home piano track) ¬Ј a synthesized `OfflineAudioContext` **demo** (Cmaj7вЖТFmaj7вЖТAm7вЖТG arpeggio) that plays with **zero input** so it's never silent. Canvas-2D: **six concentric rings rotating at `1/loopLength` rad/s** so you literally *see* the phase drift you hear, per-voice glow + playhead, a movement-tinted radial field; readout of elapsed / movement # / active voices. **First long-form (>5 min) *stateful* generative piece in the lab** вАФ the "leave it running" category the diversity menu flagged as empty (loops existed at `172` вЭ§пЄП, granular at `227` вЭ§пЄП, but never a movement-based evolving piece). Born from this cycle's research dive (Eno/Reich vs. the long-form-diffusion literature вАФ the 1978 trick wins for zero-dep, zero-latency non-repetition; RESEARCH ¬І279). Winner of a WIDE 3-builder fire вАФ siblings `260-aurora-drone` (live NOAA solar-wind вЖТ drone + aurora; the JURY-asked **2nd real-world-data sonification** after `233`) and `261-live-duet-groover` (mic вЖТ spectral-flux/two-clocks generative drums; the **rhythm member completing the AI-band trio**) are build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md. Pulled by the loved "his own piano" cluster `163` вЭ§пЄП + `227` вЭ§пЄП + `172` вЭ§пЄП and Karel's standing "use the Paths recordings" directive. Audio-file input ¬Ј canvas2d output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero server route (read-only `/api/audio` only) ¬Ј 5.13 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/259-paths-generative/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 277 вАФ adult build ¬Ј DEEP orchestration, 2 explored)

- **[/dream/256-live-duet-harmonist](/dream/256-live-duet-harmonist)** вАФ Live Duet Harmonist. `demoable`
  **Play *chords* and a jazz accompanist answers in real time вАФ rootless voicings and a walking bass that lock to the rhythm of YOUR playing, not a fixed metronome.** The **harmony member** of the multi-cycle "AI band you play with," and the polyphonic half of the duet: where `251-live-duet-trader` вЭ§пЄП tracks a single melodic line, this one hears a four-note chord. Mic вЖТ `AnalyserNode` (fftSize 4096) вЖТ fold the byte-FFT (60вАУ2000 Hz) into a **12-bin chroma** (one-pole 0.8) вЖТ **cosine-match 36 templates** (12 maj / 12 min / 12 dom7, shell-tone-weighted, b7 emphasized for dominants) вЖТ a **160 ms look-ahead "settle"** so the bed switches only when a chord persists вЖТ a **jazz voicing engine**: rootless / drop-2 comp voicings (3+7 shell plus a 9/13 color tone, Mark Levine vocabulary) with nearest-pitch `setTargetAtTime` voice-leading glides, under a **walking bass** (root on the downbeat, chord tones on 1вАУ2, a chromatic approach a semitone below the next root on beat 4). The pulse is **inferred from your attacks**, not fixed: **spectral-flux onset detection** (adaptive `mean + 1.5¬Јstd`, 100 ms refractory) вЖТ median inter-onset-interval вЖТ tempo folded to 60вАУ180 BPM вЖТ a **Chris-Wilson "two clocks" look-ahead scheduler** (25 ms tick / 100 ms window, events at exact `AudioContext` time) places the bass + comp stabs on your beat; sparse/legato playing falls back to a gentle 80 BPM so it never stalls. Canvas-2D: 12-wedge chroma ring, large root-hued center chord name + quality, a glowing rootless-voicing node arc, a walking-bass ladder, a 4-beat pulse indicator with emerald onset-flash + live BPM, and a duration-weighted chord-history trail. No-mic вЖТ auto-runs Dm7 вЖТ G7 вЖТ Cmaj7 вЖТ Am7 at 88 BPM with the full walking bass + comping (a `text-rose-300` notice + "Use microphone" retry). **First spectral-flux onset/tempo inference + two-clocks scheduler *fused with* live harmony in the lab** (the lab had chromaвЖТchord naming in `229-chord-canvas` вЭ§пЄП, but never a reactive comping engine). Born from the cycle-275 DEEP "AI band" fire's banked harmony member, re-fired DEEP this cycle (builder A = a simpler fixed-clock comping bed, build-verified + banked in IDEAS.md as the demo-reliable fallback; builder B's jazz/onset-sync version won). References: **ReaLchords** (arXiv 2506.14723, Jun 2025 вАФ online chord accompaniment, RL-shaped on harmonic+temporal coherency), arXiv 2604.07612 (the 160 ms settle), Chris Wilson *A Tale of Two Clocks*, Mark Levine *The Jazz Piano Book*. Pulled by `251-live-duet-trader` вЭ§пЄП + `229-chord-canvas` вЭ§пЄП (the chord/responsive thread). Mic input ¬Ј canvas2d output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј 5.63 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/256-live-duet-harmonist/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 276 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/253-kids-tilt-pour](/dream/253-kids-tilt-pour)** вАФ Tilt Pour рЯЂІ. `demoable`
  **For kids 4+ вАФ *tilt* the iPad to pour a glowing lava-lamp of candy-colored jelly blobs that sing when they merge. No touching the screen, no reading, no wrong notes.** Lean the tablet and 8 luminous blobs slosh and pool in whatever direction you tilt; when two of them fuse into a gooey shape, that pair rings a soft **C-major-pentatonic** note (each color owns a pitch), and a low ambient triad pad swells the more vigorously you slosh them around. The blobs are a real **metaball field** rendered in a raw **WebGL fragment shader**: each blob's scalar field uses a **Hermite smoothstep falloff** (cheaper than `exp()` on phones), summed and threshold-banded into a bright interior + glowing rim + soft additive halo (Inigo-Quilez-style smooth-min fusion), then Reinhard-tonemapped so it never clips to white. A tiny **CPU physics step** turns `deviceorientation` gamma/beta into a smoothed **gravity vector** driving gravity + damping + edge-restitution + soft blobвАУblob repulsion (velocities capped so it's always gentle). Born **directly from this cycle's research dive** on **Damian Van Der Merwe's "Painting with Math: Building an Interactive Lava Lamp Shader from Scratch"** (Apr 3, 2026) вАФ all four of his mobile-perf choices adopted (Hermite falloff, fixed 8 blobs, 2-octave noise cap, DPRвЙ§2); also references IQ smooth-min/SDF. Sound chain: sine+octave-triangle voices вЖТ feedback-delay shimmer вЖТ DynamicsCompressor limiter (always kid-safe). iOS motion permission is requested behind the big start tap (which also unlocks audio); on denial / no sensor / no tilt within ~1.8s it auto-switches to a **pointer-drag** fallback (drag steers gravity) with a `text-rose-300` notice; no-WebGL shows a notice while the pad keeps playing. **First metaball / smooth-min lava-lamp in the lab.** Dodges the week's banned `three.js` + `touch+canvas2d+pentatonic-chime` kids template (tilt input ¬Ј raw-WebGL output). Winner of a WIDE 3-builder kids fire вАФ siblings `254-kids-blow-bloom` (BLOW into the mic вЖТ dandelion seeds drift off ringing notes; the lab's first **breath-detection** input) and `255-kids-sing-garden` (voiceвЖТfbm bedtime sky + melody loopback) are build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md. Pulled by `83-kids-tilt-rain` вЭ§пЄП + `169-kids-marble-run` вЭ§пЄП + `84-wave-fluid` вЭ§пЄП. Tilt input ¬Ј raw-WebGL fragment-shader output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј 7.03 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/253-kids-tilt-pour/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 275 вАФ adult build ¬Ј DEEP orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/251-live-duet-trader](/dream/251-live-duet-trader)** вАФ Live Duet Trader. `demoable`
  **Trade fours with a partner that plays *with* you вАФ the instant you pause it darts in with a melodic answer in your key, and the instant you play again it ducks out of the way.** Play any pitched line (piano, voice, guitar) into the mic; after ~3 notes the partner starts learning your intervals (a live 1st-order **Markov** table) and inferring your **key** from a pitch-class histogram. Whenever a **~320 ms silence gap** opens it answers a key-constrained, rhythmically lively phrase on a warm additive voice (triangle + inharmonic partials + feedback-delay shimmer); the **moment a new onset of yours is detected, its gain ramps to zero in ~50 ms and the scheduled phrase is cancelled** вАФ you always have right of way. That duck-on-re-entry is the whole idea: continuous **interleaved trading**, not the fixed-2-second turn-taking of `225-aria-companion`. Pitch = ~16 ms **NSDF/McLeod autocorrelation** (RMS-gated, 70 ms onset debounce). Canvas-2D split scrolling piano-roll вАФ **YOU** amber on top, **PARTNER** teal below вАФ with glow trails, a LISTENING/ANSWERING badge, the inferred key, a filling gap-timer bar, and a live pitch dot. No-mic вЖТ an auto-running demo motif that plays, pauses, gets answered, and loops (fully showable without hardware); mic-denied shows a `text-rose-300` notice and falls into the demo. **First continuous interleaved-trading interaction in the lab** (the third reactive model after continuous-mirror and fixed-turn) and the realization of JURY.md's named request вАФ "fuse the duet-shadow seed + 225-aria-companion into real reactive accompaniment." Born from this cycle's research dive on **arXiv 2604.07612 "Towards Real-Time HumanвАУAI Musical Co-Performance"** (Apr 2026, sliding-window look-ahead co-performance); references Fran√Іois Pachet's *The Continuator* + jazz "trading fours." Winner of a DEEP 3-builder *live-reactive-accompanist* fire вАФ siblings `250-live-duet-harmonist` (chromaвЖТchord вЖТ continuous comping bass+pad bed) and `252-live-duet-groover` (spectral-flux onset вЖТ tempo inference вЖТ look-ahead two-clocks generative groove) are build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md as the next two members of a multi-cycle "AI band." Pulled by the orchestration-era love wave (`243` вЭ§пЄП / `236` вЭ§пЄП / `234` вЭ§пЄП) + the responsive/dialogue line behind `225`. Mic input ¬Ј canvas2d output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј 5.48 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/251-live-duet-trader/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 274 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/248-kids-stir-garden](/dream/248-kids-stir-garden)** вАФ Stir Garden рЯМ±. `demoable`
  **For kids 4+ вАФ GROW a living, glowing garden by MOVING your whole body in front of the camera. No touching the screen, no reading, no wrong moves.** Where the child moves, coral/leopard-spot **Turing patterns** bloom in glowing tealвЖТcoralвЖТgold and the garden gently sings. Built on a real **Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion** simulation running on the GPU via **raw WebGL2 ping-pong framebuffers** (render-to-texture, 6 sub-steps/frame, clamp-stabilized) вАФ *not* a triggered animation but a living chemistry that keeps growing on its own. **Zero-dependency frame-differencing** (a hidden 64√Ч48 offscreen canvas, no MediaPipe/ML) turns whole-body motion into a seed injected into the `v` chemical, mirrored so it feels like a mirror; 5 horizontal zones map to C-pentatonic notes (sine+triangle, gentle envelopes, feedback-delay shimmer, DynamicsCompressor limiter), an always-on pad swells with motion, and after 3s of stillness the garden auto-blooms so it never looks dead. **First reaction-diffusion / GPU-simulation piece in the kids zone, and the first raw-GLSL kids piece** вАФ the ~115 prior kids builds are touch+2D-canvas or three.js. It introduces a different *temporality* of play (plantвЖТbloom, not the lab's tapвЖТchime reflex) and extends the just-loved no-touch-camera direction of `234-kids-hand-creature` вЭ§пЄП with a lighter, no-ML input. Camera-denied вЖТ pointer "stir" fallback (`text-rose-300` notice); no-WebGL2 вЖТ readable notice + pad keeps playing; video hidden + frames discarded each tick (privacy). References Amanda Ghassaei's Gray-Scott RD shader (+ directed-field variant), cake23.de Turing-fluid, Karl Sims' RD parameter map, Daniel Rozin motion mirrors; born from this cycle's research dive on interactive stir-able RD. Winner of a WIDE 3-builder kids fire (siblings `247-kids-sing-garden` voiceвЖТGLSL-sky + `249-kids-tilt-pour` tiltвЖТmetaball-lava-lamp, both build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md). Pulled by `234` вЭ§пЄП + `104-kids-mirror-draw` вЭ§пЄП + `101-camera-song` вЭ§пЄП + `133-kids-ripple-pond` вЭ§пЄП + `84-wave-fluid` вЭ§пЄП. Camera-motion input ¬Ј raw-WebGL2-shader output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј no mic ¬Ј 5.93 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/248-kids-stir-garden/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 273 вАФ adult build ¬Ј DEEP orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/246-spectral-splat](/dream/246-spectral-splat)** вАФ Spectral Splat. `demoable`
  **Fly *through* your own music as a soft volumetric cloud of light.** Drop in a track (or use mic, or the built-in generative pad) and each FFT frame is deposited as a slab of soft, additive **Gaussian splats** вАФ anisotropic glowing blobs with a true `exp(-r¬≤)` falloff вАФ into a ring buffer parked ahead of the camera; the camera then **dollies forward through** the accumulating nebula, so older frames recede into fog. Where `243-spectral-cloud` вЭ§пЄП has you *orbit* a hard point cloud, this one has you *travel into* a soft luminous volume with body вАФ a continuous fog, not dots. Custom `ShaderMaterial` (additive, depth-write off, screen-X anisotropy), geometry allocated once / only typed-arrays rewritten per frame. Reactive: spectral **centroid** вЖТ hue drift + cloud dispersion; energy-flux **onset** вЖТ coloured shockwave shell + brightness bloom + forward speed surge; corner HUD shows source / BPM / onset. **First Gaussian-splat / soft-volumetric piece in the lab** вАФ all prior volumetric work (`243` вЭ§пЄП, `130-tsl-particle-compute` вЭ§пЄП, `236`) renders hard additive points or particle sims. Born from this cycle's research dive on **AudioGS вАФ "Spectrogram-Based Audio Gaussian Splatting"** (arxiv 2604.08967, Apr 2026); visual lineage Refik Anadol *Machine Hallucinations* + Ryoji Ikeda. Winner of a DEEP 3-builder "fly through your music" fire (siblings `spectral-tunnel` wormhole + `spectral-canyon` terrain build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md). Pulled by `243-spectral-cloud` вЭ§пЄП √Ч `130` вЭ§пЄП √Ч the paths thread (`227`/`163` вЭ§пЄП). Audio-file input (+mic/generative) ¬Ј three.js/custom-shader output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј optional mic ¬Ј 6.69 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/246-spectral-splat/README.md`

---

## Previous (Cycle 272 вАФ kids build ¬Ј WIDE orchestration, 3 explored)

- **[/dream/244-kids-sing-creature](/dream/244-kids-sing-creature)** вАФ Sing Creature вЬ®. `demoable`
  **For kids 4+ вАФ *sing* to grow and shape a glowing 3D creature that sings your melody back. No touching the screen, no reading, no wrong notes.** A luminous blob-creature floats in a soft bedtime sky; the child hums or sings into the mic and it breathes, **swells with loudness**, **changes hue with pitch** (low = deep violet вЖТ high = cyan/rose), **grows** the longer they sing, and вАФ after a beat of quiet вАФ **sings their little tune right back** as glowing pulses (call-and-response ear-training). Built on raw three.js: a high-res icosphere displaced along its normals by layered **GLSL simplex-noise in a custom `ShaderMaterial`**, fresnel rim + additive halo = glowing-jelly look. Mic = **autocorrelation pitch detection** (parabolic-interp + RMS gate), snapped to **C-major pentatonic** so anything sung sounds right; a soft sine tracks the snapped pitch so the child hears themselves in tune. Always-on ambient C/G drone + idle breathing (never silent/dead); mic is analyser-only, never recorded. **First voiceвЖТ3D-morphing-creature in the lab** (the ~110 prior kids pieces are touch + 2D canvas; `158-kids-hum-paint` вЭ§пЄП is voiceвЖТ2D paint; `234` is *hands*вЖТ3D). Mic-denied вЖТ 160px tap-and-hold fallback feeding pentatonic notes; WebGL-absent shows a notice. Winner of a WIDE 3-builder kids fire (siblings `kids-clap-dancers` onsetвЖТ3D-band + `kids-body-band` camera-motion banked in IDEAS.md). References Toca Boca/Sago calm play + voice-driven-blob lineage + Reggio-Emilia embodied music cognition (voice as a child's first instrument). Pulled by `158-kids-hum-paint` вЭ§пЄП + `100-kids-paint-song` вЭ§пЄП. Voice-pitch input ¬Ј three.js/GLSL output ¬Ј zero new deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј optional mic ¬Ј 6.13 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/244-kids-sing-creature/README.md`

  **For kids 4+ вАФ grow and play a glowing 3D creature with your hands, no touching the screen.** Hold your hands up in front of the iPad's front camera and *conduct* a blobby creature: **raise your hands** and it inflates, brightens, and rings soft pentatonic notes (C4вЖТC5 by height); **open your hands wide** and it spikes and sparkles; **bring up a second hand** and a little satellite blob orbits the main one. Built on raw three.js вАФ a high-detail icosahedron displaced in a **vertex shader** by 3D simplex noise (Ashima `snoise`), rim-lit jelly glow вАФ driven live by **MediaPipe HandLandmarker** (21 keypoints/hand, GPU, CDN-loaded, never silent thanks to an always-on sine pad). **First MediaPipe prototype in the lab and first 3D/WebGL piece in the kids zone** вАФ the entire kids zone before this was touch + canvas2d. Camera-denied or offline degrades to a self-playing pentatonic auto-demo + tap-to-bounce. References Derivative's TouchDesigner "Hand Tracking Master Class" (landmarks conducting visuals) + spite/clicktorelease vertex-displacement blob. ## Previous (Cycle 267 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/233-earth-pulse](/dream/233-earth-pulse)** вАФ Earth Pulse. `demoable`
  The **last 24 hours of global earthquakes, played as music.** Pulls the live USGS "all_day" GeoJSON feed (public, keyless, CORS-open) and turns every quake into a sounding event sequenced in compressed real time over a pulsing wireframe globe. **Magnitude вЖТ loudness + pitch** (bigger = deeper boom: M2вЙИ170 Hz, M7вЙИ30 Hz); **depth вЖТ timbre** (shallow = bright crack, deep = muffled rumble); **longitude вЖТ stereo pan**. A full day compresses into ~1.25вАУ4 min (Slow/Normal/Fast); aftershock swarms become audible flurries. The globe (react-three-fiber: wireframe graticule + occluding core + a `Points` shader colored warmвЖТviolet by depth + Bloom) flares each quake as it sounds; drag to orbit. Synthetic fallback set if the feed is blocked. **First prototype in the lab to sonify a real external API** вАФ the world writes the score; open it on different days and it's a different piece. Reference: the *silent* "Earthquake Pulse Map" WebGL globe + the "Sounds of Seismic" / IRIS SeisSound tradition. Ties to the **Earth Grounding** journey. 4 subsystems ¬Ј zero permissions ¬Ј 4.77 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/233-earth-pulse/README.md`

---

  **For kids 4+** вАФ Five BANDIMAL xylophone bars sit at the bottom (C3/violet/tallest вЖТ C4/cyan/shortest). Coloured drops fall from above, one per column, with ~3вАУ4s of fall time. **Tap a drop while it's falling** вЖТ loud bell note (triangle + √Ч2.756 partial) + 20-sparkle burst + bright bar flash. **Let it land** вЖТ quiet note + 10-sparkle splash. **Tap a bar directly** вЖТ note + sparkles, any time. Drops drift gently toward their column center as they fall вАФ catch zone is predictable. Auto-spawn: 1 drop every 1.5s, 2 demo drops at load. Pentatonic C3вАУC4 only вАФ no uncaught drop ever sounds wrong. **First kids prototype where the mechanic is catching a moving target** вАФ 231 prior kids prototypes respond to WHERE you tap; this adds WHEN (timing + moving-target tracking). Three reward tiers (catch/land/bar-tap) are immediately perceptible without score counters. Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.65 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/232-kids-rain-xylophone/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 265 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/231-mood-xy](/dream/231-mood-xy)** вАФ Mood XY. `demoable`
  Drag a dot through the **Russell circumplex emotion plane** (X = valence: sad вЖР вЖТ happy; Y = arousal: calm вЖУ вЖТ excited). The Web Audio synthesizer follows in real time: arousal drives BPM (40вАУ140), voice count (1вАУ6), attack shape (pad вЖТ staccato), and oscillator type (sine вЖТ triangle); valence drives chord quality (major / minor / diminished), filter cutoff (warm вЖТ bright), and note duration (long вЖТ short). At **excited ¬Ј happy**: bright major arpeggios, 6 voices, 45ms stagger, 120+ BPM. At **calm ¬Ј sad**: sparse diminished chords, 1 voice, long sustain, 40 BPM. Background color bilinearly interpolates between 4 dark quadrant tones (deep amber / deep purple / deep teal / deep navy). Beat-synced glow pulse on the dot breathes with BPM. Trail of 72 fading circles follows movement. **First prototype where you set emotional intent and music follows.** Zero deps ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј 2.81 kB.

Tap the pink bubble to play a random pentatonic note and bounce it; 1.2 seconds later FRIEND brightens, bounces, and plays a **consonant response** (C3вЖТG3 P5, E3вЖТA3 P4, G3вЖТC4 P4, A3вЖТC3 octave, C4вЖТG3 P4). During the exchange, 16 cyan sparkles arc from FRIEND toward YOU and a dashed quadratic arc connects the two bubbles as a "conversation thread." After FRIEND sings: "your turn вЩ™" appears and the cycle repeats. **First kids prototype where the responder has a distinct character identity** вАФ prior call-and-response prototypes (echo-drum, echo-canon) use a generic system; here FRIEND has a face, a color, and a musical voice. Every response is harmonically consonant вАФ no music theory needed. Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.99 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 261 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/227-paths-granular](/dream/227-paths-granular)** вАФ Granular. `demoable`
  Upload any audio file (WAV, MP3, or the built-in C major demo) and reshape it into a grain cloud. Four parameters: **scrub** position (which moment in the file), **grain size** (20вАУ500 ms), **density** (2вАУ30 grains/sec), **pitch shift** (¬±12 semitones), **scatter** (how far grains stray from the scrub point). Each grain is Hann-windowed, randomly panned, and given an attack+decay envelope; the result ranges from smooth frozen-chord textures to glitchy percussive clouds. Waveform strip shows the buffer; sparkle particles burst from the scrub cursor on every grain. **First granular synthesis prototype** вАФ try Karel's Welcome Home recordings for instant time-stretching of real piano textures. Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј 3.65 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/227-paths-granular/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 257 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/223-fourier-paint](/dream/223-fourier-paint)** вАФ Fourier Paint. `demoable`
  Draw any closed shape вЖТ Discrete Fourier Transform decomposes it into N rotating epicycles вЖТ
  the chain of spinning arms traces your shape while additive synthesis sounds each harmonic.
  The *shape of the drawing* IS the *timbre of the tone*: a circle вЖТ pure 55 Hz sine; a square вЖТ
  odd harmonics (sounds like a square wave); a 5-pointed star вЖТ fundamental + 5th harmonic; an
  asymmetric scribble вЖТ complex buzzy texture. Terms slider (1вАУ64) lets you hear the Fourier series
  build from one pure oscillator to 64 harmonics вАФ simultaneously a math lesson and a timbre sculptor.
  **First prototype where a 2D geometric drawing is the audio program** (reverses `13-piano-canvas`
  and `219-waveshape-draw`). Dark canvas, amber glowing tip, violet epicycle arms, violet trace path.
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј 3.3 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/223-fourier-paint/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 255 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/221-optical-flow-music](/dream/221-optical-flow-music)** вАФ Optical Flow Music. `demoable`
  Move in front of the camera вАФ the motion IS the music. Frame-differencing on a 20√Ч15 grid yields
  three aggregate signals: totalMag (motion speed) вЖТ filter cutoff + arpeggiation rate;
  hBias (rightward vs. leftward flow) вЖТ pitch snapped to C major pentatonic; vBias (downward flow)
  вЖТ reverb depth. Arrow overlay shows each cell's flow vector, colored by direction (amber=right,
  violet=left, teal=up, rose=down). Demo mode: three glowing blobs bounce around a dark canvas
  generating flow without any camera permission needed. **First prototype where motion вАФ not
  audio вАФ is the primary musical input.** Different from `110-webcam-compose` (color вЖТ music):
  here stillness = silence, only movement makes sound. Inspired by Karel's love of
  `217-dance-avatar` вЭ§пЄП. Zero permissions (demo) ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј demoable.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/221-optical-flow-music/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 253 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/219-waveshape-draw](/dream/219-waveshape-draw)** вАФ Waveshape Draw. `demoable`
  Draw a waveform on a canvas вАФ hear its timbre live via `createPeriodicWave`. Drag finger or mouse
  across the top 62% of the screen to reshape the oscillator's period; the timbre updates in real time
  as you draw. An amber overlay shows the actual oscillator output from `AnalyserNode`; a 32-bar harmonic
  chart below shows the Fourier spectrum of what you drew. Presets: Sine, Square, Triangle, Sawtooth.
  Pitch slider (A1вАУA5), volume slider. **First prototype where you draw a waveform shape and hear it as
  audio timbre** вАФ the paradigm inversion of every other prototype (which convert audio вЖТ visual). All
  218 prior prototypes use FFT for analysis; this is the first that uses DFT for synthesis. The harmonic
  chart shows why a square wave sounds buzzy (odd harmonics only), why a sine is clean (harmonic 1 only),
  and why a jagged drawn curve sounds metallic (many harmonics populated). Inspired by Karel's love of
  `153-paint-compose` вЭ§пЄП. Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.25 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/219-waveshape-draw/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 251 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/217-dance-avatar](/dream/217-dance-avatar)** вАФ Dance Avatar. `demoable`
  A 12-joint spring-physics skeleton that dances to audio вАФ head, shoulders, elbows, wrists, hips,
  knees, feet. Each body part is driven by its own frequency band: sub-bass bounces hips/feet, bass
  lifts shoulders, low-mid sways the torso left-right, mid swings arms counter-phase, high-mid flutters
  the wrists, treble nods the head. Spring physics (K=140, D=10) gives body-like movement вАФ joints
  overshoot, oscillate, and settle as real limbs do. Demo mode: 6 incommensurable LFOs produce continuous
  fluid motion from the moment the page opens. Mic mode: skeleton mirrors whatever is playing in real time.
  **First prototype with an animated human skeleton.** 216 prior prototypes visualize audio as particles,
  fluid, terrain, rings, or canvas marks; this is the first where the visualization IS a human figure.
  Inspired by DiscoForcing (ICML 2026). Live-performance fitness: high вАФ works as a stage visualizer at
  projection scale. Zero permissions (mic optional) ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.43 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/217-dance-avatar/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 250 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/216-kids-band-builder](/dream/216-kids-band-builder)** вАФ Band Builder. `demoable`
  Five glowing circles on a dark canvas вАФ Bass (violet), Mid (teal), Melody (cyan), Rhythm (amber),
  Shimmer (rose). Tap any circle to add its instrument voice to a phase-locked band at 80 BPM. Tap again
  to remove it. All five loops share a common beat clock; a new voice always enters on-beat. BANDIMAL sizing:
  bigger circle = lower pitch (Bass r=76, Shimmer r=30). When all five are on: "вЬ® Full Band! вЬ®" flash +
  sparkle burst from all circles. Thin colored lines connect active circles (visual "playing together" metaphor).
  **First kids prototype about muting/unmuting independent looping tracks** вАФ same paradigm as a DJ with stems.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.82 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/216-kids-band-builder/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 249 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/215-fm-explorer](/dream/215-fm-explorer)** вАФ FM Explorer. `demoable`
  Move your cursor (or drag on touch) across a 2D canvas to sweep through hundreds of FM timbres.
  X axis = carrier pitch (C2вАУC7, log). Y axis = modulator ratio (0.5вАУ8.0). FM index slider controls
  depth. Background color field encodes timbral complexity: emerald = harmonic ratios (organ-like),
  amber = bell-like, violet = metallic/noisy. Waveform scope strip shows FM output in real time.
  5 presets: Bell, Rhodes, Clangy, Sub, Metallic. Mic mode: RMS вЖТ FM index (play louder = more
  metallic). **First FM synthesis prototype in 214 prior prototypes.** FM underlies the DX7 (1983),
  Rhodes piano, 808 sub-bass, and bell tones вАФ three Web Audio nodes, zero deps.

  Cute face (two eyes + smile) drawn inside the head circle. Idle breathing animation (unique phase per

  Krumhansl-Kessler tonal hierarchy profiles (12-bin chroma √Ч 24 major/minor templates, dot-product
  scoring). Diatonic intervals computed per scale degree вАФ B in C major gets a minor third (D) and

  responds automatically. **First dialogue prototype** вАФ turn-taking structure (you play, Aria

## Previous (Cycle 242 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/209-kids-drum-tap](/dream/209-kids-drum-tap)** вАФ Drum Tap. `demoable`
  Four large colored drum pads fill the screen. Tap any pad for an instant percussive sound
  + ripple ring: kick (violet, top-left, biggest), hi-hat (amber, top-right, smallest), snare
  (rose, bottom-left), tom (teal, bottom-right). BANDIMAL rule: bigger = lower pitch.
  After 2+ taps and 1.5s of silence, a **1st-order Markov chain** generates an 8-step drum
  response (8th notes at 80 BPM = 3s). The drum pads flash in sequence as the response plays.
  Transition matrix builds from the user's own tap sequence вАФ the drum gradually mirrors
  which pads you tend to chain together. Tapping during response interrupts and rebuilds.
  Auto-demo plays a kick-hat-snare-hat pattern after 2.2s to show the interaction model.
  Drum sounds: kick (sine 110вЖТ40 Hz glide), hihat (noise вЖТ highpass 7500 Hz), snare
  (bandpass noise 1800 Hz + 185 Hz transient), tom (sine 155вЖТ75 Hz glide). Zero deps.
  **First kids prototype with explicit call-and-response rhythm dialogue.**
  For kids 4+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.88 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 241 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/208-param-layer](/dream/208-param-layer)** вАФ Param Layer. `demoable`
  Four concentric draggable rings sculpt a harmonic bell tone in real time. Outer (violet) = pitch
  (C2 вЖТ A5 via exponential mapping); Ring 2 (teal) = partial count (1 pure sine вЖТ 16 rich stack);
  Ring 3 (amber) = inharmonicity stretch (0% perfect harmonics вЖТ 22% metallic spread, via
  `f_n = fвВА √Ч n √Ч (1 + ih √Ч (n-1))`); Inner (rose) = decay (0.15s sharp click вЖТ 5.0s slow gong).
  A quiet 16-partial drone plays continuously so parameter changes are heard instantly. Tap the
  center вЦґ circle to fire a loud bell strike at the current timbre вАФ decays with the Decay ring.
  Center shows a live circular waveform from the AnalyserNode (summed partials, 256 sample path).
  HUD row shows live values: note name + Hz, partial count, inharmonicity %, decay seconds.
  **First prototype with a hierarchical ring control surface** вАФ outer gestures set register/mass,
  inner rings set fine timbre detail. Inspired by DEMON (arXiv:2605.28657, May 2026).
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј 3.3 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/208-param-layer/README.md`
