## Previous (Cycle 239 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/206-sdf-cave](/dream/206-sdf-cave)** вАФ Cave. `demoable`
  Audio-reactive stone cave rendered via SDF ray-marching in a WebGL fragment shader.
  The viewer is positioned **inside** the cave вАФ stalactite columns, a torus arch, and rough stone
  walls surround them on all sides. Three audio axes: **bass** drives the `smin` blend factor `k`
  (cave walls organically melt together and pull apart), **treble** adds Perlin-noise surface
  displacement (smooth stone вЖТ rough/jagged), **spectral centroid** shifts the cave light color
  (warm violet-amber at low register, ice-blue at high). Onset transients shake the camera.
  Slow orbital drift: camera pendulums gently left-right while the cave's domain-repeated
  geometry tiles seamlessly along Z. **First prototype where the viewer is inside the visual
  space** вАФ 205 prior prototypes render visuals on a surface; this renders a 3D room around you.
  **First SDF/ray-marching shader in the sandbox** вАФ no mesh geometry, pure math.
  **First prototype where bass physically deforms architecture** via live `smin` parameter.
  Demo mode (LFO oscillators) ¬Ј Mic mode (live FFT) ¬Ј WebGL required ¬Ј 4.9 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/206-sdf-cave/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 235 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/202-membrane-drum](/dream/202-membrane-drum)** вАФ Membrane Drum. `demoable`
  A circular drumhead solved with the 2D wave equation on a 64√Ч64 grid.
  Tap anywhere inside the drum to excite a Gaussian displacement вАФ the wave propagates
  outward from the strike point, reflects off the fixed circular rim, and creates standing
  wave patterns visible as a live blue/amber color map. Modal synthesis fires 6 oscillators
  at Bessel zero ratios (1.000 √Ч 1.593 √Ч 2.136 √Ч 2.295 √Ч 2.917 √Ч 3.598) вАФ the inharmonic
  overtones of a real circular membrane emerge from the physics, not from presets. Off-centre
  strikes emphasise asymmetric modes; centre strikes boost the symmetric (breathing) modes.
  Tension slider controls wave speed + fundamental (55вАУ143 Hz). Damping controls decay time.
  Waveform trace below shows the last 180 frames of centre-point displacement.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.96 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 233 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/200-harmonic-series](/dream/200-harmonic-series)** вАФ Harmonic Series Explorer. `demoable`
  Every pitched sound is the sum of sine waves at integer multiples of a fundamental. This prototype
  makes that structure visible and audible simultaneously. 16 partials displayed as togglable rows вАФ
  mute or solo any partial and hear the timbre change live. Eight instrument presets: Natural (1/n
  rolloff), Flute (almost pure fundamental), Clarinet (odd partials only вАФ a consequence of its closed
  cylindrical bore), Violin (dense harmonic cloud), Pipe Organ (all harmonics equal), Bell (inharmonic
  ratios вАФ BELL_RATIOS like 1.5, 2.47, 2.98 approximating real bell physics), Brass (partials 2вАУ5
  dominate), Oboe (complex alternating pattern). Each row shows a live animated sine trace and an
  amplitude bar. **рЯО§ Mic mode**: click Start mic to have the fundamental auto-lock to your pitch via
  4096-point parabolic-interpolated autocorrelation (same algorithm as `13-piano-canvas`). Row colors
  follow the `1-live` band palette (violet=low, amber/magenta=high). **First prototype dedicated to
  instrument-science education** вАФ lets you hear WHY a clarinet sounds hollow and a violin sounds warm.
  Zero permissions beyond optional mic ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј Web Audio API only.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/200-harmonic-series/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 231 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/198-osc-composer](/dream/198-osc-composer)** вАФ Oscilloscope Composer. `demoable`
  Design a Lissajous figure, then download the stereo WAV file that draws it on a real oscilloscope.
  Two sine oscillators at `n√Ч220 Hz` and `d√Ч220 Hz` are routed to separate stereo channels via
  `ChannelMergerNode` вАФ left channel = X axis, right channel = Y axis. Seven frequency ratios
  map to musical intervals (Unison through Minor 7th). Phase slider (0вАУ360¬∞) sweeps the figure
  through its family of shapes. X/Y balance slider stretches the aspect ratio. Traveling dot
  traces the parametric curve while audio plays. Puzzle mode: match a ghost target figure by ear
  and by eye. WAV download (вЖУ WAV): generates a 5-second 44.1 kHz 32-bit float stereo file in
  pure JS вАФ no server, no deps, works offline. Five named presets: Circle, Figure-8, Trefoil,
  Rose, Starburst. **First prototype to generate oscilloscope music** вАФ audio that draws itself.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј No mic needed.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/198-osc-composer/README.md`

  5.404√Ч partials, 4.5s decay). Ambient C3+G3 drone. Night-sky gradient.

## Previous (Cycle 227 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/195-chord-canvas](/dream/195-chord-canvas)** вАФ Chord Canvas. `demoable`
  Real-time chord detection from mic or demo audio. 12-bin chroma extraction (C2вАУC8 FFT, EMA
  smoothed) вЖТ dot-product match against 72 templates (12 roots √Ч 6 qualities: major, minor, dom7,
  m7, maj7, dim). Large chord name at top changes color by pitch class (chromatic wheel) and
  quality (major=vivid, minor=desaturated, dom7=warm shift, dim=near-grey). Scrolling timeline
  below: each chord is a colored block whose width = duration held; blocks fade with age. Bottom:
  12-bin chromagram with root pitch class highlighted. Demo: Dm7вЖТG7вЖТCmaj7вЖТBdim √Ч 3 reps.
  **First prototype to explicitly name musical structure** вАФ 194 prior prototypes visualize signal;
  this names the chord. Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.38 kB.

- **[/dream/193-anemone-tsl](/dream/193-anemone-tsl)** вАФ Anemone TSL. `demoable`
  A `TorusKnotGeometry(1.0, 0.22, 300, 36, 2, 3)` organism skinned with a custom GLSL
  ShaderMaterial. `uv.x` (0вЖТ1 along the tube path) drives three travelling waves at spatial
  frequencies 18.85, 37.70, and 94.25 cycles/tube вАФ bass rolls slowly, mid wrinkles, high-mid
  flutters. Sub-bass breathes the whole surface; onset transients send a burst that decays at
  √Ч0.88/frame. Spectral centroid shifts hue from violet (low, warm) to cyan (high, bright).
  Rim lighting adds bright cyan silhouettes; filmic tonemap for depth. Demo mode: 5 incommensurable
  LFOs. Mic mode: live audio via `useMicAnalyser`. OrbitControls auto-rotate; Bloom post-processing.
  **First torus-knot geometry in the sandbox; first travelling-wave GLSL displacement.**
  WebGL ¬Ј Two-screen start/run pattern ¬Ј `@react-three/fiber`.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/193-anemone-tsl/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 224 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/192-kids-magnet-notes](/dream/192-kids-magnet-notes)** вАФ Magnet Notes. `demoable`
  Six glowing pentatonic orbs (C3 E3 G3 A3 C4 E4) drift on a dark canvas. When two orbs come
  within 200px CSS of each other, magnetic attraction pulls them together and their triangle
  oscillators fade up as a soft chord (gain вИЭ proximity¬≤). When orbs **touch**, 24 sparkle
  particles burst at the collision point and both notes spike loud. **Tap** an orb to kick it
  toward its farthest partner. Tap open canvas to nudge all orbs outward. The app is autonomous вАФ
  orbs find each other unprompted, so a child can just watch and occasionally flick. Connection
  lines (gradient-colored, brightness вИЭ proximity¬≤) appear as orbs attract. Short plate reverb
  for warmth. **First kids prototype where proximity IS the chord** вАФ no tap needed to make music,
  just let the magnets do their work. For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.17 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 222 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/190-kids-wave-organ](/dream/190-kids-wave-organ)** вАФ Wave Organ. `demoable`
  Seven pentatonic organ pipes rise from a dark ocean floor, tallest (C3, violet) on the left to
  shortest (G4, rose) on the right вАФ BANDIMAL rule: taller = lower pitch. An autonomous wave rolls
  across the surface; when the water crests over a pipe's mouth, that pipe's triangle oscillator
  fades in with a 140ms attack. As the wave recedes the pipe fades out. At rest, C4/E4/G4 are
  already playing (a quiet C major chord). **Tap anywhere** to send a Gaussian wave surge that
  temporarily wakes the deeper, taller pipes (A3, G3, E3, and at strong surges, C3). Multiple
  taps stack for dramatic harmonic climaxes. Splash droplets arc up on each tap. Short plate
  reverb for warmth. **First kids prototype where continuous wave height = which notes play.**
  Autonomous wave and visual alive from load; audio starts on first tap (autoplay policy).
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.56 kB.

  flight**: the note fires at the destination, not at the tap point. 1вАУ2 second journey creates

  dissonance. Bigger lamp = lower pitch; child discovers this without instruction after 2вАУ3 releases.

## Previous (Cycle 219 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/187-shepard-tone](/dream/187-shepard-tone)** вАФ Shepard Tone. `demoable`
  The auditory illusion of the infinite staircase. Eight sine-wave oscillators (A1вАУA8, one
  octave apart) all rise in pitch simultaneously, weighted by a bell curve so only the middle
  range is audible. When any oscillator exits the top of the audible range, it's already inaudible
  (bell weight вЙИ 0), so the wrap-back to A1 is imperceptible вАФ the pitch ascends forever.
  **Left column**: 8 glowing circles, each sized by its current bell weight вАФ a wave of brightness
  sweeps upward continuously. **Right dial**: a clockface needle sweeps clockwise; one full rotation
  = one octave traversal (15s at default speed), its circular geometry mirroring the circular
  nature of the illusion. Controls: Rising/Falling toggle, SlowвЖТFast slider, Freeze (stops
  phase mid-spiral), Mic mode (amplitude вЖТ rate modulation). "What you hear is not what is
  physically happening" вАФ the most fundamental demonstration of constructive pitch perception.
  **First psychoacoustics prototype in the sandbox.** 186 prior prototypes visualize audio.
  This one reveals that your brain is actively constructing pitch from physical signals, and
  that construction can be deliberately tricked.
  Zero permissions (Start mode) ¬Ј Mic optional ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.38 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/187-shepard-tone/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 218 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/186-kids-breath-bloom](/dream/186-kids-breath-bloom)** вАФ Breath Bloom. `demoable`
  A breathing flower with five glowing petals вАФ one per note of the C-major pentatonic scale
  (C3 / E3 / G3 / A3 / C4). Each petal expands and contracts on a 9-second cosine breath
  cycle, staggered so they ripple around the center in a continuous wave вАФ **the flower is
  alive before the first tap**. Tap any petal: sparkle burst + note pulses louder. Tap empty
  canvas: all five petals bloom at once. Audio: triangle-wave oscillators with 3.5s
  impulse-response reverb; per-petal gain follows its breath phase smoothly.
  **"First kids prototype that breathes before any interaction."** 185 prior prototypes are
  static until touch. Breath Bloom is already animated on route load. Also the first with a
  cosine ease-in/ease-out breath curve вАФ the petals accelerate through the midpoint and
  decelerate at the extremes, like real breathing.
  Inspired by `166-kids-lantern` вЭ§пЄП, `133-kids-ripple-pond` вЭ§пЄП, `182-kids-crystal-song`.

## Previous (Cycle 217 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/185-score-structure](/dream/185-score-structure)** вАФ Score Structure. `demoable`
  The architecture of your improvisation, revealed in real time. Mic input (or demo iiвАУVвАУIвАУIV
  progression) вЖТ 12-bin **chroma extraction** вЖТ **chord detection** (24 major/minor templates,
  dot-product correlation) вЖТ scrolling **chord timeline** (right-to-left, block width = duration
  held, hue = root pitch class). Below the timeline: a live **chromagram** (12 vertical bars,
  root highlighted). Every 8 seconds: **section classifier** labels the current window as Intro /
  Build / Climax / Resolution / Coda based on onset density, chord-change rate, and spectral
  centroid. Three gauges (Density, Chord rate, Register) update in real time. Ghost section label
  floats in the canvas background. Demo: triangle-wave iiвАУVвАУIвАУIV in C, self-analyzed.
  First prototype to surface **musical structure** rather than signal вАФ 184 prior prototypes
  visualize FFT/pitch/timbre; this one reads compositional shape. Natural complement to
  `28-chord-canvas` (single chord), `24-piano-roll` (pitch roll), `22-code-score` (score).
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Mic or demo ¬Ј 185-score-structure ¬Ј cycle 217.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/185-score-structure/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 216 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/184-kids-gravity-harp](/dream/184-kids-gravity-harp)** вАФ Gravity Harp. `demoable`
  Six glowing horizontal strings on a dark canvas: C5/A4/G4/E4/D4/C4 (topвЖТbottom, BANDIMAL rule).
  Tap to drop a colored ball вАФ it falls through the strings, each one plucking a **Karplus-Strong**
  note as the ball passes. **Pass-through physics**: strings absorb 38% kinetic energy per crossing
  (vy √Ч 0.62) without reversing direction, so a ball traverses all 6 strings top-to-bottom then
  bounces off the floor and returns bottom-to-top вАФ a descending then ascending pentatonic scale
  from a single tap. Each string glows and visually vibrates (fundamental mode shape) on contact;
  sparks burst at the collision point. Up to 8 balls fall simultaneously.
  **"A mallet falling through a harp."** Key difference from `109-kids-bounce-notes` (wall bounce,
  1D): here strings are permeable energy-absorbers, and the entire pitch range is traversed per ball.
  2 demo balls auto-spawn (no permissions needed). Ambient C2 + G2 pad from first tap.
  Inspired by `169-kids-marble-run` вЭ§пЄП (physics+pitch), `105-pluck-field` вЭ§пЄП (KS synthesis).
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.57 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/184-kids-gravity-harp/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 215 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/183-piano-motion](/dream/183-piano-motion)** вАФ Piano Motion. `demoable`
  Watch a piano being played вАФ two cartoon hands (violet left / rose right) float above a
  61-key keyboard (C2вАУC7) and spring-animate to each key as music sounds. **Bass register
  (below C4)** drives the left hand; **treble (C4вАУC7)** drives the right. Active keys
  glow violet when pressed. Three modes: **Bach demo** (Invention No. 1 fragment, all notes
  pre-scheduled, both voices visible); **mic** (play live вАФ hands follow in real time via
  FFT peak detection per register); **recording** (paste a Resonance recording UUID вЖТ
  `/api/audio/[id]` вЖТ animated playback).
  Spring physics: k=0.12, damping=0.60 вАФ fast enough to track melodies, smooth enough to
  look like a real hand sliding rather than teleporting. First prototype visualizing the ACT
  of playing rather than the sound of it. Implements AGENT.md directive: "use his real piano
  tracks as the audio source."
  Inspired by PianoFlow (arxiv 2604.12856, ¬І229). Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј 4.34 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/183-piano-motion/README.md`

## Cycle 214 вАФ kids build

- **[/dream/182-kids-crystal-song](/dream/182-kids-crystal-song)** вАФ Crystal Song. `demoable`
  Six glowing crystal formations rise from a dark cave floor. **Taller crystal = lower pitch**
  (BANDIMAL rule). **Tap** to ring; **hold** to sustain a glass-bell note (3 sine partials:
  fundamental + octave + 2-octave, 2.2s decay on release). **Hold 4+ crystals together** вЖТ a
  resonance flash lights the whole cave. Crystals shimmer autonomously before first touch вАФ the
  cave is alive from the moment the page loads. Ambient C2 drone from first tap.
  **"First kids prototype with sustained tones and glass bell timbre."** 181 prior prototypes
  play on tap-down; this sustains while held. Completely distinct from KS pluck (marble-run,
  pluck-field), triangle wave, or pure sine вАФ the additive partials give a crystalline, slightly
  metallic ring. New dimension: a child discovers that holding longer = longer note (duration as
  a musical parameter). Four-crystal resonance rewards full-hand engagement.
  Inspired by `105-pluck-field` вЭ§пЄП (resonant physical synthesis), `166-kids-lantern` вЭ§пЄП
  (dark canvas + glowing objects to discover), `169-kids-marble-run` вЭ§пЄП (height-as-pitch).
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.07 kB.

  Five full-height canvas zones: рЯ™µ **Wood** (lowpass noise + 185Hz body thud) ¬Ј рЯФФ **Metal**
  (820Hz bandpass Q=18, 820ms bell ring) ¬Ј рЯТІ **Water** (noise sweeping 900вЖТ180Hz over 320ms)
  ¬Ј рЯ•Б **Earth** (72Hz sub-kick, 440ms decay) ¬Ј рЯЂЩ **Glass** (2440Hz sharp ping, 86ms).
  **Tap** any zone for a hit. **Hold** for a 12.5Hz rapid-fire roll. **Two fingers** вЖТ louder
  accent + zone-color full-screen flash. Visual textures visible before first tap: wavy wood
  grain, diagonal metal hatch, animated water waves, stippled earth dots, sparkle glass crosses.
  **"The first kids prototype about timbre, not pitch."** All 180 prior prototypes use C-major
  pentatonic вАФ the musical dimension is always high vs. low. Texture Drum asks: what does
  material sound like? A 3yo comparing Wood and Glass discovers instrumental timbre without
  any theory вАФ just ears and fingers.
  Inspired by Hitmachine (2025) + `98-kids-drum-circle` вЭ§пЄП + `105-pluck-field` вЭ§пЄП.

## Previous (Cycle 211 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/180-cellular](/dream/180-cellular)** вАФ Cellular. `demoable`
  Conway's Game of Life on a 64 √Ч 16 grid, where each column maps to a musical pitch (C2вЖТC5,
  log-spaced). Every Life generation tick fires triangle-wave notes for all columns containing
  at least one live cell. **Gliders** trace rising 4-note melodies that walk right and vanish.
  **Pulsars** (period-3 oscillators) produce repeating 2-bar loops. **R-pentomino** evolves
  chaotically for 1,103 generations вАФ sounds like free-jazz improv. **Acorn** grows for 5,206
  generations, melodically unpredictable throughout. **Random 20%** self-organizes from dense
  noise into rhythmic clusters over tens of seconds.
  Click/drag the canvas to draw or erase cells. BPM slider (40вАУ120) controls tick rate.
  **"What if generative music was also life?"** вАФ first sandbox prototype where the musical
  structure is not reactive (every frame) or API-generated (one-shot) but self-organizing:
  initial conditions вЖТ evolving autonomous composition. The spatial position of patterns IS their
  pitch: left = bass, right = treble, symmetry = acoustic balance.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.02 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/180-cellular/README.md`

- **[/dream/179-kids-voice-monster](/dream/179-kids-voice-monster)** вАФ Voice Monster. `demoable`
  A glowing blob-monster on a dark starry canvas. **Hum or sing** into the mic вАФ the monster
  grows with your amplitude and its color shifts with pitch (low=violet, mid=teal, high=rose).
  A hunger bar fills as you sing. After **30 accumulated seconds** of voice, the monster bounces
  excitedly then **sings back a melody** from the distinct pitches it detected (up to 8 notes,

  **"Feed me with your voice вАФ I'll sing back what I heard."**
  First kids prototype with a character that accumulates a memory of the child's singing.
  Inspired by Apr 2025 fMRI research: improvisation activates neural reward circuits more than

## Previous (Cycle 209 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/178-splat-bloom](/dream/178-splat-bloom)** вАФ Splat Bloom. `demoable`
  500 luminous oriented ellipses arranged in a **Gaussian cloud** around the canvas centre
  (ѕГ = 22% of canvas), rendered with `globalCompositeOperation = "screen"` вАФ overlapping
  splats add light, never occlude. The dense centre always blooms to near-white; sparse edges
  show individual coloured splats clearly. Star-cluster quality without explicit brightness control.
  **Bass** вЖТ nearest 100 splats bloom outward (scale √Ч1.6 at full bass, fade slightly).
  **Treble** вЖТ all 500 splats slowly rotate (field swirls at high treble).
  **Spectral centroid** вЖТ global hue target shifts violet (265¬∞) вЖФ amber (35¬∞) at 1¬∞/splat/frame.
  **Onset** вЖТ 50 random splats scatter with a velocity impulse; spring back (k = 0.015) over ~2 s.
  Demo mode: three LFOs. Mic mode: live FFT via `useMicAnalyser`.
  **"A painting that breathes."** Qualitatively different from particles (discrete) and fluid
  (density field) вАФ a *texture field*: statistically distributed, individually oriented, additively composited.
  Inspired by WebSplatter (¬І222, Feb 2026). Aligns with `130-tsl-particle-compute` вЭ§пЄП and `153-paint-compose` вЭ§пЄП.
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Mic optional ¬Ј 3.68 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 208 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/177-kids-lego-sequencer](/dream/177-kids-lego-sequencer)** вАФ Lego Beats рЯІ±. `demoable`
  A 2D **block sequencer** for kids (ages 3+). 8-step √Ч 6-pitch grid: each row is a note in the
  C-major pentatonic scale (C3вЖТE4), each column is one beat. Tap any block to activate it вАФ a
  white cursor sweeps left to right and plays every lit block, looping endlessly. **Drag** to paint
  a run of notes. **вИТ/+** buttons adjust BPM (40вАУ160). **вЬХ Clear** to start fresh.
  Visual: lego-brick blocks (rounded rect + plastic sheen + center stud) with bounce-and-glow
  animation on play. Ambient C3+G3 pad fills the silence between notes.
  **"Place bricks to build a melody."** First 2D pitch√Чtime grid in the kids zone вАФ all prior
  kids prototypes are 1D (single row of dots) or spatial (tap-anywhere). This introduces the
  piano-roll metaphor to kids: X = time, Y = pitch. Construction-as-composition.
  Inspired by BrickMusicTable (arxiv 2411.13224, Nov 2024): lego block grid sequencer validated
  with 150+ children aged 3вАУ13. Zero permissions, zero API, zero deps. 2.84 kB.
  Aligned with Karel's loves: `160-kids-paint-loop` вЭ§пЄП (visual composition вЖТ playback),
  `98-kids-drum-circle` вЭ§пЄП (beat construction).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/177-kids-lego-sequencer/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 207 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/176-sdf-cave](/dream/176-sdf-cave)** вАФ Cave. `demoable`
  A WebGL1 fragment shader renders a stone cave interior via **SDF ray-marching** вАФ the first
  sandbox prototype where the viewer is *inside* the visual space. Camera orbits slowly inside
  the chamber looking toward centre. **Bass** drives the `smin` blend factor (0.05вЖТ0.68): stalactites
  and walls melt together on heavy bass, crystallise on silence. **Treble** roughens the stone
  surface via value-noise displacement. **Spectral centroid** shifts the cave glow from deep
  violet (bass-heavy) to ice blue (treble-heavy). **Onset** shakes the camera and pulses the
  surfaces white. Demo mode: three slow LFOs simulate a breathing cave with no audio output.
  Mic mode: live 6-band FFT drives all parameters via `useMicAnalyser`.
  **"You are inside a space that breathes with your music."** Completely new visual paradigm вАФ
  175 prior prototypes render visuals *on* the canvas plane; this one puts you inside the geometry.
  SDF ray-marching + inline GLSL, zero deps, zero API. Renders at 55% CSS resolution for
  comfortable 60fps on mid-range GPUs.
  Influenced by `107-ocean-presence` вЭ§пЄП (immersive environment), `84-wave-fluid` вЭ§пЄП (GPU-only path).
  Research basis: MUTEK 2026 Spha√Ѓra (¬І224), Revision 2026 Shader Showdown (¬І225).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/176-sdf-cave/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 205 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/175-vocal-choir](/dream/175-vocal-choir)** вАФ Vocal Choir. `demoable`
  Sing or hum a note вАФ three harmony voices materialise around you in 3D space via HRTF
  spatialization: a **major third** (violet, upper-left, azimuth вИТ45¬∞), a **perfect fifth**
  (teal, upper-right, azimuth +45¬∞), and a **bass octave** (rose, below, elevation вИТ20¬∞).
  All three follow every pitch change with 50ms portamento вАФ smooth glides as you move
  between notes. Canvas: four glowing orbs in a choir formation, orb radius breathing with
  amplitude. A live note-name label ("C3", "GвЩѓ4") tracks the detected pitch.
  **"You sing one voice. Three more appear."** All 174 prior prototypes are either reactive
  (audio вЖТ visuals) or generative (API вЖТ audio). Vocal Choir does both: your voice is the
  input, and the output wraps spatially back around you. First choir prototype in the sandbox.
  Different from `23-pitch-harmonize` (raw pitch-shift, same timbre) вАФ this generates
  additive voices at distinct frequencies for genuinely separate harmonic parts.
  Wear headphones ¬Ј Mic optional (demo mode) ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.2 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 204 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/174-kids-raindrop-rhythm](/dream/174-kids-raindrop-rhythm)** вАФ Raindrop Rhythm (kids). `demoable`
  Three glowing clouds in a dark sky вАФ violet (C3), amber (G3), rose (C4). **Tap any cloud** вЖТ
  3-5 glowing teardrops scatter and fall with gravity + gentle sine drift. **Hold a cloud** вЖТ
  continuous rain (one drop per 200ms). When a drop lands on the water surface below, it rings
  a bell note and leaves an expanding ripple. Auto-rain cycles through all three clouds every
  second so the canvas is never silent. C3+G3+C4 form a C major arpeggio вАФ any combination of
  clouds sounds consonant.
  **"The note plays when the drop lands, not when you tap."** Same delay-as-pedagogy principle
  as `133-kids-ripple-pond` вЭ§пЄП (two waves meet вЖТ chord) and `171-kids-snow-globe` (snowflake
  lands вЖТ bell). Here the child has control over *which pitch* (which cloud) and *how much rain*
  (tap vs hold), making this the most musically intentional of the three landing-note prototypes.
  Directly inspired by `169-kids-marble-run` вЭ§пЄП (physics = music) and Karel's loves of
  `133-kids-ripple-pond` вЭ§пЄП, `166-kids-lantern` вЭ§пЄП (hidden discovery). Three-cloud polyphony:
  different children can each "own" a color and contribute separate voices.
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.82 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/174-kids-raindrop-rhythm/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 202 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/173-kids-garden-bloom](/dream/173-kids-garden-bloom)** вАФ Garden Bloom (kids). `demoable`
  Hold the soil to grow a glowing flower вАФ stem rises, petals unfold one by one as notes.
  Hold 0.75s = 1 petal; 2s = 3-note chord; 4s = full 5-petal pentatonic chord.
  X position sets timbre: violet/piano ¬Ј amber/bells ¬Ј teal/pluck ¬Ј rose/pad.
  Release вЖТ flower loops its chord softly. Six flowers fill the garden вЖТ grand staggered
  chord вЖТ 12-second ceremonial sway-and-fade вЖТ garden resets. Demo flowers pre-planted
  at startup. **First kids prototype where hold duration = accumulating musical growth.**

## Previous (Cycle 201 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/172-loop-station](/dream/172-loop-station)** вАФ Loop Station. `demoable`
  4-slot phase-locked loop station вАФ pure Web Audio API. **Load demo** synthesizes four loops
  offline (sub-bass drone, pentatonic melody, high arpeggio, rhythm pattern) and starts them
  all on the same beat grid with no permissions needed. **Tap REC** on any slot to record from
  mic; the loop closes on the next tap and snaps to the beat-1 boundary вАФ a 1-bar loop and a
  2-bar loop automatically stay in rhythmic alignment. Per-slot bar-length picker (1/2/4 bars),
  MUTE (crossfade to zero), вЬХ (clear). Waveform canvas per slot shows the recorded buffer as
  amplitude bars with a sweeping playhead while looping. TAP TEMPO sets BPM from median
  inter-tap interval. **"Build a multi-layer performance in real time."**
  This is the first sandbox prototype about *constructing* a composition вАФ not reacting to audio,
  not generating via API, but layering loops deliberately. The phase-lock is the key surprise:
  close a second loop and it snaps into rhythmic alignment with the first, no quantization step.
  Influenced by Karel's loves of `153-paint-compose` вЭ§пЄП, `138-lmdm-echo` вЭ§пЄП, `148-spatial-palette` вЭ§пЄП.
  Demo path: Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 4.55 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/172-loop-station/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 200 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/171-kids-snow-globe](/dream/171-kids-snow-globe)** вАФ Snow Globe (kids). `demoable`
  Tap anywhere on a dark night sky вАФ a burst of 5вАУ8 glowing snowflakes scatter from the touch
  point and drift down with gentle sinusoidal wobble. **The note plays when the flake lands**, not
  when you tap: a triangle-wave bell chime rings on touchdown, colored sparks burst at the landing
  point. **Tap high on the screen вЖТ high note** (rose = C4); **tap low вЖТ low note** (violet = C3).
  Five pitches across C-major pentatonic. Hold a finger for continuous snowfall ("blizzard mode").
  Demo mode auto-rains from center-height for 3.5 s on first open вАФ shows the mechanic before any
  touch. 60 golden-ratio stars twinkle in the deep navy background. Faint snow glow at the ground.
  Soft C3+E3+G3 ambient pad throughout.
  **"First kids prototype where LANDING is the musical event вАФ not the tap."**
  170 prior kids prototypes play a note on gesture (tap-down, drag, hold). Snow Globe plays when
  physics resolves: the child taps, watches the flake fall, then hears the ground ring. Cause and
  effect are separated by ~0.5вАУ1.4 s of gravity вАФ the same delay-as-pedagogy principle that makes
  `133-kids-ripple-pond` вЭ§пЄП so effective (two ripples meet вЖТ chord). The "high up = high note"
  mapping is self-discovering: after one tap at the top and one at the bottom, a 3yo has the model.
  Directly inspired by Karel's loves of `133-kids-ripple-pond` вЭ§пЄП (physics delay = music),
  `100-kids-paint-song` вЭ§пЄП (tap gesture = music), `152-kids-star-paint` вЭ§пЄП (dark sky + sparkles).
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.76 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/171-kids-snow-globe/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 199 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/170-spectral-morph](/dream/170-spectral-morph)** вАФ Spectral Morph. `demoable`
  Drag the morph slider to blend the harmonic spectrum between two waveforms. 40 sine
  partials (harmonics of C3) are amplitude-interpolated independently вАФ at 50% you hear
  a genuine acoustic hybrid between, say, a sawtooth and a sine: a timbre that exists
  between them, not a volume crossfade. Three stacked bar charts show Source A (dim),
  the live blend (bright), and Source B (dim) in real time as you drag. Source picker:
  Sawtooth / Triangle / Square / Sine for both A and B вАФ mix any two.
  **"The first sandbox prototype to synthesize audio from spectral manipulation, not just
  analyze it."** 169 prior prototypes use FFT for read-out (AnalyserNode) вАФ this one uses
  it as a compositional parameter: the harmonic amplitude vector IS the instrument.
  Formulas are exact Fourier series (sawtooth=1/k, triangle=1/k¬≤ odd k, square=1/k odd k).
  Inspired by Karel's loves of `153-paint-compose` вЭ§пЄП and `138-lmdm-echo` вЭ§пЄП (audio as
  transformable material). Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј 2.79 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/170-spectral-morph/README.md`

---

## Previous (Cycle 198 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/169-kids-marble-run](/dream/169-kids-marble-run)** вАФ Marble Music (kids). `demoable`
  Draw glowing colored ramps on a dark canvas вАФ then drop marbles and watch them fall,
  bounce off the ramps, and play Karplus-Strong pluck notes on each collision.
  Ramp color encodes pitch: **high ramp = high note** (rose=E4 top, violet=C3 bottom),
  the same physical analogy as string length on a real instrument вАФ a child discovers it
  without needing any explanation. Three demo ramps are pre-loaded so the canvas plays
  immediately. Marbles auto-launch every 4 seconds (up to 6 live); tap **Drop рЯОµ** for
  instant addition. Draw new ramps with a finger drag (>30px). **Clear** resets to the
  demo layout.
  **"First kids prototype where the child builds the machine before the music plays."**
  All 168 prior kids prototypes are reactive (tap/drag вЖТ immediate note). Marble Music
  separates construction from performance: draw first, then observe what physics makes.
  Three cognitive layers: passive (watch), active (drop more marbles), constructive
  (redesign the ramp layout to change the melody). Inspired by Karel's loves of
  `105-pluck-field` вЭ§пЄП (KS synthesis), `133-kids-ripple-pond` вЭ§пЄП (physics = music),
  `100-kids-paint-song` вЭ§пЄП (drawing = music). Culturally validated: BooSnoo (2026),
  Sago Mini Music Machine (2026), Wintergarten Marble Machine (viral).
  For kids 4+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.24 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/169-kids-marble-run/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 197 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/168-piano-roll](/dream/168-piano-roll)** вАФ Piano Roll. `demoable`
  Play piano into your mic вАФ each note appears as a glowing colored bar scrolling left.
  Pitch sets the vertical row (C2 bottom to C6 top, 48 semitones); color shifts from
  violet (low) to red (high), matching the `1-live` band palette. Black-key rows are
  slightly darker, giving a familiar keyboard reference without being distracting.
  The "now" cursor sits near the right edge; the currently-detected note shows a live
  tail extending to it вАФ you see the pitch in real time, not just after it ends.
  Note name (e.g. "FвЩѓ4") updates in the status bar while held.
  **Demo mode** plays a 26-note C major passage; notes scroll in from the right like a
  player-piano roll. **BPM slider** adjusts scroll speed (30вАУ200).
  **"First notation-style prototype in the sandbox."** All 167 prior prototypes visualize
  audio as abstract art (fluid, particles, terrain) or physics (pendulums, ripples).
  Piano Roll renders recognizable musical information вАФ a pianist sees their phrases as
  intervals, scales, and rhythm. Natural triptych with `13-piano-canvas` (abstract painting)
  and `22-code-score` (writeвЖТplay). Influenced by `138-lmdm-echo` вЭ§пЄП and `153-paint-compose` вЭ§пЄП.
  Mic optional (demo mode) ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.59 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/168-piano-roll/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 195 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/167-aria-companion](/dream/167-aria-companion)** вАФ Aria. `demoable`
  Play piano into your mic. After two seconds of silence, Aria responds вАФ a phrase
  built by walking a Markov bigram of your own note transitions. The table grows
  across the session: by the 5th call-and-response, Aria sounds like she's been
  listening carefully. Two-panel scrolling piano roll: YOU (warm orange bars) on
  top, ARIA (cool blue bars) below, both scrolling at 80px/s with a live-tail
  for the currently-detected note. Demo button (no mic): plays a C-pentatonic
  phrase and fires the first response in ~5s.
  **"The piano responds when you rest."** 166 prior prototypes are reactive (every
  frame) or generative (one-shot API call). Aria is the first **dialogue** prototype:
  listens, waits, then composes a response from what it heard. Zero ML inference,
  zero API, zero deps вАФ the Markov chain is ~25 lines of JS.
  Mic optional (demo mode) ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.88 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/167-aria-companion/README.md`

- **[/dream/166-kids-lantern](/dream/166-kids-lantern)** вАФ Night Garden (kids). `demoable`
  A near-black canvas hides 16 pentatonic stars вАФ each one holds a note. Hold your finger
  anywhere and a warm amber lantern follows: stars within the radius glow and play their pitch
  (triangle waves, C-major pentatonic C3вАУA4). Stars outside stay as a faint twinkle.
  Move slowly вЖТ arpeggio as individual stars enter the light. Hold still вЖТ sustained harmony.
  Sweep broadly вЖТ a moving chord across the canvas.
  **"First kids prototype about exploration and revelation."** 165 prior prototypes respond to
  explicit gestures (tap, draw, drag, rhythm). Night Garden has no buttons, no tap targets.
  The whole canvas is one gesture field; the lantern is the key. A 3yo discovers that moving
  their finger slightly reveals a new sound; an older child deliberately hunts for all 16 stars.
  Inspired by Karel's loves of `133-kids-ripple-pond` вЭ§пЄП (canvas = instrument),

## Previous (Cycle 193 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/165-cymatics](/dream/165-cymatics)** вАФ Cymatics. `demoable`
  Chladni plate standing-wave patterns from audio. Each audio frequency resonates a
  distinct nodal-line pattern on a virtual square plate. 25 modes from simple (1,1) to
  complex (5,5) вАФ a four-lobed cross, six-petaled flower, 50-cell symmetric grid.
  **Demo mode** sweeps a sine oscillator through all 25 modes at 3.5 s each; the pattern
  transforms continuously. **Recording mode** accepts a Resonance recording UUID вЖТ
  `/api/audio/[id]` вЖТ Karel's actual piano recordings drive mode selection in real time.
  Color follows the dominant FFT band (violet=bass, cyan=low-mid, emerald=mid, amber=high).
  **"Chladni figures are the literal physics behind the name 'Resonance.' This is what
  the app is named for вАФ acoustic standing waves made visible."**
  Inspired by `138-lmdm-echo` вЭ§пЄП (Karel's recordings as audio input), `84-wave-fluid` вЭ§пЄП
  (fluid physics visuals), `105-pluck-field` вЭ§пЄП (physical modeling). Fulfills AGENT.md
  directive: "let his existing music be the input."
  Zero permissions (demo) ¬Ј Recording-ID input ¬Ј Zero new deps ¬Ј 3.75 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 192 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/164-kids-pendulum-harp](/dream/164-kids-pendulum-harp)** вАФ Pendulum Harp (kids). `demoable`
  Five glowing pendulums hang from a bar at the top of a dark canvas. Each pendulum
  is a different length вЖТ different natural period вЖТ different speed of oscillation.
  Each time a bob swings through the bottom of its arc it plucks a pentatonic note
  (sine wave, bell-like decay). Longer pendulum = lower note = bigger bob (BANDIMAL
  rule). Tap any pendulum to push it вАФ tap more pendulums and their different periods
  create an emergent polyrhythm that never simply repeats. All five start displaced
  on alternating sides so the canvas immediately plays without any touch needed.
  Sparkle burst on each pluck. C3+G3 ambient pad throughout.
  **"First kids prototype where physics sets the rhythm вАФ not the child's timing."**
  163 prior prototypes fire notes on tap/drag/draw events. Pendulum Harp fires notes
  when a physically-simulated bob reaches its natural turning point. The child adds
  energy; gravity decides when each note plays. Same surprise-discovery mechanic as
  `133-kids-ripple-pond` вЭ§пЄП (collision fires the chord) and `109-kids-bounce-notes`
  (wall bounce fires the note), but now the timing emerges from pendulum physics rather
  than collision events.
  Directly inspired by Karel's loves of `105-pluck-field` вЭ§пЄП (tactile pluck = immediate note),
  `98-kids-drum-circle` вЭ§пЄП (polyrhythm), `133-kids-ripple-pond` вЭ§пЄП (physics = music).
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.8 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/164-kids-pendulum-harp/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 191 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/163-paths-visualizer](/dream/163-paths-visualizer)** вАФ Paths Visualizer. `demoable`
  Lorenz strange-attractor visualization that responds to audio in real time. The attractor
  trail is colored by frequency band (sub-bass = violet, treble = pink); bass energy drives the
  orbit scale; treble sharpens the line width. Six radial bloom gradients (one per band) pulse
  around the canvas center. A bass-onset ring expands on strong beats. **Demo mode** plays a
  synthesized piano phrase so the visualization works with zero setup. **Live mode** accepts a
  Resonance recording ID вАФ calls `/api/audio/[id]` for a signed URL, routes the `<audio>` element
  through `MediaElementAudioSourceNode вЖТ AnalyserNode вЖТ destination`, and visualizes Karel's
  actual piano recordings from the Paths.
  **"First prototype that uses Karel's own recordings as the audio source for AV visualization."**
  Directly fulfills AGENT.md directive: "let his existing music be the input." Connected to love
  signal `138-lmdm-echo` вЭ§пЄП (Karel's piano phrase analyzed + echoed) вАФ extends that concept to
  full-track real-time visualization.
  Demo mode ¬Ј Recording ID input ¬Ј Zero new deps ¬Ј 2.9 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/163-paths-visualizer/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 190 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/162-kids-bubble-pop](/dream/162-kids-bubble-pop)** вАФ Bubble Pop (kids). `demoable`
  Colorful glowing bubbles drift upward through a dark canvas, swaying gently. Five bubble colors
  map to five pentatonic pitches (violet=C3, emerald=E3, amber=G3, rose=A3, cyan=C4).
  **Tap any bubble to pop it** вАФ sparkle burst (18 particles) + triangle-wave note. **Drag your
  finger** across bubbles to pop a chain and play a fast melody or glissando. Bigger bubbles
  sing lower (BANDIMAL rule: radius 52вЖТ20). Two-oscillator triangle pair (+7¬Ґ detuning) gives
  each note warmth; lower pitches ring longer (C3 = 0.72s decay, C4 = 0.40s). Bubbles respawn
  continuously вАФ 10 seeded at start, new one every 1.2вАУ1.9s, cap of 14 live. C3+G3 ambient pad
  keeps the canvas alive between pops. Fade-in on spawn (500ms), pop-ring expansion animation.
  **"First prototype where destruction is the musical act."** 161 prior prototypes reward touching,
  holding, or drawing. Bubble Pop rewards the pop вАФ the release вАФ the burst.
  Inspired by Karel's love of `105-pluck-field` вЭ§пЄП (tactile pluck = immediate note), `95-kids-breath-bubbles`
  (bubble aesthetic, inverted mechanic), `152-kids-star-paint` вЭ§пЄП (sparkle burst visual language).
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.62 kB.

- **[/dream/160-kids-paint-loop](/dream/160-kids-paint-loop)** вАФ Loop Garden (kids). `demoable`
  Draw a freehand glowing stroke anywhere вЖТ it immediately loops as a pentatonic melody.
  **Four color zones** give four timbres: left=violet/piano (sine, crisp attack), mid-left=amber/bells
  (sine +8¬Ґ detune, warm decay), mid-right=teal/chime (sine вИТ6¬Ґ detune, shorter decay),
  right=rose/pads (sine, slow 70ms attack, long sustain). **Y position = pitch** (C-major
  pentatonic, C3 bottom вЖТ C5 top). A glowing traversal dot sweeps each stroke's path in loop.
  Up to 4 simultaneous loops. **Tap any stroke to delete it** вАФ sparkle burst. Clear resets.
  Demo mode seeds 3 loops at canvas open so the garden is immediately alive.
  **"First kids prototype combining freehand drawing + multi-timbral loop station."**
  Inspired by Karel's love of `153-paint-compose` вЭ§пЄП (adult version), `100-kids-paint-song` вЭ§пЄП,
  `111-kids-shape-loop` вЭ§пЄП. Extends the drawing-as-music lineage into layered multi-timbral territory.

## Previous (Cycle 187 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/159-synesthetic-sketch](/dream/159-synesthetic-sketch)** вАФ Synesthetic Sketch. `demoable`
  Music as shape вАФ not just color. Every audio feature maps to a different visual dimension on
  an accumulating Canvas2D: **spectral centroid вЖТ hue** (violet=low, rose=high), **spectral spread
  вЖТ shape** (circle=pure sine, triangle=slight overtones, square=mid spread, hexagon=wide spread,
  star=complex noise), **harmonic richness вЖТ inner ring count** (0вАУ4 concentric rings),
  **amplitude вЖТ object scale**, **onset вЖТ spark burst**. Objects deposit every 4 frames with
  additive blending вАФ overlapping shapes brighten, building a nebula-like visual record of the
  session. A 0.3%/frame fade prevents burn-in; the canvas takes ~3 min to naturally clear.
  **"First prototype to map audio to morphological shape вАФ not just color."**
  158 prior prototypes map audio to color, fluid, particles, or geometry. None classify audio
  by *shape type*. A pure sine leaves circles; a chord leaves hexagons with inner rings; percussion
  leaves star bursts. The canvas is the acoustic biography of the session.
  Download as PNG ¬Ј Demo mode (no mic) ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 4.28 kB.
  Influenced by Karel's loves: `153-paint-compose` вЭ§пЄП (accumulating visual artifacts),
  `130-tsl-particle-compute` вЭ§пЄП (rich visual output from audio), `84-wave-fluid` вЭ§пЄП.
  Research basis: musicolors (arxiv 2503.14220, multi-dimensional synesthetic visualization).
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/159-synesthetic-sketch/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 186 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/158-kids-hum-paint](/dream/158-kids-hum-paint)** вАФ Voice Painting (kids). `demoable`
  Sing or hum вАФ your voice becomes a colored painting on a dark canvas. **High notes fly up,
  low notes drift down** (log-scale pitch вЖТ Y position). **Every pitch glows in its own hue**
  (low voice = warm amber/violet, high voice = cool cyan/rose). Amplitude controls stroke width:
  sing louder for a thicker brush. The painting accumulates in a left-to-right scroll, wrapping
  at the edge to fill the screen over a long session. Press **вЦґ Hear it!** вАФ up to 56 sampled
  notes from the session play back as sine tones, replaying the session as a short melody.
  Demo mode auto-draws **Twinkle Twinkle** (no mic needed) вАФ the visual shape of the opening
  CвАУCвАУGвАУGвАУAвАУAвАУG is recognizable as a pattern of flat stripes and jumps.
  **"First kids prototype where your voice is the paintbrush."** All 157 prior prototypes use
  touch (tap, drag, draw). This one replaces gesture with voice, unlocking vocal music-making
  without any reading or instruction. A 3yo humming randomly sees their voice trace a path;
  an older child can try to match the demo's color pattern by singing the right notes.
  Inspired by Karel's love of `100-kids-paint-song` вЭ§пЄП, `152-kids-star-paint` вЭ§пЄП, and the
  KIDS.md seeded idea `kids-hum-to-paint`.
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Mic optional ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.3 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 185 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/157-concept-steer](/dream/157-concept-steer)** вАФ Concept Steer. `demoable`
  A hexagonal radar chart where each of six vertices controls a named musical dimension:
  **Brightness** (filter), **Density** (BPM + voices), **Regularity** (timing jitter),
  **Complexity** (chord voicing), **Energy** (attack + gain), **Mode** (majorвЖТdim).
  Drag any vertex вАФ the polygon reshapes and the synthesizer tracks immediately.
  Live chord name label updates as Complexity √Ч Mode shift (C вЖТ Csus4 вЖТ Cm вЖТ Cdim9 etc.).
  Four presets: Classical Fugue, Dark Ambient, Jazz Improv, Drone.
  **"Navigate music as a space of named concepts вАФ not moods, not knobs."**
  The six axes come from sparse autoencoder research (arxiv 2505.18186, May 2026): they are
  what music AI models actually learn internally. This UI makes those implicit axes explicit.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.23 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/157-concept-steer/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 184 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/156-kids-star-connect](/dream/156-kids-star-connect)** вАФ Constellation Song (kids). `demoable`
  Thirteen glowing stars pre-placed on a dark sky in three loose clusters. **Draw a line from one
  star to a neighboring star** вАФ both pitches ring as a two-voice interval (triangle waves, 1.8s
  decay). **Close a triangle** (connect all three sides) вЖТ three-note chord plays with staggered
  onset, the triangle interior shimmers pale blue, and 15 colored sparkles radiate from the centroid.
  Star color encodes pitch class: violet=C, emerald=E, amber=G, rose=A, cyan=C5.
  Soft C3+G3 ambient pad throughout. вЖЇ Clear resets all connections.
  **"First prototype where the musical structure is hidden in the sky вАФ the child reveals it by connecting."**
  All 155 prior prototypes produce sound from tapping, dragging, or freehand drawing. This one
  produces an interval only when you connect two existing stars (the *relationship* is the sound),
  and a chord only when three stars form a closed triangle (the *graph structure* is the music theory).
  Companion to `152-kids-star-paint` вЭ§пЄП вАФ that one creates stars, this one reveals them.
  Inspired by Karel's love of `152-kids-star-paint` вЭ§пЄП and `148-spatial-palette` вЭ§пЄП.
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.7 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/156-kids-star-connect/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 183 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/155-piano-hands](/dream/155-piano-hands)** вАФ Piano Hands. `demoable`
  Canvas piano keyboard (C3вАУB4, 2 octaves). **Ghost fingers descend from above and press
  the keys as notes play.** Pitch class вЖТ finger hue: C=violet, E=warm-green, G=amber,
  A=rose, B=magenta. Light trail above each active finger; keys illuminate in the finger's
  hue while pressed. **Demo**: F√Љr Elise opening, AudioContext-scheduled triangle-wave
  oscillators, fingers synced via 16ms look-ahead queue. **Mic**: autocorrelation pitch
  detection (4096-sample time-domain) each rAF frame вАФ detected MIDI spawns a finger,
  silence for 320ms lifts all.
  **"The pitch becomes a hand вАФ the chord is visible before you read the labels."**
  First prototype where the piano keyboard is the visual output, not a control surface.
  Inspired by PianoFlow (arXiv:2604.12856). Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј Mic optional.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/155-piano-hands/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 182 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/154-kids-clap-back](/dream/154-kids-clap-back)** вАФ Clap Back (kids). `demoable`
  A call-and-response rhythm game for 4-year-olds. The prototype plays a 4-beat pattern вАФ
  **violet circle glows bright on active beats, dim on rests** вАФ then turns **green** ("your turn!")
  and runs the same 4-beat clock again. Child taps the screen on the active beats. **Big sparks**
  (22 particles) for on-beat taps within ¬±165ms (¬±22% of beat); **small sparks** (9 particles) for
  off-beat taps вАФ never wrong, always rewarded, just bigger for timing. C4/E4/G4/A4 triangle plucks.
  Four beat-indicator dots below the circle show the pattern's shape. Soft C3+G3 ambient pad.
  **5 patterns cycle from easy to syncopated**: all-4 (learn tempo) вЖТ skip-3 вЖТ skip-2 вЖТ skip-4 вЖТ backbeat (2+4 only).
  **"First kids prototype where WHEN you tap is the parameter вАФ not what you tap or where."**
  All 153 prior prototypes reward any tap within 50ms. Clap Back rewards timing: the same tap at a
  different moment in the 750ms beat window produces a dramatically different visual response.
  The three-phase color cycle (violet/demo вЖТ green/wait вЖТ cyan/listen) gives a child a clear
  procedural cue with no text needed. Inspired by Karel's love of `98-kids-drum-circle` вЭ§пЄП.
  For kids 4+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.63 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/154-kids-clap-back/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 181 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/153-paint-compose](/dream/153-paint-compose)** вАФ Paint Compose. `demoable`
  Dark canvas with 7-color palette, 3 brush sizes, BPM slider (40вАУ160). **Paint a stroke вАФ it
  immediately begins looping as a melody.** Stroke geometry is the musical score: Y position at each
  sampled point вЖТ pentatonic pitch (C2 bottom, C5 top); hue вЖТ waveform (warm=sawtooth, cool=sine,
  mid=triangle); X centroid вЖТ stereo pan; brush width вЖТ amplitude; arc length вЖТ note count (2вАУ8).
  All voices loop simultaneously at the shared BPM. Flash animations travel along each stroke's note
  points, making the melody visible as a moving light sequence. Max 6 voices; oldest evicted on
  overflow вАФ you edit by painting over. Clear resets; вЖУ PNG saves the painting.
  **"What if painting and composing were the same act?"**
  Diagonal stroke = glissando. Wavy line = phrase rocking between registers. Horizontal = drone.
  Warm colors (rose/amber вЖТ sawtooth) sit forward in the mix; cool colors (cyan/blue вЖТ sine) recede.
  You mix by choosing hues.
  Inspired by ViTex (arXiv:2603.01984, Mar 2026) and Karel's love of `100-kids-paint-song` вЭ§пЄП.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.42 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/153-paint-compose/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 180 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/152-kids-star-paint](/dream/152-kids-star-paint)** вАФ Star Song (kids). `demoable`
  A dark night sky with 90 twinkling background stars. **Drag anywhere** вАФ every ~46 px of travel
  a glowing 5-pointed star appears and plays a **Karplus-Strong pluck** (bell-like string resonance,
  pre-computed 2.5 s buffer per pitch). Y position = pitch: top of screen = C5 bright, bottom =
  C3 deep; 9 pitches across C-major pentatonic. Stars connect with glowing constellation lines as
  you draw. Lift your finger to lock the constellation into the sky. After 16 s, the constellation
  **arpeggios itself** вАФ unique pitches replay from high to low over 3 s вАФ then fades over 3.5 s.
  Up to 6 constellations coexist simultaneously, each on its own 22.5 s lifecycle. Soft C3+E3+G3
  ambient pad throughout. Hint text fades after 9 s ("Draw across the sky вЬ¶").
  **"First kids prototype where the path you draw persists in the sky and then sings back at you."**
  150 prior prototypes are reactive (draw вЖТ immediate sound) or ephemeral (fades immediately).
  Star Song is the first where a drawing remains visible for 22 s after you stop, then spontaneously
  sings its own arpeggio вАФ a small surprise reward for patience and long sessions. A child who
  draws a big swooping arc from bottom-left to top-right hears a full ascending scale 16 seconds
  later. A child who makes quick dots near the top hears high plucks. The spatial memory of
  "where I drew" persists as the sound memory of "what notes I made."
  Inspired by Karel's love of `105-pluck-field` вЭ§пЄП (KS synthesis) and `100-kids-paint-song` вЭ§пЄП
  (drawing = music). Combines both: KS timbre + drawing-as-music + persistent visual artifact.
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.86 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/152-kids-star-paint/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 179 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/151-ritual-compose](/dream/151-ritual-compose)** вАФ Oracle. `demoable`
  Three ancient coins on a dark canvas. **Tap to toss all three simultaneously** вАФ six times.
  Each toss produces one hexagram line: heads majority = yang (solid), tails = yin (broken).
  After six tosses the complete hexagram appears (1 of 64), with its Chinese character, King Wen
  name, and a 2-sentence poetic interpretation. Press **Generate Journey Music** вЖТ `fal-ai/lyria3/pro`
  receives a music prompt derived from the hexagram's meaning and returns 30s of ambient music that
  plays through the 6-band bloom radial visualizer. **"Re-cast"** resets everything.
  **"First prototype to treat a Resonance session as a ritual act."**
  149 prior prototypes respond in real time. Oracle requires ceremony: six deliberate taps before
  any music appears. The I Ching's 64-hexagram emotional vocabulary maps onto music surprisingly
  well вАФ hexagram 29 (The Abysmal) вЖТ deep water resonance; hexagram 58 (The Joyous) вЖТ bright
  arpeggios; hexagram 52 (Keeping Still) вЖТ mountain silence, sustained single drone.
  ~$0.08/generation ¬Ј FAL_KEY ¬Ј Zero new npm deps ¬Ј 9.76 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/151-ritual-compose/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 178 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/150-kids-beat-builder](/dream/150-kids-beat-builder)** вАФ Beat Builder (kids). `demoable`
  A two-row, 6-step loop sequencer. **Top row = melody** (6 cool-colored dots, C major pentatonic
  C3вЖТE4). **Bottom row = drums** (6 warm-colored dots: rose=kick, amber=snare, emerald=hi-hat,
  cyan=tom, pink=clap, violet=shaker). One sweeping cursor crosses both rows simultaneously. Tap
  any dot to light it; the cursor fires it each time it passes. BPM ¬±16 buttons (40вАУ160). Clear resets
  all. Ambient C3/E3/G3 pad runs from start.
  **"First kids prototype with two simultaneous tracks вАФ melody above, drums below."**
  All 149 prior kids prototypes use a single sound type per tap event. Beat Builder is the first
  where the child operates two independent musical layers in one grid. The emergent discovery:
  melody notes placed on the same column as a drum hit land on a percussive accent вАФ the child hears
  this without any explanation and starts placing notes deliberately.
  Drum synthesis identical to `98-kids-drum-circle` вЭ§пЄП. Sequencer grid from `145-kids-dot-seq`.
  For kids 4+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.81 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/150-kids-beat-builder/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 176 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/149-kids-color-mix](/dream/149-kids-color-mix)** вАФ Color Mix (kids). `demoable`
  Three large colored circles on a dark canvas вАФ rose (C3), amber (E3), violet (G3) вАФ placed
  in a triangle. **Drag any circle** to move it. When two circles overlap their colors blend
  (screen compositing: rose+amber=orange, rose+violet=magenta, amber+violet=warm green) and
  their notes get louder together. When all three converge: the overlap zone glows **bright
  white** and a full C major chord rings out. The visual peak and auditory peak are simultaneous.
  Each isolated circle breathes with a gentle ¬±5px pulse to signal it's alive and waiting.
  Gain transitions via `setTargetAtTime(ѕД=50ms)` вАФ no pops. Faint C/E/G labels inside each
  circle for parents; invisible to kids in play mode.
  **"First kids prototype where the proximity between three distinct objects IS the music."**
  All 47 prior kids prototypes respond to single-object events (tap, drag, hold, draw).
  This is the first where the relationship between three moveable objects is the primary
  musical parameter. A child discovers color theory and music theory as the same interaction.
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.0 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 175 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/148-spatial-palette](/dream/148-spatial-palette)** вАФ Spatial Palette. `demoable`
  Full-screen dark canvas with colored synthesis voice dots. **Drag any dot** вАФ X axis is stereo
  pan (left=hard left, right=hard right), Y axis is pitch (top=C6, bottom=C2, one row per
  semitone). Drag the E4 dot down one row вЖТ chord label flips "C" вЖТ "Cm" instantly. **Scroll
  over a dot** вЖТ brighter/drier or darker/wetter (lowpass filter fc 200вЖТ8000 Hz + reverb send).
  **Double-click** вЖТ cycles waveform: sine вЖТ triangle вЖТ sawtooth вЖТ square. **Long-press** вЖТ
  removes voice with fade-out. **Click empty canvas** вЖТ adds a new voice at that pitch/pan
  (up to 8 total). Pre-placed C major triad on open. Shared 2.5s reverb IR. Composite
  waveform scope strip at the bottom. Chord name (24 major/minor templates) updates live.
  **"Position is music. Drag the E4 one row down to hear your major chord go minor."**
  First prototype where the musical relationship between voices is spatially visible: a minor third
  is literally one row closer than a major third on the semitone grid. The canvas makes interval
  theory tactile. Inspired by CHI 2026 6DoF gesture mixing research (spatial sculpting >
  sliders for musical expressivity).
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.87 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/148-spatial-palette/README.md`

  A large glowing circle pulses at a steady BPM. Each beat: the circle flashes a pentatonic color
  (C3вЖТE3вЖТG3вЖТA3вЖТC4 cycling), a quiet metronome pluck fires, and the note name briefly appears
  inside the circle. **Tap anywhere** вАФ sparks fly and a louder note plays. **On-beat taps**
  (within ¬±154ms at 70 BPM) produce 20 sparks + a secondary burst from the circle center; off-beat
  taps produce 9. No score, no fail state. A thin progress arc sweeps clockwise once per beat,
  giving an advance cue. BPM +/вИТ buttons (¬±10, 40вАУ120 BPM).
  **"First kids prototype about temporal attention вАФ tapping with a pulse, not just tapping."**
  46 prior kids prototypes reward any tap, regardless of timing. Beat Pulse rewards *when* you tap вАФ
  via a non-judgmental bigger-sparkle gradient. A 3yo enjoys the sparks; a 5yo chases the beat.
  Inspired by Karel's love of `98-kids-drum-circle` вЭ§пЄП (rhythm) and the "tempo and body" seed
  from Cycle 172 KIDS.md learnings.
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.81 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 173 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/146-eco-bloom](/dream/146-eco-bloom)** вАФ Eco Bloom. `demoable`
  A procedural rainforest grows before you. Three tree species (20¬∞/30¬∞/40¬∞ branch angle, depth 6/5/4)
  unfold simultaneously from seeds at the canvas bottom using recursive L-system branching. Each new
  branch segment plays a **Karplus-Strong pluck** (physical string model: delay-line feedback on seeded
  noise). Depth maps to pitch вАФ shallow trunks = low register, fine terminal twigs = high. All pitches
  C-major pentatonic; three simultaneous trees = three-voice polyphony that's always consonant.
  Leaf clusters accumulate at terminal branches, rotating gently in virtual wind. Background fades from
  near-black toward deep forest green as canopy density increases. **Rain toggle** (white noise through
  lowpass 1.1 kHz). **Bird calls toggle** (rapid 5-note arpeggio every 8 s, appears after ~18 s).
  **"First prototype where patient growth over time is the primary musical metaphor."** 142 prior
  prototypes were reactive or event-driven. Eco Bloom rewards watching. Tap canvas to plant more trees
  (max 6). Clear resets to fresh seeds. Directly inspired by Refik Anadol's DATALAND: Machine Dreams:
  Rainforest (opening June 20, 2026 вАФ 26 days away at build time). Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.27 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/146-eco-bloom/README.md`

  Six glowing colored dots in a row (C major pentatonic: violet=C3 вЖТ rose=E4). A bright white
  cursor sweeps left to right continuously. **Tap any dot to light it up** вАФ when the cursor
  passes a lit dot, that note plays. Tap again to turn it off. The result is a one-bar loop
  that plays forever at the current BPM. +/вИТ buttons adjust speed (40вАУ160 BPM in 16 BPM steps).
  Full-column tap zones (canvas height √Ч column width) give generous hit targets. All pentatonic
  combinations are consonant вАФ no wrong patterns.
  **"First kids prototype where the child constructs a musical pattern that then plays itself."**
  All 144 prior prototypes are reactive (tap вЖТ immediate note) or event-driven. This is the
  first where a child builds a looping composition by deliberate gesture, then observes it play.
  Different cognitive mode: composition over performance. Inspired by Karel's love of
  `98-kids-drum-circle` вЭ§пЄП (rhythm) and `111-kids-shape-loop` вЭ§пЄП (additive layering).

  Two-mode Stable Audio 3 prototype. **Mode A вАФ Write Journey**: pick one of 8 Resonance journey
  themes (Cosmic Homecoming, Earth Grounding, Inner Sanctuary, Ocean Breath, Snowflake, Ghost,
  Inner Fire, Mycelium Dream), edit the prompt, choose 2/4/6 min вЖТ SA3 generates up to 6 minutes
  of coherent ambient journey music. **Mode B вАФ Extend Your Playing**: record 5вАУ30 s of piano via
  mic вЖТ SA3 treats it as a causal prefix and generates a musical continuation. Amber waveform =
  your recording; blue waveform = AI continuation. Six-band bloom visualizer plays during output.
  Download button. **"The first prototype that breaks the 30-second generation ceiling."**
  All prior generation prototypes top out at 30вАУ90 s. SA3 Large (released May 20, 2026) makes
  6-minute coherent ambient music feasible in a single generation pass.
  Note: fal.ai SA3 endpoint may still be in partner-access rollout; error is surfaced clearly.

## Previous (Cycle 167 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/141-chord-canvas](/dream/141-chord-canvas)** вАФ Chord Canvas. `demoable`
  Play any chord into the mic вАФ it appears instantly as a large glowing name (C, FвЩѓm, Bdim)
  and paints a colored block on a scrolling 30-second timeline. Hue = root pitch class
  (C=violet, cycling chromatically); saturation = quality (major=vivid, minor=desaturated).
  A 12-bin chromagram at the bottom shows which pitch classes are active; active chord tones
  (root, third, fifth) highlight brighter with a colored underline.
  **First prototype to name musical structure.** 140 prior prototypes react to audio signal
  properties вАФ energy, spectrum, pitch. This one says "that's an FвЩѓ minor." Algorithm: chroma
  extraction (C2вАУAвЩѓ6), template matching against 24 triad templates, 5-frame stability filter.
  Demo mode: iiвАУVвАУI in C (Dm вЖТ G7 вЖТ C repeating) shows the timeline writing a chord chart.
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј mic optional ¬Ј 3.4 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 166 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/140-kids-string-bridge](/dream/140-kids-string-bridge)** вАФ String Bridge (kids). `demoable`
  Hold two fingers on the dark canvas вАФ a glowing string stretches between them and sings.
  **Closer fingers = shorter string = higher note** (same physical law as guitar/kalimba).
  Moving fingers >12 px "plucks" the string (triangle wave, 12ms attack, 450ms decay).
  Visual: standing-wave animation вАФ fundamental mode shape `sin(ѕА√Чt)√Чcos(2ѕА√Чphase)`, vibration
  rate proportional to pitch (0.8 Hz at C2, 5.5 Hz at C5). Color shifts violetвЖТamber with pitch.
  Single finger anchors at canvas center for solo thereminvox play вАФ pulling away lowers pitch,
  approaching raises it. 3-octave C-major pentatonic (C2вАУC5, 13 steps). Faint note-name label
  fades with the vibration. Amplitude floor 0.18 while held; faster fade on release.
  **"The gap between your fingers is the instrument."** First kids prototype where the
  *relationship* between two simultaneous touch points IS the musical parameter вАФ not position,
  duration, path, or physics of individual contacts.
  For kids 4+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.86 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 163 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/138-lmdm-echo](/dream/138-lmdm-echo)** вАФ Echo Chamber. `demoable`
  Record a piano phrase (up to 15 seconds). While you play, real-time harmonic analysis accumulates: 12-bin chroma vector вЖТ chord quality (major/minor/neutral), onset detection вЖТ BPM estimate, spectral centroid вЖТ register (low/mid/high). After you stop, the three features combine into an ACE-Step style prompt and generate a 30-second AI piano echo. Both tracks play back simultaneously вАФ your original panned left (вИТ35¬∞), the AI echo panned right (+35¬∞) вАФ through a shared six-band bloom visualizer. Waveform strips show both tracks with a live progress cursor.
  **"The echo responds to the musical meaning of the phrase, not its timbre."** Inspired by the "generative delay" concept from arXiv:2605.22717: AI-generated music as an expressive delay unit вАФ you play, the system processes harmonic meaning, a transformed version returns. Unlike `44-vocal-bgm` (audio-to-audio seeding), this uses text-to-audio where the prompt is derived from analysis. Unlike `33-aria-companion` (immediate Markov note response), this waits for a complete phrase then delivers a longer AI reply.
  Mic required ¬Ј FAL_KEY required ¬Ј ACE-Step $0.006/30s.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/138-lmdm-echo/README.md`

---

  Hold anywhere on a dark screen. A glowing orb of light appears at your touch вАФ the longer you hold, the brighter and wider it grows (core radius 28 вЖТ 92 px over 4 seconds; halo 22% вЖТ 50% opacity). Release: the glow "exhales" as a fading ring expands outward. Ring size and speed scale with hold duration вАФ long holds launch big fast rings, quick taps leave small slow ones. Five leftвЖТright color zones map to C-major pentatonic (violet=C3, rose=E3, amber=G3, emerald=A3, cyan=C4). Multi-touch: up to 5 simultaneous orbs = 5-note chord. Hint text visible when pond is empty.
  **"First kids prototype where hold-duration is the musical parameter."** All 35 prior kids prototypes respond to tap-down events (tap, drag, draw, tilt). This one rewards stillness. The two-phase breath structure вАФ tension while holding (glow grows), exhale on release (ring expands) вАФ is completely new to the zone. Contemplative, headphone-beautiful, suitable before sleep. Loved prototypes `100-kids-paint-song` and `104-kids-mirror-draw` (both involve sustained deliberate gesture) inspired the direction.
  For kids 3+ ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.17 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/137-kids-hold-glow/README.md`

---

## Previous (Cycle 161 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/136-kali-sustain](/dream/136-kali-sustain)** вАФ Kali Sustain. `demoable`
  A C2 root drone cycling through six just-intonation intervals: **Perfect Fifth (3вИґ2)** вЖТ **Perfect Fourth (4вИґ3)** вЖТ **Major Third (5вИґ4)** вЖТ **Minor Third (6вИґ5)** вЖТ **Harmonic Seventh (7вИґ4)** вЖТ **Whole Tone (9вИґ8)** вЖТ repeat. Each interval holds for 12 seconds then glides to the next over 12 seconds вАФ 144s total cycle. Four audio voices: root sine, sub-Hz LFO for beating, harmony sine tracking the current ratio, quiet octave warmth. **Mic mode** detects your sung pitch via autocorrelation and retunes the entire drone to your voice. Ratio clock visual shows all six intervals as nodes on a circle; a glowing dot sweeps clockwise through them; inner arc tracks hold vs. glide phase. Background hue blends between interval color palettes.
  **"The first prototype where the interval itself is the subject."** Previous harmonic prototypes (`105-pluck-field`, `107-ocean-presence`) use harmony as texture. Kali Sustain makes the ratio the foreground: you watch and hear exactly which interval is sounding and when it changes. The 7вИґ4 harmonic seventh sits outside 12-TET and always surprises. Inspired by Kali Malone's pipe organ just-intonation work.
  Demo mode (C2 root) ¬Ј Mic mode (autocorrelation) ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.95 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/136-kali-sustain/README.md`

---

## Previous (Cycle 159 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/134-anemone-av](/dream/134-anemone-av)** вАФ Anemone. `demoable`
  A bioluminescent sea anemone rendered in Three.js R3F. Eight cyan/violet tentacle arms radiate from a glowing central stalk, animated by sinusoidal LFOs modulated by audio: **sub-bass sways the entire organism**, low-mid spreads the tentacles outward, **high-mid flickers the glowing tips**, onsets pulse the whole body +9% for 80ms. Crown ring of 6 sky-blue spheres at the top of the stalk. Bloom post-processing from `@react-three/postprocessing`. Demo mode breathes with internal LFOs; mic mode makes it fully reactive to live sound. Dark background, no UI chrome during playback.
  **"The first intentionally organic 3D form in the sandbox."** Previous 3D prototypes (`130-tsl-particle-compute`, `106-beat-cut`, `75-houdini-particle-flock`) are mathematical/geometric. Anemone reads as alive вАФ it breathes even when silent. Direct response to Karel's love of `130-tsl-particle-compute`.
  Zero new deps (three@0.182, R3F, @react-three/postprocessing all pre-installed) ¬Ј WebGL required ¬Ј 3.99 kB.

Multi-touch: each finger drops its own ripple. The whole-screen is the instrument вАФ no buttons to find, no wrong taps. The physics of wave interference teaches itself through play: drop two stones far apart, watch the rings spread, wait for the moment they touch and sing together.
  **"Two rings meet вАФ and the pond makes a chord."** First kids prototype about wave interference. Related family: `90-kids-puddle-jumper` (single-ring splash); `109-kids-bounce-notes` (physics-makes-music autonomous).

## Previous (Cycle 157 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/132-shepard-tone](/dream/132-shepard-tone)** вАФ Shepard Tone. `demoable`
  Eight sine waves across eight octaves, each fading in at the bottom of the audible range and out at the top. All eight glide upward together. Result: **an auditory illusion of a tone that ascends forever without ever resolving**. Discovered by Roger Shepard (1964) вАФ the most famous auditory illusion in music. RATE slider (0.5вАУ30 BPM), Ascending/Descending toggle, three modes: Glide (smooth), Whole-tone (6 discrete steps per octave вАФ you hear the major whole-tone scale ascending), Semitone (12 steps вАФ slower, textbook-clear). Freeze button holds the current 8-oscillator chord. Phase ring (bottom-right) orbits once per octave traversal; center shows current note name (A, Bb, B, C...). Canvas: 8 glowing circles (A1=bottom, A8=top), brightness/size вИЭ bell-curve gain, hue cycles violetвЖТroseвЖТamber as each octave completes.
  **"A tone that climbs forever. The staircase has no top floor."** First psychoacoustics/auditory illusion prototype in the sandbox. Resonance angle: the Shepard tone proves perceptual ascent can be unbounded вАФ the listener travels far without going anywhere. That IS the journey thesis.
  Headphones recommended ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.6 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/132-shepard-tone/README.md`

## Previous (Cycle 156 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/131-kids-orbit](/dream/131-kids-orbit)** вАФ Orbit Garden (kids). `demoable`
  Five glowing planets orbit a central sun, each on its own ring. **Tap any ring** вЖТ a planet appears at your tap angle, plays its note (triangle + 2nd harmonic, reverb tail), and begins orbiting. Inner planets spin faster and sing higher (C4/3.5s period); outer planets are slow and low (C3/13s). Each planet plays its note again on every completed orbit вАФ place all five and listen to the polyrhythm build. Tap an occupied ring to teleport its planet to a new angle and retrigger its note. **"Clear"** button resets everything. No reading required; the five orbit rings are visible as faint dashed circles the moment you start.
  **"Five rings. Five notes. Each planet makes its own rhythm вАФ together they make music that's impossible to predict."** First kids prototype about polyrhythm-from-physics (joins `109-kids-bounce-notes` and `83-kids-tilt-rain` in the "physics autonomously makes music" family).

## Previous (Cycle 155 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/130-tsl-particle-compute](/dream/130-tsl-particle-compute)** вАФ Lorenz Attractor (WebGPU Compute). `demoable`
  50,000 particles simulated on the GPU via a WGSL compute shader, each following the Lorenz strange attractor equations. The attractor naturally collapses into its iconic butterfly shape within seconds. **Audio reactive**: microphone bass вЖТ ѕГ (chaos width), treble вЖТ ѕБ (energy), onsets вЖТ turbulence kick. Demo mode oscillates ѕГ and ѕБ with slow LFOs so it's always alive without a mic. Orbit with mouse or touch. Color gradient: slow particles = violet, mid = emerald, fast = cyan. Additive blending makes dense regions brighten. Falls back gracefully if WebGPU is unavailable.
  **"50,000 particles. One equation. Everything chaotic, nothing random."** First compute-shader prototype in the sandbox вАФ GPU physics at 60fps.
  WebGPU required ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј ~400 lines.

Ocean-floor background: seaweed sways with slow `sin()` drift, 10 micro-bubbles rise continuously, sandy floor gradient at the bottom.

Each phase is 30 s of ACE-Step output: **Opening** (sparse piano, vast reverb, 28 BPM) вЖТ **Descent** (minor arpeggios, cello drone, 55 BPM) вЖТ **Awakening** (ethereal pads, harmonic widening, 80 BPM) вЖТ **Peak** (full orchestral climax, 112 BPM) вЖТ **Integration** (bittersweet resolution, 70 BPM) вЖТ **Return** (single piano, near-silence, 25 BPM). All six phase prompts are editable before starting. Press **вЦґ Begin Journey** вЖТ phases generate and play sequentially (one at a time вАФ each generates then plays before moving to the next). Bloom visualizer responds to each phase's audio. Phase timeline at the bottom advances live. Stop anytime. Reset to re-run with edited prompts.
  **"What does the 6-phase arc sound like? Edit these 6 lines and find out."** First prototype that turns the abstract journey arc concept into heard, AI-generated music вАФ directly answers Karel's `5-arcs` question with audio.

The mapping: **hue вЖТ chord quality** (warm reds = C major, yellows = C7, greens = Cm, cyan = Cm7, violet = Cmaj7, magenta/purple = Cdim); **saturation вЖТ harmonic richness** (near-grey image = 1 pure sine; vivid image = 4 triangle-wave voices with slight detuning); **brightness вЖТ register + tempo** (dark = bass C2 at 35 BPM, bright = treble C5 at 120 BPM). The chord arpeggios continuously; a 6-band bloom ring animates to the synth output. **8 journey-palette swatches** (Cosmic, Earth, Sanctuary, Ocean, Snowflake, Ghost, Fire, Mycelium) for instant exploration вАФ no image needed. Chord name displayed in large monospace over the bloom.

## Previous (Cycle 145 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/123-landscape-resonance](/dream/123-landscape-resonance)** вАФ Landscape Resonance. `demoable`
  Audio-reactive 3D terrain fly-through rendered in raw WebGL + GLSL (no Three.js). A ray-marched heightfield derived from 5-octave FBM value noise. **Bass lifts mountains**: louder playing = towering peaks, the camera rises to match so they loom at the screen edges. **Treble adds surface roughness**: a second noise octave makes the terrain more jagged at high frequencies. **Onsets** trigger a 100ms blue-white lightning flash. **Fog** thickens with overall amplitude (quiet = clear far horizon, loud = atmospheric blur). Color gradient: deep violet valleys вЖТ emerald slopes вЖТ near-white peaks; the entire gradient shifts dynamically with bass scale. Forward fly-through speed: 0.38 units/sec (a full terrain feature takes ~47 seconds вАФ deliberately slow and meditative). Demo mode: three LFO oscillators (55/180/440 Hz) with amplitude-modulating sub-LFOs create a slow breathing terrain without mic. Live performance: bass-driven mountain peaks on a projector screen would be genuinely cinematic.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 3.63 kB ¬Ј WebGL required.

## Previous (Cycle 141 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/119-poem-fluid](/dream/119-poem-fluid)** вАФ Poem Fluid. `demoable`
  A WebGL Navier-Stokes fluid sim where the **turbulence state of the water drives a Markov chain text layer**. Start in **Still water** mode: the canvas is near-black, dark teal wisps drift slowly, and full Ghost-narrative sentences surface one at a time вАФ "The water remembers every sound that has passed through this place." Now **drag your finger** to stir. The turbulence score rises; sentences fragment into phrases, then single words, then a cascade of fragments at different positions. Release вАФ the fluid stills вАФ and sentences begin to surface again. Add mic: audio onsets spike turbulence (beat = shatters a sentence into words), bass drives pressure pulses that add swirling velocity to the water.
  Inspired by Memo Akten & Katie Hofstadter's *The Thinking Ocean* (Whitney Museum Artport, 2026): generative text that lives in the physical state of the fluid, not on top of it.
  Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 6.5 kB.
