## Previous (Cycle 140 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/118-kids-mirror-melody](/dream/118-kids-mirror-melody)** вАФ Mirror Melody (kids). `demoable`
  A split canvas: rose on the left, cyan on the right. **Draw on either half** вАФ glowing dots trail your finger and play a pentatonic note in real time (Y=pitch, top=high). The **mirror path appears instantly** on the opposite half in the complementary color, playing the same note panned to the other ear. Two-voice stereo duet from a single gesture. Paths accumulate and fade over 7 seconds; a soft ambient CвАУGвАУC pad fills any silence. Multi-touch: two fingers create two independent mirror pairs simultaneously.
  **"Left hand / right hand вАФ draw both at once."** The prototype is its own music theory lesson: holding a finger high on both sides creates a two-voice unison; drawing one finger high and one low creates a two-voice interval. A 4yo discovers this in under 30 seconds without any instruction.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.26 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 139 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/117-data-cosm](/dream/117-data-cosm)** вАФ DATA-COSM. `demoable`
  Ryoji Ikeda aesthetic brought to the browser. A full-canvas scrolling matrix of **synthetic particle physics events** in CERN CMS format (`[ќЉ+] pt=  48.3 eta= -1.270 phi=  2.950 m=0.1060 q=+1`) rendered in monospace on pure black. Every new event: characters **scatter from random offsets then snap into place** (300ms), a sine pulse fires at the current scale's tone frequency, trail particles arc upward. A continuous **sub-bass 38Hz drone** underlies вАФ felt not heard.
  Three **temporal scales** auto-advance every 40s with a white flash + scatter-all transition:
  - **QUANTUM** вАФ 8 events/s, 4kHz tones, 10px font, 90px/s scroll: dense flickering number matrix
  - **BIOLOGICAL** вАФ 1 event/s, 440Hz tones, slower cadence: graceful, measured
  - **COSMIC** вАФ 1 event/10s, 110Hz sub-bass tone, 20px font, centered on black: a single event worth contemplating
  "All of nature's data is the same material." The three scales comment on each other вАФ the identical data format means completely different things at different temporal densities.
  Tap to activate audio. Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.38 kB.

After 10 seconds the flower **seeds itself**: petals scatter as sparkles and a new bud sprouts 30вАУ62px away, inheriting the pitch ¬±1 note. **Tap any flower to burst it** вАФ sparkle explosion + pop note. Up to 12 flowers coexist; the garden self-organizes over time toward harmonic clusters as notes drift ¬±1 each generation.

  **"The most contemplative kids prototype yet вАФ designed for quiet play before sleep."** No tap targets. No fail state. No goal. Hold вЖТ bloom вЖТ watch the garden grow itself. Ambient C3+E3+G3 pad so the screen is never silent even before the first flower.

  Drag your finger anywhere вАФ a glowing wand follows it, leaving a rainbow color trail. Y position = pitch (pentatonic, top=high, bottom=low). Drag speed = note rate: slow sweep вЖТ long sustained tones; fast sweep вЖТ rapid arpeggios. Quick tap вЖТ drum hit (noise burst). Choose from 4 orchestras before starting: **Playground** рЯО™ (bright triangle waves, amber), **Space** рЯЪА (slow-attack sine waves, violet), **Forest** рЯМ≤ (warm triangle, emerald), **Ocean** рЯМК (flowing sine with 3-note drone, cyan). Ambient drone chord for that orchestra plays quietly always вАФ canvas never goes silent. Demo mode auto-conducts a Lissajous figure until first touch (wand already moving = no cold start).
  **"Your finger is the conductor's baton."** First kids prototype where a single continuous gesture controls both pitch AND rhythm simultaneously. No buttons, no tap targets вАФ the whole screen is the instrument.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.84 kB.

---

## Previous (Cycle 133 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/112-bio-echo](/dream/112-bio-echo)** вАФ Bio Echo. `demoable`
  Play piano into the mic вАФ watch a forest grow, layer by layer, in real time. Five frequency strata map to five ecological layers: **sub-bass grows root tendrils** (deep violet lines crawling upward with Brownian drift); **bass builds the trunk** (amber pillar that only grows, never shrinks вАФ every bass-heavy passage is permanently recorded in its height); **mid blooms the canopy** (emerald leaf-ellipses accumulating at 34вАУ61% canvas height); **onsets send birds** (each attack fires a white bezier wing-arc into the sky вАФ play 60 attacks and the sky fills with birds); **treble fills the sky** (tiny white star-dots at top 14%).
  The canvas never clears вАФ by the end of a piece, a complete forest ecosystem has grown that encodes the entire musical session. Download as PNG.
  **"Every frequency band is a layer of the forest вАФ sub-bass digs the roots, treble lights the sky."** Inspired by Refik Anadol's DATALAND (opens June 20, 2026, LA). Trunk gradient from accumulation (no gradient code вАФ the canvas's own physics creates it).
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј mic optional (demo mode) ¬Ј 3.6 kB.

Next: **Cycle 134 вЖТ `kids-conductor-wand`** or `kids-weather-music`. **Cycle 135 вЖТ `live-harmonize`**.

## Previous (Cycle 132 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/111-kids-shape-loop](/dream/111-kids-shape-loop)** вАФ Shape Loop (kids). `demoable`
  Draw any closed shape with your finger вАФ when it closes, a glowing traversal dot orbits the perimeter and triggers a pentatonic note at each of the evenly-spaced trigger points (small colored dots on the shape). **Y position = pitch**: draw a tall shape and hear high notes; draw a wide flat shape and hear mid-register loops; draw a circle for a near-constant-pitch drone. Draw multiple shapes вАФ each loops independently, creating polyphonic layers. Tap any existing shape to erase it. Auto-close: a dashed ring near the start point shows where to return to вАФ when your finger enters it, the shape closes and starts playing immediately.
  **"Your drawing loops as a melody forever."** First kids prototype about additive compositional layering вАФ the child doesn't react to something, they construct a composition by drawing.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.84 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 131 вАФ adult build)

- **[/dream/110-webcam-compose](/dream/110-webcam-compose)** вАФ Webcam Compose. `demoable`
  Point your camera at anything вАФ the image becomes a chord. Dominant hue вЖТ chord quality (warm reds=major, cool blues=minor, violets=diminished, greens=suspended, pinks=augmented). Brightness вЖТ register (dark=C2 bass, bright=C4 treble). Saturation вЖТ harmonic richness (1вАУ3 triangle-wave voices per chord tone). Frame delta вЖТ arpeggio vs pad. Split view: left = camera feed with colored quadrant zone borders, right = 6-band bloom ring from the synthesis AnalyserNode (shows chord harmonics). Demo mode cycles all 5 chord qualities without camera permission.
  **"Point at a plant, a painting, a window. Each one plays a different chord."** First prototype where musical output is fully determined by where you look. Inspired by LUMIA (arxiv 2512.17228, Dec 2025). Zero API ¬Ј Zero ML ¬Ј webcam optional ¬Ј 4.66 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 130 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/109-kids-bounce-notes](/dream/109-kids-bounce-notes)** вАФ Bounce Notes (kids). `demoable`
  A glowing ball bounces around the canvas with gravity and elastic reflections. Each wall plays a different pentatonic note: **bottom=C3** (deep, satisfying), **top=A4** (bright, tingly), **left=G3** and **right=E4** (mid). Ball lights up on impact (flash glow), dims between bounces. **Tap anywhere to spawn a new ball** at that position вАФ up to 5 balls playing simultaneously. More balls = richer self-playing music. The child sets physics in motion; physics makes the music.
  **"A ball bounces. The wall sings back."** First kids prototype where the music is autonomous вАФ the child doesn't need to keep tapping to keep the sound going. Inspired by the Bouncy / Sound Drop paradigm.

Tap any bar to pluck it with Karplus-Strong string synthesis вАФ noise burst into tuned ring-buffer feedback loop, decay 1.5вАУ4s. **Taller bars ring lower; shorter bars ring higher** вАФ the physical law of string instruments, no words needed. Drag across for a glissando; multi-touch plucks multiple strings simultaneously. Demo auto-arpeggios silently until first touch. Ambient C-E-G pad. Start screen shows a mini bar-height preview so the instrument's shape is visible before play. Eight C-major pentatonic notes (C3вАУA4), all combinations consonant.

## Previous (Cycle 125 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/106-beat-cut](/dream/106-beat-cut)** вАФ Beat Cut. `demoable`
  6,000 particles orbit in 6 journey-themed species (Cosmic Homecoming = violet, Earth Grounding = emerald, Ocean Breath = cyan, Snowflake = ice-blue, Inner Sanctuary = amber, Ghost = purple). Six camera presets вАФ one per journey вАФ hard-cut on every audio onset. No lerp, no tween: a hard snap, like a live edit suite firing on the beat. Spring-attractor physics (O(N)) keeps the cloud alive between cuts. Bloom post-processing. Demo mode: synthetic onset timer 700вАУ1500ms. Mic mode: spectral flux fires camera cuts on attack transients.
  **"The music cuts the camera. TouchDesigner's camSequencer, ported to the browser."** First prototype where the audio event IS the edit decision, and Karel's 6 published journey themes all coexist in one scene.
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј WebGL (Three.js, already installed).

A subtle pitch-gradient strip at the bottom (violetвЖТpink) shows the note mapping without text. No reading required, no fail state. A child who draws leftвЖТright hears an ascending scale; rightвЖТleft hears a descent; a squiggle hears a wandering tune.

## Previous (Cycle 116 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/99-kids-panning-safari](/dream/99-kids-panning-safari)** вАФ Panning Safari (kids). `demoable`
  Five animals вАФ duck рЯ¶Ж, frog рЯРЄ, elephant рЯРШ, cat рЯР±, parrot рЯ¶Ь вАФ drift left and right across a night savanna at their own speeds. Each animal is panned to its current X position via `StereoPannerNode`: far left = left ear, far right = right ear, center = center. Tap any animal to trigger its synthesized call at the animal's current pan position. Animals also call automatically every 3вАУ7 seconds as they wander. Distinct synthesized voices: duck = bandpass noise quack; frog = AM sine ribbit (140 Hz carrier modulated at 18 Hz); elephant = low sawtooth rumble through 280 Hz lowpass; cat = sine glide 580вЖТ340 Hz; parrot = chirp 1400вЖТ1900вЖТ850 Hz. Dashed drop-line from each animal to a pan ruler strip at the bottom; colored dot on ruler shows exact pan position. Background: night sky, ground strip, 38 static stars. Soft ambient pad from first tap. Hit radius 62 px for 4yo accuracy.
  **"First kids prototype about spatial audio вАФ tap the elephant on the left, its rumble fills your left ear."** Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.61 kB. Best with headphones.

## Previous (Cycle 115 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/81-cassette-speed](/dream/81-cassette-speed)** вАФ CassetteAI vs ACE-Step Speed Race. `demoable`
  Side-by-side speed and quality comparison of two FAL music-generation backends. Pick one of five presets (Forest Dawn, Stone Chamber, Cosmic Drift, Jazz Sketch, Ocean Breath) or type freeform tags, then hit **Generate Both** вАФ both backends start simultaneously. Left panel (violet) runs CassetteAI (`cassetteai/music-generator`, distilled model, ~2s); right panel (cyan) runs ACE-Step (`fal-ai/ace-step`, full diffusion, ~20вАУ40s). Each panel shows a live millisecond timer, then a waveform strip on completion, then a вЦґ Play button. Playback feeds a six-band bloom visualizer (violetвЖТcyanвЖТgreenвЖТyellowвЖТorangeвЖТmagenta). When both complete a speed summary line appears: "Cassette: X.Xs ¬Ј ACE-Step: Y.Ys ¬Ј X√Ч faster."
  **"Same prompt. Both start at once. Now you can hear whether the 10√Ч speed gap costs anything you'd notice."** Direct empirical tool for Karel to decide whether to swap `6-compose`'s ACE-Step backend for faster iteration loops.
  FAL_KEY required ¬Ј 2 API calls / generation ¬Ј waveform + bloom visualizer.

## Previous (Cycle 114 вАФ kids build)

- **[/dream/98-kids-drum-circle](/dream/98-kids-drum-circle)** вАФ Drum Circle (kids). `demoable`
  Six large colored percussion pads in a 3√Ч2 grid вАФ red (kick), orange (snare), yellow (hihat), teal (tom), blue (clap), purple (shaker). Tap any pad to play its synthesized drum sound: kick is a sine sweep 150вЖТ40 Hz; snare is bandpass noise + short 200 Hz sine body; hihat is highpass noise above 7 kHz; tom is a slower sine sweep 110вЖТ55 Hz; clap is a double bandpass noise burst (0 ms + 22 ms apart вАФ the gap between bursts is the perceptual cue for "clap"); shaker is highpass noise above 5.5 kHz. Background canvas draws expanding colored rings from each tap point. CSS scale (0.88) + bright glow on press. Quiet C/E/G ambient pad from first tap. Multi-touch: every finger gets its own ring. Zero permissions, zero API, zero deps. Min pad size 26vmin (вЙ•80px).
  **"Six colors, six sounds вАФ the first kids prototype about rhythm rather than pitch."** All 10 previous kids prototypes use C-major pentatonic melodic notes. This is the first pure percussion prototype вАФ tap a rhythm, layer sounds, no music theory needed.
  Zero permissions ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 2.12 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 113 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/80-room-acoustic](/dream/80-room-acoustic)** вАФ Room Acoustic. `demoable`
  Simulate a physical room and hear how it changes your piano. Draw a rectangular room (1.5вАУ60m wide, up to 80m deep), pick wall/floor materials (Stone ќ±=0.03 вЖТ Carpet ќ±=0.40), and press **вЦґ play chord** вАФ a C-major chord sounds in that space via a Web Audio `ConvolverNode` loaded with the computed impulse response. The image-source method computes up to 3rd-order reflections; RT60 (Sabine estimate) updates live and color-codes from studio-dry to cathedral-vast. Drag the amber вЩ™ source and violet рЯСВ listener dots to reposition; IR rebuilds automatically. 9 presets: Closet ¬Ј Bedroom ¬Ј Studio ¬Ј Hall ¬Ј Concert Hall ¬Ј Cathedral ¬Ј Cave ¬Ј Stone Chamber ¬Ј Forest Clearing.
  **"Move a wall. Hear the room change."** The Stone Chamber preset sounds ringy and metallic (RT60 вЙИ 2.5s, stone everywhere); the Cathedral is vast and blurred (RT60 вЙИ 3.8s); the Closet is almost silent-dry (RT60 вЙИ 0.08s, carpet). First prototype about acoustic space physics вАФ not signal analysis, not synthesis, but the physics of a room.
  Zero API ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј 4.98 kB.

## Previous (Cycle 111 вАФ build)

Audio-reactive: bass drives bloom, treble adds edge shimmer, onsets inject color pulses. Demo mode or live mic.

  Full-screen canvas instrument for two simultaneous players. Each touch contact gets a glowing colored orb вАФ first finger = violet, second = rose. Y-position maps to a pentatonic pitch (C3вАУC5); slide up = higher note, slide down = lower. The pentatonic constraint guarantees any two simultaneous notes sound beautiful together вАФ no wrong combinations possible. Smooth pitch glide (fretless feel). When both voices are active, an animated dashed gradient line connects them visually. Sparkle particle trail on movement. Idle hint: two pulsing colored dots show where to put fingers. Pointer capture keeps tracking even at screen edges.
  **"Two fingers, two voices вАФ parent and child each hold a note and slide together. Always in harmony."**
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј Zero permissions ¬Ј 2.66 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/93-kids-share-screen/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 101 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/85-spectrogram-paint](/dream/85-spectrogram-paint)** вАФ Spectrogram Paint. `demoable`
  Your sound crystallizes into a living painting. FFT data scrolls as a waterfall (time leftвЖТright, pitch bottomвЖТtop, log scale 20 HzвАУ8 kHz) with a Ryoji Ikeda-style hot colormap: silence = black, dim = violet/cyan, peak = white. The spectrogram feeds a **Canvas2D ping-pong feedback loop** вАФ each frame the display decays at 98.4%, zooms 1.002√Ч, and drifts slightly, then the fresh spectrogram is injected additively. Notes leave trails that bloom outward and slowly evaporate. Demo mode animates 11 C-major scale frequencies with LFO envelopes. Mic mode maps your real playing directly to the display.
  **"Play a chord: three bright white lines crystallize, bloom outward, then fade like breath on glass."**
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј 2.76 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/85-spectrogram-paint/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 97 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/87-piano-transcript](/dream/87-piano-transcript)** вАФ Piano Transcript. `demoable`
  Play piano into the mic вАФ the prototype writes while you play. YIN pitch detection (~35 lines,
  zero deps) converts each note to a filled rectangle on a scrolling Canvas2D piano roll. X axis = time
  (20 s visible window, scrolls leftward), Y axis = MIDI pitch (C2вАУC7). Color gradient: warm amber at the
  low end, Resonance violet in the middle registers, cool cyan at the top.
  Phrases (вЙ•2 s of silence between groups) get a subtle violet bracket around them.
  "Save PNG" exports the full session to a timestamped 1920√ЧN image at 64 px/second.
  YIN runs every 3rd RAF frame (~20 Hz); pitch median-smoothed over 5 readings to suppress octave errors.
  **"This prototype writes while you play вАФ a permanent record of your session."**

## Previous newest (Cycle 94 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/79-fm-explorer](/dream/79-fm-explorer)** вАФ FM Explorer. `demoable`
  2-operator FM synthesis: a modulator oscillator drives the carrier's frequency AudioParam.
  Two sliders control the entire DX7 timbre space вАФ C:M ratio (which harmonic series) and ќ≤
  modulation index (how rich/noisy). The right panel shows the **live sideband spectrum** as
  Bessel function coefficients J_n(ќ≤): you see exactly why DX Piano at ќ≤=2.5 has almost no
  carrier energy (JвВА(2.5) вЙИ 0.05) and all the sound lives in JвВБ and JвВВ.
  Six presets: DX Piano ¬Ј Bell ¬Ј Reed ¬Ј FM Bass ¬Ј Metallic ¬Ј Glass Harmonica.
  ADSR envelope. Space bar / pointer hold = play note.
  Demo mode: slow LFO breathes ќ≤ so the spectrum animates without mic. Mic mode: bass вЖТ ќ≤,
  onset вЖТ retrigger envelope вАФ loud playing gets grittier, attacks reshape the timbre.
  **"78 prototypes, none had FM synthesis until now."**
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј 5.29 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/79-fm-explorer/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 93 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/78-node-synth](/dream/78-node-synth)** вАФ Node Synth. `demoable`
  The Web Audio API as a visual patch bay. Oscillators, gain stages, filters (lowpass/highpass/bandpass/
  notch/peaking), and delay effects appear as draggable node cards. Draw bezier wire connections between
  output and input ports вАФ audio flows in real time. The starter patch (Oscillator вЖТ Gain вЖТ Speakers)
  plays immediately; add a Filter between them and sweep its frequency to hear the lowpass open up.
  Delay node has an internal feedback loop so echo trails build with each wire reconnect.
  Try: Oscillator вЖТ Filter вЖТ Gain вЖТ Speakers + Oscillator вЖТ Delay вЖТ Gain (wet blend with echo).
  **"The synthesizer you see is the synthesizer you hear."**
  Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј 4.67 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/78-node-synth/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 91 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/74-touchdesigner-feedback](/dream/74-touchdesigner-feedback)** вАФ TD Feedback. `demoable`
  TouchDesigner's TOP feedback loop, ported to WebGPU. Two ping-pong render textures loop on
  themselves each frame вАФ the output of frame N becomes the input of frame N+1, transformed
  by a slight rotation + zoom + hue shift + brightness decay. Audio (bass/mid/treble/onset) injects
  a colored bloom layer each frame; the feedback amplifies and spirals it into complex self-similar
  patterns within 3вАУ4 seconds. Four sliders: ROTATION (¬±15вА∞ rad/frame), ZOOM (0.992вАУ1.012√Ч),
  HUE DRIFT, DECAY. вЖЇ RESET clears to black. Demo mode works without mic permissions.
  WebGPU required ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј 5.2 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/74-touchdesigner-feedback/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 89 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/71-shader-evolve](/dream/71-shader-evolve)** вАФ Shader Evolve. `demoable`
  Natural selection of audio-reactive WGSL shaders. Four mutated variants run simultaneously in a 2√Ч2
  WebGPU grid. Click any cell to promote it to a full-res 60fps focus view. Click **вЖї EVOLVE** to breed
  four new mutations from the selected variant. **вШЕ SAVE** stores the current selection to a persistent
  gallery (up to 6 slots, localStorage) вАФ click a tile to restart evolution from a saved ancestor.
  **вЬО EDIT** opens the raw WGSL for manual refinement. Each mutation randomly multiplies 3вАУ5 of 16
  named shader parameters by a factor in [0.4, 2.5] вАФ always valid WGSL, often dramatically different.
  `ringFreq` mutated to 45+ creates moir√©-like interference; `sat` near 0 produces monochrome shaders
  with their own aesthetic. The selection UI is purely visual: look at four things, pick the one that
  "feels right," breed. Audio uniforms: `uBass`, `uMid`, `uTreble`, `uOnset`, `uTime`, `uBPM` вАФ demo
  mode works without mic permissions. WebGPU required. Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј 5.82 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/71-shader-evolve/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 88 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/70-pitch-algo-compare](/dream/70-pitch-algo-compare)** вАФ Pitch Compare. `demoable`
  Three pitch detection algorithms running simultaneously on every audio frame вАФ see where they
  agree and where they diverge. **Orange** = Autocorrelation (ACF peak). **Blue** = YIN (cumulative
  mean normalized difference, ~15% fewer octave errors). **Green** = HPS (harmonic product spectrum,
  4 harmonics вАФ great for piano and strings). A **gold dashed cursor** appears when вЙ•2 algorithms
  agree within 1.5 semitones; a faint piano tone plays on each new consensus note.
  Demo uses sawtooth oscillators cycling through 8 pitches вАФ sawtooth has all harmonics so HPS works
  well and the comparison is immediately meaningful. Mic mode: play single notes to see consensus,
  play low bass or chords to watch algorithms diverge. Piano roll C2вАУC7, confidence bars per algorithm.
  **"Which algorithm is right? Sometimes all of them. Sometimes none."**
  First prototype making pitch detection internals visible and learnable. Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј 4.67 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/70-pitch-algo-compare/README.md`

---

  Three coins cast six times вЖТ one of 64 hexagrams вЖТ music shaped by archetypal qualities.

  I-Ching tradition to audio: Hexagram 1 (The Creative) plays bright major arpeggios at 80 BPM

  First prototype connecting music to a divination tradition. High surprise factor.

## Previous newest (Cycle 85 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/68-wgsl-synth](/dream/68-wgsl-synth)** вАФ WGSL Synth. `demoable`
  Write a WebGPU shader that responds to your playing вАФ live, in the browser.
  Split-screen: left = WGSL fragment shader (editable textarea); right = fullscreen WebGPU canvas.
  Six pre-wired audio uniforms: **uBass, uMid, uTreble, uOnset, uTime, uBPM** (+ uResX/uResY) вАФ fed
  every frame from the AnalyserNode or demo LFOs. Edit any line; shader recompiles 400ms later.
  Errors shown with line numbers; the last valid pipeline keeps running вАФ no black frames.
  Default shader: pulsing radial rings + grid shimmer + onset flash, HSV color cycle.
  Demo: LFO oscillators animate the shader without mic permissions. Mic: play piano вАФ bass expands
  the rings, chords shimmer the grid, a sharp attack flashes white.
  **The lowest-level tool in the sandbox**: write raw WGSL; audio is the parameter.
  Natural partner to `claude-shader` (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY pending) which writes the WGSL for you.
  WebGPU required ¬Ј Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј ~3.8 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/68-wgsl-synth/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 84 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/67-structure-viz](/dream/67-structure-viz)** вАФ Structure Viz. `demoable`
  Your music as a map of itself. Every 1.5 seconds: capture FFT вЖТ 32 log-spaced feature bins вЖТ
  normalized vector. Compute the N√ЧN **self-similarity matrix** (cosine similarity). Display as a
  heatmap: dark purple = dissimilar, bright white = same material. The diagonal is always white.
  Checkerboard kernel novelty function detects section boundaries; greedy similarity clustering
  assigns labels A / B / AвА≤ / C вАФ matching letter means recurring material.
  **Demo (вЦґ ABA)**: three oscillator phases (C3 chord вЖТ A4 chord вЖТ C3 returns). By bar 22 (вЙИ33s)
  you see the classic "three bright blocks along the diagonal" with two bright off-diagonal corners
  confirming that A = AвА≤. The timeline strip below shows `A | B | AвА≤`.
  **Mic mode**: play any music вАФ verse-chorus-verse, theme-variation-return, anything with
  recurring sections will produce off-diagonal bright squares.
  **First prototype that shows structure rather than content** вАФ not what frequencies are present,
  but how sections relate. 66 prior prototypes visualize audio signal; this one visualizes form.
  Zero deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј 3.81 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/67-structure-viz/README.md`

---

- **[/dream/66-chatterbox-ghost](/dream/66-chatterbox-ghost)** вАФ Chatterbox Ghost. `demoable`
  Record 5вАУ10 seconds of any voice вЖТ Chatterbox Turbo renders all six Ghost narrative scenes in
  that cloned voice, with physical action tags embedded in the text: `[sigh]`, `[gasp]`, `[slowly]`,

  4 new prototype ideas added to IDEAS.md. Highlights:

  - **Chatterbox Turbo (¬І137)** вАФ 5-second voice cloning + paralinguistic tags `[sigh]`, `[gasp]`.
    FAL_KEY in use. $0.025/1000 chars вАФ cheapest TTS in the sandbox. First model that can clone
    a specific voice. Build next cycle: `chatterbox-ghost` вАФ hear the Ghost narrations in Karel's
    own voice (or any 5s reference clip).

  - **ImprovNet (¬І138, arxiv 2502.04522)** вАФ play a seed phrase вЖТ AI generates a full 32-bar
    structured improvisation with controllable style transfer (jazz/classical/blues degree slider).
    First "complete the composition" AI in the queue. No API yet; monitor.

  - **Pianist Transformer (¬І139, arxiv 2512.02652)** вАФ 135M-param model, human-level expressive
    piano rendering from flat MIDI. Apache 2.0. HuggingFace demo. Proxy-callable. вЖТ `expressive-render`.

  - **Self-similarity matrix (¬І143)** вАФ zero-dep browser section detection. FFT вЖТ N√ЧN cosine
    similarity colormap вЖТ section boundary lines. First prototype that shows musical STRUCTURE
    (does the chorus come back?) not signal content. вЖТ `structure-viz`. Buildable zero deps.

  - **D3PIA (¬І140), PianoFlow (¬І141), NCLMCTT (¬І142)** вАФ three new research-direction prototypes
    queued pending API availability.

  **Open questions for Karel**:
  - NEW: Bundle a 5s Ghost voice reference for `chatterbox-ghost`? Could be Karel's own voice.
  - GEMINI_API_KEY вЖТ `lyria-jam`, `lyria-ghost`, `binaural-lyria`
  - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY вЖТ `claude-shader`

## Previous newest (Cycle 81 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/65-dialogue-score](/dream/65-dialogue-score)** вАФ Dialogue Score. `demoable`
  Contour-mirroring AI piano dialogue. Play a phrase (8+ notes + 2s silence); the system detects
  its melodic shape вАФ ascending вЖЧ, descending вЖШ, arch вИІ, valley вИ®, or neutral вАФ then generates
  Aria's response with the **same contour**. Markov chain biases note choices from your playing
  history; contour constraint enforces pitch direction at each step. Both work together.
  Ghost notes (dashed blue) appear before Aria plays вАФ full anticipation preview from `39-anticipate`.
  Header shows: `your phrase вЖЧ ascending вЖТ aria mirrors вЖТ aria responds вЖЧ ascending`.
  Demo: C major scale ascending вЖТ Aria responds ascending. First prototype where Aria's response
  has musical logic, not just statistical probability. "The AI mirrors your musical thought."
  Inspired by "Dialogue in Resonance" (arxiv 2505.16259, 2026). Zero deps ¬Ј Zero API ¬Ј 5.29 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/65-dialogue-score/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 80 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/64-eleven-dialogue](/dream/64-eleven-dialogue)** вАФ Eleven Dialogue. `demoable`
  The Ghost is no longer alone. Six Ghost scenes as two-character dramatic exchanges вАФ Ghost + Visitor вАФ
  voiced by ElevenLabs V3 with inline emotional tags embedded per phrase: `[slowly, reverently]`, `[pauses]`,
  `[whispers]`, `[awed]`, `[infinite calm]`, `[long pause]`. Ghost uses Adam voice (deep, measured);
  Visitor uses Alice voice (lighter, questioning). Three API calls per scene, played sequentially with
  550ms silence between turns.
  Canvas: two glowing orbs separated by a vertical divider вАФ Ghost amber-warm left, Visitor cool-blue right.
  Active speaker's orb pulses with live amplitude data; an expanding ring marks speaking. All six scenes
  pre-scripted with editable textareas; V3 tag hints in the UI.
  Stone Chamber: *"[slowly] The resonance here [pauses] is ancient."* ¬Ј *"[nervous, awed] I didn't know it would feel this alive."* ¬Ј *"[whispers] Everything that ever sounded here вАФ still does."*
  "The Ghost is no longer alone." ElevenLabs V3 via FAL_KEY ¬Ј ~$0.02/scene ¬Ј 4.09 kB.
  вЪ† Endpoint `fal-ai/elevenlabs/tts/eleven-v3` is a naming-convention best-guess; paste error text if wrong.

## Previous newest (Cycle 79 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/63-synesthetic-sketch](/dream/63-synesthetic-sketch)** вАФ Synesthetic Sketch. `demoable`
  Six audio features вЖТ six visual dimensions on one accumulated canvas.
  **Spectral centroid** вЖТ hue (violet=bass, red=treble). **Bandwidth** вЖТ shape type: circle (pure
  tone), hexagon (mid spread), 7-pointed star (wideband). **Harmonic peaks** вЖТ inner concentric ring
  count (0вАУ4). **Amplitude** вЖТ object scale. **Rhythm regularity** вЖТ scatter radius (regular playing =
  tight glowing cluster at center; improvised/irregular = wide scattered field). **Onset events** вЖТ
  radial spark burst at random canvas position.
  Objects accumulate additively with slow 0.4%/frame decay. Canvas persists across modes. Download as PNG.
  Demo: 6 incommensurable LFOs cycle through all shape types automatically. Mic: play a pure note
  вЖТ circles; chord вЖТ multi-ringed star; tap steady вЖТ center cluster; improvise вЖТ scattered field.
  "Not just what color your music is вАФ what shape it is." Inspired by musicolors (RESEARCH.md ¬І131).
  Zero deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј 4.26 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/63-synesthetic-sketch/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 77 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/62-collage-compose](/dream/62-collage-compose)** вАФ Collage Compose. `demoable`
  Three inputs вЖТ one composition. Pick a **Ghost scene** (Stone Chamber, Root Portal, Underground Pool,
  Tiny Planet, Forest Dawn, Cosmic Ascension), pick a **mood word** (meditative / dreaming / ascending /
  melancholic / ethereal / grounded / tense / vast), and optionally **hum a melody** into the mic
  (up to 15s). The live "ACE-STEP PROMPT" panel shows exactly how the three inputs combine into tags.
  Click **Compose вЖТ** вЖТ ACE-Step generates a 30s track.
  - **With hum**: `audio-to-audio` вАФ the model literally hears your melody and builds around it.
  - **Without hum**: `text-to-audio` вАФ scene + mood alone still constrain more than a single description.
  Waveform strip: amber (your hum) | blue (generated). Bloom visualizer during playback.
  Footer shows which endpoint was used вАФ it switches live when you record.
  Inspired by Mozualization (CHI 2025): multimodal music gen from image + audio + keyword.
  FAL_KEY ¬Ј $0.006/track ¬Ј 4.65 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/62-collage-compose/README.md`

  XML emotion tags (`<reverent>`, `<whispers>`, `<sad>`, `<fearful>`, etc.).

  A / B / C wins, All good, Try again. Tallies stored per scene in localStorage.
  Pre-loaded C tags chosen to match the Ghost emotional arc: `<reverent>resonance</reverent>`,
  `<fearful>stirs</fearful>`, `<sad>remembers</sad>`, `<happy>together</happy>`. Edit and
  experiment вАФ the textarea is fully live.
  Key question: does phrase-level tag control (Orpheus) produce more interesting Ghost narration
  than global style direction (Gemini)? The vote reveals it.
  Gemini TTS ¬Ј Orpheus TTS ¬Ј FAL_KEY ¬Ј ~$0.01вАУ0.02/row ¬Ј 4.7 kB.

## Previous newest (Cycle 75 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/60-music-palette](/dream/60-music-palette)** вАФ Music Palette. `demoable`
  Your audio becomes a 5-color palette. Bass energy вЖТ lightness (28вАУ72%); treble-to-total
  ratio вЖТ hue anchor (250¬∞=sad/blue вЖТ 50¬∞=happy/warm yellow); spectral spread вЖТ saturation.
  Five swatches at ¬±30¬∞ and ¬±60¬∞ hue offsets breathe via a slow EMA (~1.5s time constant).
  Below the swatches: the `1-live` bloom ring showing the raw audio energy. Download the
  current palette as a labeled SVG вАФ each download is a color snapshot of that musical moment.
  Demo: 6 incommensurable LFOs (never exactly repeating) drift the palette from warm to cool.
  "Your music as a color story." Zero deps ¬Ј zero API ¬Ј 4.15 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/60-music-palette/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 71 вАФ build)

- **[/dream/57-sound-to-image](/dream/57-sound-to-image)** вАФ Sound-to-Image. `demoable`
  10 seconds of audio вЖТ acoustic fingerprint вЖТ Flux Schnell scene image of what your music looks like.
  Click **вЦґ Demo** for an immediate result (C major chord вЖТ sea cave or stone chamber). Click **рЯО§ Start mic**
  and play anything for 10 seconds. The prototype extracts: RMS energy, spectral centroid, zero-crossing rate,
  12-bin chroma (chord quality + root note), and autocorrelation pitch. Averages across ~100 frames вЖТ natural-language
  description ("soft, smooth tonal, warm bass-dominant music вАФ C major, hopeful, central pitch 294 Hz") вЖТ one of 6
  scene archetypes вЖТ `fal-ai/flux/schnell`. Image fades in over 1.8 seconds.
  First prototype to generate a *semantic scene* from audio вАФ not abstract art, not notation, but a physical place.
  FAL_KEY in use ¬Ј ~$0.02/image ¬Ј 4.49 kB.

## Previous newest (Cycle 68)

- **[/dream/55-webgpu-audio-fx](/dream/55-webgpu-audio-fx)** вАФ GPU Audio FX. `demoable`
  First prototype where audio samples themselves are computed on the GPU. A C-major chord
  (C4+E4+G4+C5) is synthesized in JS, uploaded to a WebGPU storage buffer, then processed
  through two sequential WGSL compute shader passes: **Pass 1** вАФ pitch-shift via
  speed-adjusted linear interpolation (0.5√Ч = octave down, 2.0√Ч = octave up); **Pass 2** вАФ
  6-tap FIR feedforward reverb (delay taps at 21вАУ105 ms, gain 0.40вЖТ0.07 per tap). Result
  reads back to CPU and plays looped. GPU timing displayed (typically 30вАУ80ms вАФ PCIe transfer
  dominated). Waveform comparison strips (original blue vs GPU-processed orange). All 54 prior
  prototypes use Web Audio API; this is the first where the signal DSP runs on the GPU.
  WebGPU required ¬Ј Zero new deps ¬Ј 3.85 kB.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/55-webgpu-audio-fx/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 67)

- **[/dream/54-maestro-stems](/dream/54-maestro-stems)** вАФ Maestro Stems. `demoable`
  Generate a 2.5-minute instrumental track вАФ then hear each stem played back from its own
  position in 3D space. Drums from directly overhead (+60¬∞), bass from below (вИТ30¬∞), melody
  from front-right (+30¬∞), harmonic filler from front-left (вИТ30¬∞). Five style presets
  (Cinematic, Jazz Trio, Ambient, Folk, Electronic). Editable prompt. Per-stem mix sliders
  (live gain, no restart). Per-stem mute. Top-down sphere canvas (same HRTF approach as
  `29-scene-spatial` and `53-ghost-sfx`). Raw API response in `<details>` for debugging.
  Qualitatively different spatial experience from `7-spatial` (frequency bands) вАФ this splits
  by musical role: the drum overhead is overhead *because it's the drum*, not because it's in
  the treble range. First prototype where a full AI-generated band plays around the listener.
  FAL_KEY in use ¬Ј $0.10/track ¬Ј 4.59 kB.
  вЪ† Endpoint `beatoven/music-generation` + `stems: true` input from RESEARCH.md ¬І101.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/54-maestro-stems/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 63)

- **[/dream/52-concept-steer](/dream/52-concept-steer)** вАФ Concept Steer. `demoable`
  A hexagonal radar chart synthesizer whose six axes are the vocabulary music AI models use
  internally: **Brightness** (filter fc 400вАУ6000 Hz), **Density** (BPM 40вАУ140 + voice count
  1вАУ5), **Regularity** (strict grid vs. free timing + jitter), **Complexity** (unison вЖТ 9th
  chord voicings), **Energy** (attack 0.8sвЖТ0.04s + gain), **Mode** (majorвЖТminorвЖТdiminished,
  continuous interpolation). Drag any handle; the synthesizer follows in real time. Four
  presets: Classical Fugue, Dark Ambient, Jazz Improv, Drone. Background glow shifts with
  Brightness + Mode. HUD shows current BPM + chord quality. Zero deps, zero API.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/52-concept-steer/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 62)

- **[/dream/51-diatonic-harmony](/dream/51-diatonic-harmony)** вАФ Diatonic Harmony. `demoable`
  Play a melody; the key is detected from what you play (Krumhansl-Kessler chroma correlation),
  and each detected note gains its diatonic third and fifth as sine oscillators вАФ panned ¬±28¬∞
  for spatial separation. Three-color scrolling piano roll: **orange** (melody) ¬Ј **light blue**
  (3rd) ¬Ј **deep blue** (5th). Scale degree 7 gets a diminished fifth instead of perfect fifth вАФ
  visibly different bar position, audibly more tense. Demo: Bach BWV 772 with full auto-harmonies.
  Different from `23-pitch-harmonize`: that shifts a fixed interval; this detects the key and
  generates *scale-correct* voices. Zero deps, zero API.
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/51-diatonic-harmony/README.md`

    autoencoders find that transformer music models organize internally around: **Brightness**,

## Previous newest (Cycle 59)

- **[/dream/50-tap-rhythm](/dream/50-tap-rhythm)** вАФ Tap Rhythm. `demoable`
  The first prototype any non-musician can immediately use. Tap or clap a rhythm into the
  mic вЖТ onset detection measures your tempo вЖТ 32-step circular drum loop plays back in your
  detected BPM. Amplitude classifies drum type: gentle tap = kick (violet), medium = snare
  (cyan), hard/clap = hi-hat (amber). Clock-face display with rotating hand. Click any step
  to toggle. BPM slider. Demo mode (no permissions): 4-on-the-floor preset loads instantly.
  Drum synthesis: Web Audio only вАФ kick = sine frequency glide, snare = bandpass noise,
  hat = highpass noise. **Live-performance fit**: tap a groove at a venue, loop starts in 2s.
  Zero deps, zero API. Build: 5.13 kB.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/50-tap-rhythm/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 58)

- **[/dream/49-anemone-av](/dream/49-anemone-av)** вАФ Anemone AV.
  A bioluminescent sea anemone dancing to audio. 14 tentacles in a forward-kinematics
  chain of 4 segments each вАФ sub-bass sways the trunk, low-mids ripple branches,
  treble pulses the glowing violet tip beads. Percussive hits cause a full-body flash
  (all tips scale 1.4√Ч for ~200ms). Drag to orbit. Strong bloom makes the form glow
  against pure black. Zero new deps вАФ all Three.js packages were already installed.
  Demo mode: 6 incommensurable LFOs animate the form without mic permissions.

  **Why this is different from `21-three-mesh-av`**: the icosahedron is mathematical
  geometry that deforms. The anemone is a *living form* вАФ flexible tentacles with FK
  amplification (tips move 2.8√Ч the root), staggered phases so they ripple around the
  ring, bioluminescent color grading from cyan at the base to violet at the tips.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/49-anemone-av/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 57)

- **[/dream/48-arc-compose](/dream/48-arc-compose)** вАФ Arc Compose.
  Write a Resonance journey arc using structural section tags, hear MiniMax Music 2.6 generate a
  60вАУ90s AI piece that follows that exact structure.

  **Left panel**: A textarea pre-loaded with a four-section cinematic arc (`[Intro]` single piano
  in vast reverb / `[Build Up]` cello enters, tension / `[Chorus]` full orchestral peak / `[Outro]`
  piano alone, then silence). Eight section-tag buttons ([Intro], [Verse], [Pre-Chorus], [Build Up],
  [Chorus], [Bridge], [Outro], [Inst]) append directly to the arc. Style/genre field below.

  **Right panel**: Six-band radial bloom visualizer (same palette as `1-live`) during playback.
  Waveform strip with cyan playhead sweep. Replay from cache (no re-generation). Download MP3.

  **Why this is different**: `6-compose` sends a text description вЖТ music, with no structural
  control. `arc-compose` uses section tags as first-class parameters: `[Intro] вЖТ [Build Up] вЖТ
  [Chorus] вЖТ [Outro]` shapes the arc of the generated piece. This is the `18-elevenlabs-compose`
  idea (38 cycles in the queue) finally buildable at $0.03 instead of $1.13.

  вЪ† **API note**: Endpoint `fal-ai/minimax/music-01` and parameters from fal.ai naming conventions.
  If the prototype shows an error, paste the raw message and the agent fixes it next cycle.

  **$0.03/generation ¬Ј FAL_KEY already in use**
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/48-arc-compose/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 55)

- **[/dream/47-mood-journey](/dream/47-mood-journey)** вАФ Mood Journey.
  Click your **NOW** mood on the Russell circumplex (valence √Ч arousal), then click your **GOAL**
  mood, pick a duration (Quick 2m / Short 5m / Normal 10m / Deep 20m), and press **вЦґ Begin journey**.
  The dot glides from Now to Goal automatically вАФ no interaction needed. The music walks with it.

  **Two simultaneous audio layers both track the gliding position**:
  - Mood synthesis (from `38-mood-xy`): BPM, chord quality, register, attack, arpeggio, filter brightness
  - Isochronic tones (from `42-binaural`): ќ≤ 16Hz / ќ± 10Hz / ќЄ 6Hz / ќі 2Hz matching arousal level

  Canvas shows a quadrant color wash (amber=energetic+happy, purple=energetic+sad, teal=calm+happy,
  navy=calm+sad), a blue trail of the positions visited, a dashed green path to the goal, and a
  glowing dot at the current position. Noise layer (pink/brown) available mid-journey.

  **"Surrender control to the arc."** The first prototype that auto-generates a complete emotional
  trajectory rather than responding to manual input. Based on the proactive music therapy research
  cluster (RESEARCH.md ¬І84). Zero deps, no API keys.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/47-mood-journey/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 54)

- **[/dream/46-osc-composer](/dream/46-osc-composer)** вАФ Oscilloscope Composer.
  Design a Lissajous figure using three sliders (L freq, R freq, phase), then download the
  **stereo WAV that draws it** on an oscilloscope in XY mode. Five preset shapes map to musical
  intervals: Circle (1:1 unison), Figure-8 (1:2 octave), Trefoil (2:3 perfect fifth), Rose
  (3:4 perfect fourth), Starburst (3:5 major sixth). Puzzle mode shows a target figure alongside
  yours вАФ tune to match and collect the "вЬУ Matched!" badge.

  **The only prototype where the download IS the point** вАФ the WAV's L channel is the X axis
  and R channel is the Y axis. Load it in `20-scope` (Phase Portrait mode) and the figure
  reappears. Load it on a real oscilloscope and it draws on screen.

  **Musical intervals as geometry**: a perfect fifth is a 2:3 frequency ratio вАФ which draws
  a three-lobe trefoil. A perfect fourth draws a four-lobe rose. The visual shape IS the
  harmonic relationship. Zero deps, no API keys.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/46-osc-composer/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 53)

- **[/dream/45-guided-session](/dream/45-guided-session)** вАФ Guided Brainwave Session.
  Pick a journey ("Stressed вЖТ Calm", "Scattered вЖТ Calm", "Wired вЖТ Drowsy", "Alert вЖТ Deep Rest"),
  set a step duration (Quick 30s / Normal 5m / Deep 10m), and press **Begin journey**. Isochronic
  tones walk your brainwave frequency from the starting state to the goal state вАФ no headphones
  required (works with any speaker).

  Each step plays isochronic tones (amplitude-modulated carrier at 200 Hz) at the target frequency,
  then smoothly sweeps to the next waypoint with a 4-second time constant. At ќ≤вБЇ 24Hz: tight
  staccato rings. By ќ± 10Hz: gentle ripples. By ќЄ 4Hz: three-second expanding pulses. The canvas
  slows visibly as the journey descends вАФ the rings are a clock of the session's progress.

  **Path breadcrumb** shows each waypoint with the current one highlighted. **Progress bar** per
  step with timer. **"вЖТ next"** button available after 50% of step duration (sink faster if you
  want). **Auto-advance** after the full duration. **Noise layer** (pink for ќ≤/ќ±, brown for ќЄ/ќі)
  auto-switches on each step. **Journal textarea** (same localStorage pattern as `42-binaural`).
  **Session summary** on completion showing time per waypoint.

  **First Resonance prototype that is a genuine wellness tool** вАФ based on the proactive music
  therapy research cluster (RESEARCH.md ¬І¬І74, 75, 80). Descending-frequency arcs validated by
  three Frontiers 2026 papers. Zero deps; no API keys needed.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/45-guided-session/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 52)

- **[/dream/44-vocal-bgm](/dream/44-vocal-bgm)** вАФ Vocal BGM.
  Record 5вАУ15 seconds of humming, singing, or piano. Pick an arrangement style (jazz trio /
  ambient / cinematic / rock / folk). Click **Arrange вЖТ**. ACE-Step 1.5 on fal.ai receives
  your recording and generates a 30-second full-band arrangement where your melodic contour
  is the lead motif вАФ the AI adds drums, bass, chords, and harmony beneath your melody.

  **Different from `43-stable-extend`**: stable-extend continues your recording *forward*
  in time. vocal-bgm wraps a full band *around* your phrase вАФ your melody stays as the
  primary voice. Think of it as "here's the tune, now play it for me as a jazz trio."

  Genre selector shows the full tag string sent to ACE-Step (e.g. "jazz piano trio, warm,
  acoustic, 70 BPM, upright bass, brush drums") so the model's inputs are legible.
  `[inst]` lyrics tag prevents the model from adding AI vocals on top of your humming.
  Waveform strip: **amber** (your melody) | **blue** (full arrangement).
  Bloom visualizer during playback (same six-band palette as `1-live`).

  вЪ† **API note**: endpoint `fal-ai/ace-step/audio-to-audio` from RESEARCH.md ¬І77. If the
  prototype shows an error, the raw message is displayed вАФ paste it and we'll fix in the
  next cycle.

  **$0.006/arrangement. FAL_KEY already in use.**
  Design notes: `src/app/dream/44-vocal-bgm/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 47)

- **[/dream/42-binaural](/dream/42-binaural)** вАФ Binaural Beat Synthesizer.
  Two pure sine waves вАФ one per ear вАФ with a precise frequency difference the brain perceives
  as a beat at that difference frequency. The beat has no physical existence in the air; it's
  neurological. This is called the *frequency following response*, and it's the closest thing
  to "direct brain audio" in the Web Audio API.
  **Five brainwave states**: ќі (0.5вАУ4 Hz, deep violet) ¬Ј ќЄ (4вАУ8 Hz, indigo, meditative) ¬Ј
  ќ± (8вАУ13 Hz, cyan, relaxed вАФ default) ¬Ј ќ≤ (13вАУ30 Hz, green, focused) ¬Ј ќ≥ (30+ Hz, amber,
  high cognition). The canvas color shifts with the state; the ring expansion speed matches
  the beat frequency. At ќі 2 Hz: two slow tidal pulses per second. At ќ≥ 40 Hz: rings blur into
  constant shimmering amber glow (appropriate вАФ gamma is continuous, not discrete beats).
  **Two modes**: *binaural* (headphones required вАФ two separate ear tones) vs *isochronic*
  (speakers OK вАФ amplitude modulated carrier, audible as tremolo). Five presets. Live carrier
  and beat frequency sliders update the oscillators without restarting.
  **The second psychoacoustics prototype** (after `40-shepard-tone`): both explore the gap
  between what is physically present in the sound and what the brain perceives.
  **"A tone that doesn't exist вАФ until you listen to it."** Zero deps; pure Web Audio API.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/42-binaural/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 46)

- **[/dream/41-code-vis](/dream/41-code-vis)** вАФ Code Vis.
  A split-screen live coding environment: textarea DSL on the left, glowing canvas on the right.
  Each line of code is a synthesizer voice: `C4 tri 0.8` вЖТ a triangle-wave oscillator at C4 at
  amplitude 0.8. Edit the score; 400ms later the audio crossfades and the canvas updates.
  **Visual**: N voices arranged in an N-gon (triangle for a triad, square for a tetrad). Each ring
  glows in the `1-live` frequency hue (violet=bass, red=treble) and pulses at the BPM rate with a
  heartbeat sin¬≤ envelope. The circular constellation reads as a chord diagram: the pitch structure
  IS the shape. Default: C major triad вЖТ three differently-colored rings in a triangle.
  **DSL syntax**: `NOTE WAVE AMP` (e.g. `F#3 saw 0.4`, `Bb2 tri 0.7`, `A5 sin 0.3`).
  Waves: `sin tri saw sq`. BPM slider changes pulse rate live. вЖУ PNG saves a frame.
  **"Write a chord in 10 seconds. Hear it. See it."** Zero deps; pure Web Audio + Canvas2D.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/41-code-vis/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 45)

- **[/dream/40-shepard-tone](/dream/40-shepard-tone)** вАФ Shepard Tone.
  An endless musical staircase. The tone rises forever вАФ and never arrives.
  8 sine oscillators (A1вАУA8), each gaining a bell-curve amplitude that peaks at A4 (440Hz) and
  fades to near-silence at the extremes. As all oscillators glide upward together, the loud middle
  tones always seem to be rising вАФ but when A8 fades and the wrapped A1 re-enters, the transition
  is inaudible because both extremes are below the consciousness threshold.
  **Controls**: Rate slider (0.5вАУ30 BPM = octaves/min); Ascending/Descending toggle; Glide/Whole-tone/
  Semitone interval modes (each gives a different temporal rhythm to the illusion); Freeze (suspends
  the glide, revealing the chord); Mic mode (louder playing вЖТ faster ascent).
  **Visual**: a rotating logarithmic spiral (the helical pitch model вАФ chromatic height √Ч register);
  a glowing dot tracks the current phase position. Oscillator column (right): each of the 8 circles
  glows proportional to its current gain вАФ bright at center (A3вАУA5), dim at extremes (A1, A8).
  The glow sweeps upward then silently resets from the bottom. The visual IS the illusion.
  **"The first prototype about the gap between physical sound and perceived sound."**
  First psychoacoustics prototype in the sandbox. Zero deps; pure Web Audio oscillators.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/40-shepard-tone/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 43)

- **[/dream/39-anticipate](/dream/39-anticipate)** вАФ Aria Anticipate.
  Extends `33-aria-companion` with ReaLJam-style ghost-note anticipation. After you play a phrase
  and pause, Aria's entire planned response appears as **dashed blue outlines** in the ARIA panel вАФ
  all notes at once, before a single note sounds. You see the full melodic shape of the response
  in silence. Then each note solidifies with a bright flash as it sounds, left to right.
  The canvas uses a split past/future time window: user notes scroll left, Aria's ghost notes
  sit to the right of the center "now" line and sweep left to meet it as they play.
  **"Watch Aria decide before she plays."** Inspired by ReaLJam (CHI 2025): showing AI intention
  before execution was the single highest-rated design feature in human-AI piano experiments.
  Zero deps; pure Web Audio + Canvas2D.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/39-anticipate/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 42)

- **[/dream/38-mood-xy](/dream/38-mood-xy)** вАФ Mood XY.
  The Russell circumplex model as a musical instrument. A 2D canvas: X = valence (sad вЖР happy),
  Y = arousal (calm вЖХ energetic). Drag the dot anywhere. The synthesizer follows in real time:
  **arousal** controls BPM (40вАУ140), voice count (1вАУ4), register (C3вАУC5), attack (0.8sвЖТ0.04s),
  and whether chords arpeggiate or sound simultaneously. **Valence** controls chord quality
  (major / minor / dim), filter brightness (400вАУ5000 Hz), and note sustain length (+40% longer
  when sad). Background color shifts with quadrant: amber (excited+happy) вЖТ purple (excited+sad)
  вЖТ teal (calm+happy) вЖТ navy (calm+sad). Pastel trail shows your path.
  **Four immediately distinct sounds**: drag to top-right (bright major arpeggios, 120 BPM) ¬Ј
  top-left (dark diminished runs, 110 BPM) ¬Ј bottom-right (sustained major pads, 55 BPM) ¬Ј
  bottom-left (sparse minor chords, 40 BPM).
  **The first prototype where audio is generated FROM emotional coordinates, not analyzed from
  audio input.** "Navigate your musical mood." Zero deps; pure Web Audio + Canvas2D.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/38-mood-xy/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 41)

- **[/dream/37-ratio-lab](/dream/37-ratio-lab)** вАФ Ratio Lab.
  A 9√Ч5 Tonnetz lattice: each node is a just-intonation ratio relative to A3 (220 Hz drone).
  **Right = P5 (√Ч3/2). Up = M3 (√Ч5/4). Diagonal = m3 (√Ч6/5).** Click any node to hear it
  against the drone вАФ consonant intervals feel "locked in," no beating. Multiple nodes ring
  simultaneously. Hover for JI ratio + Hz + cents deviation from equal temperament.
  Color encodes consonance: **amber/warm (simple ratio, large)** вЖТ **cool blue (complex, small)**.
  Mic mode: autocorrelation pitch detection highlights the nearest lattice node with a pulsing
  blue ring. Hold a chord on piano вАФ multiple nodes glow and their triangle shape on the lattice
  IS the chord quality (major = one orientation, minor = inverted).
  **The first prototype about tuning theory.** "Navigate harmony as a landscape."
  Zero deps; pure Web Audio + Canvas2D.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/37-ratio-lab/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 40)

- **[/dream/36-pluck-field](/dream/36-pluck-field)** вАФ Pluck Field.
  24 Karplus-Strong virtual strings in a 4√Ч6 grid: C pentatonic from C2 to G6. Click any
  cell to pluck вАФ the string vibrates as an animated standing wave and rings with synthesized
  plucked-string audio (no oscillators, no samples: feedback delay loop IS the string). The
  first prototype built on **physical modeling synthesis**. Low strings ring for ~3 seconds;
  high strings decay in ~0.5s вАФ all from the physics. Hold multiple cells to hear chords bloom.
  **Touch/drag = glissando** (sweep your finger across cells like a harp).
  Mic mode: percussion onsets pluck random strings in the octave range matching your centroid.
  Color: violet (C2) вЖТ orange (G6), same palette as `1-live`.
  **"What if the canvas was a harp?"** Zero deps; pure Web Audio API.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/36-pluck-field/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 39 вАФ research)

- **Cycle 39 was a research sweep** (no new prototype). 8 new entries in RESEARCH.md (¬І¬І53вАУ60).
  5 new prototype ideas added to IDEAS.md. Highlights:

  - **`36-pluck-field`** (build next) вАФ Karplus-Strong virtual string field. Click canvas cells
    to pluck 24 tuned virtual strings (C pentatonic, 4 octaves). Each string = 3 Web Audio nodes
    (`DelayNode` feedback loop). Physical modeling synthesis вАФ none of the 35 existing prototypes
    use it. "What if the canvas was a harp?" Zero deps, one cycle.

  - **`37-ratio-lab`** (build next) вАФ Tonnetz just intonation lattice explorer. Click any ratio
    node to hear the just-intonation interval against a drone. Mic mode shows where your pitch
    falls on the lattice. First tuning-theory prototype in the sandbox. High surprise value.
    Inspired by LIMITER (arxiv 2507.08675).

  - **`38-mood-xy`** вАФ Russell circumplex emotion synthesis. Drag a dot on a 2D valence√Чarousal
    plane; Web Audio synthesizes music in real time (tempo, chord quality, register, brightness
    all driven by coordinates). First prototype where audio is *output* from emotional coordinates,
    not *input*. Inspired by AffectMachine-Pop (arxiv 2506.08200).

  - **`39-anticipate`** вАФ ReaLJam-inspired ghost-note anticipation display. Extends
    `33-aria-companion`: AI's planned response notes appear as ghost bars before they play.
    "Watch Aria decide before she plays." Inspired by ReaLJam (arxiv 2502.21267, CHI 2025).

  - **`40-browser-musicgen`** вАФ In-browser MusicGen via Transformers.js (~390MB ONNX model,
    cached after first load, zero API cost). Potential path for the long-queued `6-compose`.
    Needs Karel OK on model download size.

  - **Open question for Karel**: OK on ~390MB Transformers.js model download for in-browser
    AI music generation? Also: Gemini key for `lyria-jam`? MediaPipe CDN for `gesture-music`?

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 38)

- **[/dream/35-loop-station](/dream/35-loop-station)** вАФ Loop Station.
  The first prototype where you **build** a composition rather than react to one. Four
  phase-locked recording slots: tap **вЧП REC** to record from mic, **вЦ† STOP** to close the
  loop вАФ it starts playing immediately, locked to the same grid as all other slots. MUTE,
  CLEAR, and 1/2/4-bar length selectors per slot. BPM tap-tempo button.
  Demo loads 4 pre-synthesized loops (sub-bass drone, piano phrase, arpeggio, click track)
  all phase-locked at 80 BPM вАФ try вЦґ Load demo loops for an immediate layered result.
  Canvas mini-waveform per slot with scrolling playhead. Color scheme matches `1-live`
  (violet=sub-bass, green=low-mid, orange=high-mid, yellow=mid).
  **"Build a multi-layer performance in real time."** Zero deps; pure Web Audio API.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/35-loop-station/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 37)

- **[/dream/34-spectral-morph](/dream/34-spectral-morph)** вАФ Spectral Morph.
  The first prototype to **resynthesize** rather than visualize. Two audio sources (A and B)
  are FFT'd every 256 samples by an inline AudioWorklet (1024-point Cooley-Tukey, hand-rolled).
  The morph slider blends their magnitude spectra in the frequency domain, then IFFTs back to
  audio with Source A's phase. At t=0.5 you hear a genuinely new timbre вАФ not a crossfade.
  Demo: sawtooth (many harmonics) вЖТ sine (one harmonic). Best: try Source B = **noise** вАФ
  the saw-to-noise cross-dissolve is something a crossfade can never produce.
  Visual: three stacked spectrum strips (B top, Blend middle, A bottom); vertical cursor shows
  morph position live.
  **"Morph between your piano and a sawtooth вАФ through the spectrum, not a mixer."**
  Zero deps; pure Web Audio + inline FFT worklet.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/34-spectral-morph/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 36)

- **[/dream/33-aria-companion](/dream/33-aria-companion)** вАФ Aria Companion.
  The first **dialogue** prototype in the sandbox вАФ all 32 previous prototypes are reactive;
  this one listens, waits, and responds. Play a melody on piano or sing; after 2 seconds of
  silence, Aria generates a response phrase using a Markov chain learned from your own note
  transitions. The longer you play, the more Aria mirrors your interval tendencies.
  Visual: split dual piano roll вАФ YOU (warm orange, top) + ARIA (cool blue, bottom).
  **"The piano responds when you rest."** Zero deps; no ML. ~20 lines of Markov JS.

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/33-aria-companion/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 35 вАФ research)

- **Cycle 35 was a research sweep** (no new prototype). 9 new entries in RESEARCH.md (¬І¬І44вАУ52).
  3 new prototype ideas added to IDEAS.md. Highlights:

  - **`aria-companion`** (build next) вАФ turn-taking piano dialogue agent. User plays a phrase;
    after 2s of silence the system generates a Markov-chain response and plays it back as a piano
    sound. "The piano responds when you rest." First **dialogue** prototype in the sandbox (all
    32 previous are *reactive*, not compositional). Inspired by Aria-Duet, NeurIPS 2025.
    Zero deps, one cycle.

  - **`spectral-morph`** вАФ AudioWorklet FFT magnitude interpolation. Morph slider blends the
    spectral character of two audio sources вЖТ resynthesized output. Unique: first prototype to
    resynthesize from frequency-domain manipulation rather than just analyze it. Zero deps,
    one cycle.

  - **`loop-station`** вАФ 4-slot BPM-synced live loop station. First prototype that lets you
    BUILD a multi-layer composition over time rather than playing/watching. Loop boundary
    crossfade eliminates clicks. Demo pre-loads 4 loops. Performance-relevant. Zero deps,
    one cycle.

  - **Design Space for Live Music Agents** (arxiv 2602.05064, Feb 2026): taxonomy of 184 live
    music systems. Key insight: "dialogue agents" (listen вЖТ compose вЖТ respond) are the
    least-explored category, and the sandbox has zero. `aria-companion` fills this gap.

  - **Web Audio API вАФ Configurable Render Quantum** (Q4 2026 spec): buffer size below 128 samples
    вЖТ sub-3ms audio latency. Will improve all real-time pitch-detection prototypes once shipped.

  - **iPlug3** (Jan 2026): WebGPU + SDL3 + MCP audio plugin framework вАФ scripts mirror web APIs.
    **Best current path to "Resonance as an installation"** (Tauri mode, venue deployment).

  - **Kling 2.6**: Ghost image + motion prompt вЖТ 5s cinematic clip + native audio, $0.14/sec.
    New option for ghost-animate (alongside HappyHorse, Veo 3.1 Fast). Speech synthesis: the
    Ghost can say a line from the journey narrative in the clip. Admin-only, needs FAL_KEY.

  **Open questions for Karel**:
  - Gemini API key still needed for `30-lyria-jam` (infinite steering AI music).
  - CDN dep (~8MB) still pending for `31-gesture-music` (hand gesture вЖТ synth).
  - `iPlug3` вАФ is the "Resonance as an installation" path worth a dedicated design cycle?

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 34)

- **[/dream/32-mood-vis](/dream/32-mood-vis)** вАФ Mood Viz.
  Audio features (energy, spectral brightness, band coefficient of variation) drive a rule-based
  classifier that picks one of six visual modes automatically вАФ and transitions between them as
  the music changes character. Six moods вЖТ six aesthetics: Lissajous (minimal/silence), ink rings
  (calm+bright), orbital drift (calm+dark), radial bloom (energetic+bright), pulse field
  (energetic+dark), spectral mandala (complex). 7% trail persistence gives natural ~1s crossfades.
  Demo cycles through all six moods at 5s each without a mic. HUD shows current mood + amplitude,
  centroid, spread in real time. **"The visualizer that listens вАФ and decides."**

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/32-mood-vis/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 33)

- **[/dream/29-scene-spatial](/dream/29-scene-spatial)** вАФ Scene Spatial.
  Six Ghost narrative scenes (Stone Chamber вЖТ Cosmic Ascension), each with a hand-authored 3D
  soundscape built from oscillators, filtered noise, and FM chirps вАФ no audio files. Sources
  placed on a sphere via Web Audio HRTF PannerNode. Drag any colored dot to reposition a sound
  source in real time; the HRTF updates instantly. Canvas shows top-down sphere view (F/B/L/R
  compass; вЦ≤/вЦЉ for elevation). Reverb from a per-scene procedurally generated impulse response.
  **"Each Ghost scene has a sound as distinctive as its visuals вАФ wear headphones."**
  Best demo: Forest Dawn (canopy birds above, stream left, piano right вАФ three distinct azimuths).

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/29-scene-spatial/README.md`

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 32)

- **[/dream/28-chord-canvas](/dream/28-chord-canvas)** вАФ Chord Canvas.
  Play a chord on piano (or mic any pitched source) вАФ the chord name appears in huge monospace
  type: "Dm", "G", "C". Hue is the root note (C=red, D=yellow, G=blue, A=violet). A scrolling
  timeline strip below shows your chord history as colored blocks; wider = held longer. A 12-bar
  chromagram shows pitch-class energy in real time. Demo mode plays iiвАУVвАУI (Dm7вЖТG7вЖТCmaj7) with
  audible triangle oscillators вАФ you hear the chords as the detector names them.
  **"The first prototype to explicitly name musical structure."**
  Natural complement to `24-piano-roll` (pitch positions) and `22-code-score` (written notation).

  Design notes: `src/app/dream/28-chord-canvas/README.md`

## Previous newest (Cycle 30)

- **[/dream/26-score-follow](/dream/26-score-follow)** вАФ Score Follow.
  Bach Invention No.1 displayed as a static piano roll. Play along on piano or sing вАФ
  the score lights green as you match each note (¬±1.5 semitone tolerance). The cursor
  advances only when you play the right pitch; it pauses on silence, backs up one note
  after 1.5s of wrong-note playing (forgiveness mode). Yellow triangle at the cursor
  shows your detected pitch in real time. Demo mode plays the score and self-matches вАФ
  cursor advances perfectly through all 35 notes.
  **"The first prototype where your playing is evaluated, not just visualized."**
  Natural partner to `24-piano-roll` (see what you played) and `22-code-score` (write
  the score). This one asks you to *reproduce* it.

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 29)

- **[/dream/25-cellular](/dream/25-cellular)** вАФ Cellular.
  Conway's Game of Life where each column of the grid is a musical pitch (C2 left вЖТ C5 right).
  Living cells trigger triangle-wave notes; the *shape* of a pattern IS its melody.
  Glider = a wandering 4-note motif that walks up and down the pitch axis. Pulsar = a strict
  3-tick rhythmic chord machine. Acorn/R-pentomino = methuselahs that evolve chaotically for
  hundreds of generations. Click/drag to paint cells; BPM slider (40вАУ120). No mic needed.
  **"What if generative music was also life?"**

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 28)

- **[/dream/24-piano-roll](/dream/24-piano-roll)** вАФ Piano Roll.
  Play piano or sing вАФ each note appears as a glowing colored bar scrolling left, placed at its
  MIDI pitch on a vertical axis (C2 bottom, C7 top). The exact representation every DAW uses,
  rendered live from mic input. Hue matches `1-live` and `13-piano-canvas` (low pitch = cool,
  high = warm). Piano key sidebar highlights the active key. BPM slider sets scroll speed.
  Demo mode plays Bach Invention No.1 silently and paints its own notes вАФ the roll fills itself
  from the score in real time. **"What you played, as notation."**
  Completes the piano-representation triptych: `13-piano-canvas` (abstract painting),
  `22-code-score` (written score), `24-piano-roll` (scrolling notation).

## Previous newest (Cycle 27 вАФ research)

- **Cycle 27 was a research sweep** (no new prototype). 8 new entries in RESEARCH.md (¬І¬І29вАУ36).
  4 new prototype ideas added to IDEAS.md. Highlights:

  - **`24-piano-roll`** (build next) вАФ live scrolling piano roll from mic pitch detection. Every
    DAW has one; this is the first in the dream sandbox. Companion to `13-piano-canvas` (abstract
    painting) and `22-code-score` (written notation). Zero deps, one-cycle build.
  - **`25-cellular`** вАФ Conway's Game of Life as a musical instrument. Living cells trigger pitched
    notes; gliders make repeating loops, oscillators make rhythmic patterns. Completely different
    generative paradigm from all 23 existing prototypes. Inspired by CLAVIER-36.
  - **`26-score-follow`** вАФ live score cursor: play the Bach fragment from `22-code-score` on your
    piano; the score highlights as you match notes. Autocorrelation pitch detection + symbol tracking.
  - **`27-gpu-additive`** вАФ particles ARE Fourier partials; GPU physics IS the synthesizer. Most
    technically ambitious idea in the queue (2+ cycles). Requires WebGPU.
  - **Kling 3.0 update** вАФ multi-shot storyboarding + native audio enables a *full Ghost journey arc*
    as a coherent video sequence (4 shots, character consistency, native audio). Better than
    HappyHorse for multi-shot arcs. Single-clip: HappyHorse still wins.
  - **WASM AudioWorklet** trend confirmed as 2026 standard for browser DSP. Could upgrade
    `23-pitch-harmonize` with WASM FFT vocoder, but needs Karel approval on the build-step approach.
  - **Score following research active** (arxiv 2505.05078, May 2026) вАФ 174ms latency, browser-feasible.

## Previous newest (Cycle 26)

- **[/dream/23-pitch-harmonize](/dream/23-pitch-harmonize)** вАФ Pitch Harmonize.
  First prototype that **transforms** audio in real time. Mic вЖТ AudioWorklet ring-buffer
  pitch shifter вЖТ HRTF 3D position. Pick an interval (+4th, +5th, +8va, -8va), drag the
  harmony to any azimuth (вИТ90¬∞ left вЖФ +90¬∞ right). With headphones: you and your
  pitch-shifted copy float apart in space. Visual: **dual phase-portrait vectorscope** on
  one canvas вАФ orange trail = dry, blue trail = harmony. At a fifth interval, the two
  ellipses tilt at different angles (the interval IS a geometric relationship between them).
  Zero npm deps; AudioWorklet loaded from inline Blob URL.
  **"Become your own accompanist."**

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 25)

- **[/dream/22-code-score](/dream/22-code-score)** вАФ Code Score.
  Write a melody in the textarea; press play. Each note sounds and simultaneously paints itself
  onto the canvas. Score DSL: `C5 E D5 E E5 E F5 E` (note + duration), `rest Q`, `[C4 E4 G4] Q`
  (chords), `// comments`. Durations: W H Q E S = whole вЖТ sixteenth. BPM slider (40вАУ200).
  **Rising phrases arc upward, descending phrases drift down** вАФ the melodic contour IS the
  stroke's shape. Chord tones stack as parallel colored bars above the root.
  Default demo: Bach Invention No.1 in C major (BWV 772). Save painting as PNG.
  **The reverse of `13-piano-canvas`** вАФ instead of playing вЖТ painting, you write вЖТ both
  hear and see.

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 24)

- **[/dream/21-three-mesh-av](/dream/21-three-mesh-av)** вАФ Three.js Mesh AV.
  First prototype using Three.js + React Three Fiber. An icosahedron whose surface deforms
  live with audio вАФ **bass expands the equatorial belt**, **treble pushes the polar caps**,
  organic noise breathes the surface at silence. Custom GLSL vertex shader with view-space
  Fresnel rim glow. Bloom post-processing from `@react-three/postprocessing` makes displaced
  vertices glow into soft halos. Drag to orbit, scroll to zoom. Demo mode (no mic) and mic mode.
  **21 prototypes, and this is the first to use Three.js** вАФ it was sitting installed and unused
  for 20 cycles.

## Previous newest (Cycle 22)

- **[/dream/20-scope](/dream/20-scope)** вАФ Vectorscope.
  Two modes: **Lissajous demo** (no permissions) plots two sine waves against each other вАФ
  each musical ratio (octave, fifth, fourth, M3rd, m3rd) traces a distinct closed figure.
  The CRT phosphor persistence makes cusps glow bright and fast arcs dim, exactly like
  a real oscilloscope. **Phase portrait** (mic) plots the live signal against its own past
  at an adjustable delay вАФ a single piano note makes an ellipse, a chord makes overlapping
  loops, percussion makes an explosive spray. Rainbow colors from direction-of-travel hue
  (atan2 of trajectory tangent). **The geometry of harmony вАФ visible.**

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 21)

- **[/dream/19-cymatics](/dream/19-cymatics)** вАФ Cymatics.
  Sand particles settling into Chladni figures вАФ the geometric node line patterns of a
  vibrating plate. 2000 amber grains drift onto the exact curves where sound is stationary.
  Eight modes from simple (1,2) Ring to intricate (5,6) Snowflake. Additive blending makes
  the node lines glow bright against black. Demo auto-cycles every 4.5s; mic mode maps
  spectral centroid to mode; manual buttons always override. **The hidden geometry of
  frequency вАФ what Resonance literally means.**

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 20)

- **[/dream/18-granular](/dream/18-granular)** вАФ Granular Cloud.
  Your audio shattered into overlapping grains and reassembled as a glowing cloud. Each dot IS
  a grain playing: X = where in the recent audio buffer it was sampled from, Y = its pitch shift
  in cents. Hue encodes buffer age (blue = older audio, orange = most recent). Additive blending
  makes dense grain regions glow bright. Four sliders: density, pitch range, grain size, scatter.
  Demo mode (synthetic oscillators, no permissions) and mic mode. **First prototype that transforms
  rather than visualizes вАФ the dots are the sound.**

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 19)

- **[/dream/17-acoustic-trail](/dream/17-acoustic-trail)** вАФ Acoustic Trail 3D.
  Your audio mapped to its own coordinate space: spectral centroid вЖТ X, treble ratio вЖТ Y,
  bass energy вЖТ Z. Each frame leaves a glowing point; the trail accumulates into a 3D scatter
  cloud that is the acoustic fingerprint of the performance. Additive blending means dense regions
  (repeated acoustic patterns) glow brighter. Drag to rotate. Color = centroid warmth (indigo =
  dark/bassy вЖТ orange = bright/treble). Demo mode runs 6 LFO-modulated oscillators that trace a
  slow Lissajous path over 30 seconds. **First prototype where audio becomes its own geometry.**

---

## Previous newest (Cycle 17)

- **[/dream/16-particle-life-gpu](/dream/16-particle-life-gpu)** вАФ Particle Life GPU.
  9,000 particles across 6 species simulated entirely on the GPU via WGSL compute shaders вАФ
  10√Ч the count of `/dream/8-particle-life` (CPU, 900). Tiled N-body reduces GPU bandwidth
  64√Ч. Additive blending creates galaxy-cluster glow; dense cores bloom white-hot, tendrils
  spiral like galactic arms. Requires WebGPU. Same audio mapping: band energy вЖТ species
  turbulence, onset вЖТ matrix reshuffle. Demo mode shows periodic reshuffles automatically.
  **10√Ч more particles. GPU-native emergent behavior.**

## Previous newest (Cycle 16)

## Previous newest (Cycle 15)

- **[/dream/14-typography](/dream/14-typography)** вАФ Kinetic Typography.
  Six Resonance phrases вАФ RESONANCE, SOUND INTO LIGHT, BODY OF MUSIC, EACH NOTE A WAVE,
  FREQUENCIES, OF BEING вАФ cycle every 8 seconds. Each letter is a physical object assigned
  to a frequency band; bass hits scatter bass-colored letters, treble shimmer agitates
  the high-frequency ones. Spring dynamics assemble the phrase from scatter over ~1.5s.

  turbulence live. **First prototype where language itself is the visual material.**

## Previous newest (Cycle 14)

- **[/dream/13-piano-canvas](/dream/13-piano-canvas)** вАФ Piano Canvas.
  Your improvisation becomes a painting. Mic input вЖТ autocorrelation pitch detection вЖТ
  each note leaves a glowing brush stroke on a persistent canvas. Pitch вЖТ hue (A4=0¬∞,
  rotating ~60¬∞/octave), loudness вЖТ weight (1.5вАУ8 px), duration вЖТ length. Rising melodic
  lines arc upward; descending ones drift down. Strokes accumulate; save as PNG when done.
  Demo mode plays a silent wandering melody so the canvas paints itself automatically.
  **The first prototype where the session leaves a permanent visual artifact.**

## Previous newest (Cycle 12)

- **[/dream/12-tessellate](/dream/12-tessellate)** вАФ Tessellate.
  A 40√Ч28 grid of Truchet tiles whose topology rewires on every beat. Each tile is a
  quarter-arc in one of two orientations; adjacent arcs form long connected curves across
  the canvas. On a bass hit, 12% of tiles flip simultaneously вАФ the curves disconnect and
  reconnect into entirely new paths in a flash of white. Between beats, bass energy drives
  a slower drizzle of individual tile flips. Two complementary arc colors (warm + cool)
  rotate through the spectrum; mids control saturation. Op-art aesthetic вАФ the first
  tile-based geometric prototype in the sandbox.
  **Start demo** for immediate visuals (no permissions). **Start mic** for live audio response.
  **Reshuffle** button resets the grid topology.

---

## Previous (Cycle 11)

- **[/dream/11-terrain](/dream/11-terrain)** вАФ Spectrogram Terrain.
  Your audio history becomes a 3D landscape: frequency on the X axis, time receding to the
  horizon on Z, amplitude as terrain height. 64 log-spaced frequency columns (30 Hz вЖТ 20 kHz),
  80 frames of time-history. Bass forms blue mountains; treble draws bright orange ridges.
  The newest frame is at your feet; the oldest fades to the horizon.
  **Start demo** for instant visuals (silent oscillators with LFO breathing). **Start mic**
  for live input вАФ piano chords show as overtone-series ridgelines.

## Previous (Cycle 10)

- **[/dream/10-strange](/dream/10-strange)** вАФ Strange Attractor + FM Synthesis.
  The Lorenz chaotic system traces a butterfly in 3D and simultaneously drives FM
  synthesis вАФ you see **and** hear the same chaos evolve. x-position flips carrier
  pitch between low (left wing, cool blue) and high (right wing, warm orange). z-height
  shapes harmonic richness (pure sine at bottom, buzzy at top). Wing transitions are
  irregular, non-repeating melody notes вАФ because they're deterministically chaotic.
  **Mic mode**: your volume reshapes ѕГ, accelerating wing transitions.
  **Start demo** for instant visuals + audio (no permissions, no upload).

## Previous (Cycle 9)

- **[/dream/9-reaction-diffusion](/dream/9-reaction-diffusion)** вАФ Gray-Scott
  Reaction Diffusion. Two virtual chemicals on a GPU grid create Turing patterns:
  coral, fingerprints, dividing spots, maze walls вАФ emergent from diffusion rates
  alone. Bass raises the feed rate; treble raises the kill rate; percussive hits
  inject new seed colonies. Click the canvas to inject manually. 6 presets.
  **Start demo** for instant visuals (no permissions).

## Previous (Cycle 8)

- **[/dream/8-particle-life](/dream/8-particle-life)** вАФ Particle Life. 900
  particles across 6 species governed by a random 6√Ч6 attraction/repulsion matrix.
  Emergent flocking, predator-prey spirals, orbiting clusters вАФ nobody programmed
  them. Audio energy injects velocity noise per species. Percussive onsets reshuffle
  the matrix mid-song. Matrix heatmap in the corner shows the current rules.
  **Start demo** for instant (no permissions). **Start mic** for live audio response.

## Previous newest (Cycles 4вАУ5)

- **[/dream/4-operator](/dream/4-operator)** вАФ Operator Panel вАФ two-pane
  live performance interface. Left: performer canvas (6 AV scenes). Right:
  scene picker, BPM tap, mic crowd-noise meter, MIDI detection. Keys 1вАУ6
  trigger scenes; Space taps BPM. Dip-to-black transitions. MIDI notes
  C3вАУA3 trigger scenes via hardware.
- Cycle 4 was a research cycle (no new prototype). See RESEARCH.md for 8 findings.

- **[/dream/3-fluid](/dream/3-fluid)** вАФ Fluid вАФ real-time Navier-Stokes ink-in-water
  driven by audio. Bass pulses the center, treble stirs turbulence, pitch shifts dye color.
  Drag to stir manually. Ambient drift mode for no-mic use.
- Cycle 4 was a research cycle (no new prototype). See RESEARCH.md for 8 findings.
