### 298-kids-echo-friend
**Status**: `demoable` ¬Ј **Cycle shipped**: 302 ¬Ј **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

Open `/dream/298-kids-echo-friend`. **For kids (4+).** Press the big **"Sing to me вЬ®"** button, then **sing any little phrase** вАФ a soft glowing creature *listens*, **sings your phrase back** in its own warm voice, and **remembers** it. Every few rounds it plays a growing **little song** made of everything you've sung together. No reading, no timer, no score, no fail; the creature hums a gentle drone so it's never silent.

This is the kids lane's first **call-and-response with memory** вАФ the antidote to the lane's local minimum ("poke a cute thing вЖТ it drones, no memory"). It reframes the banked `294-kids-voice-garden` **out of the C-pentatonic rut** (quantizes to **D-Dorian**) and moves `296-kids-firefly-tilt`'s remember-and-replay mechanic onto a **fresher input** (voice, not tilt) вАФ exactly the two queued kids moves from STATE ¬І301.

Four subsystems into one pipeline: (1) **real-time monophonic pitch detection** вАФ YIN/NSDF (normalized square difference) autocorrelation on raw `AnalyserNode` PCM, no ML, no CDN model, mic analysis-only (never recorded/uploaded), each detected Hz snapped to D-Dorian; (2) **call-and-response memory engine** вАФ collects a phrase frame-by-frame, detects ~0.8вАУ1.2 s of silence as phrase-end, dedupes into notes, pushes to a growing memory, and every 3rd phrase replays the whole accumulated song; (3) **warm sing-back synth** вАФ sine + triangle through lowpass + short delay, drone-duck while echoing, all through a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter so it can never get loud/harsh; (4) **hand-written WebGL2 GLSL** creature вАФ an SDF blob with domain-warped organic jiggle on an aurora ground, pitch-driven hue (violetвЖТamberвЖТtealвЖТlime across the Dorian range), one orbiting **memory orb per remembered phrase**, and a sing-back pulse ring. Degrades gracefully: **no mic / permission denied вЖТ a hands-free auto-demo** sings 6 hand-authored D-Dorian phrases through the *identical* pitchвЖТechoвЖТmemory pipeline (so the whole loop demos itself on a phone with no mic), with a readable `text-rose-300` notice; **no WebGL2 вЖТ rose notice, audio still runs**; iOS-safe (mic + AudioContext created inside the click handler). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + WebGL2 are client-side; no recording, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **SingingSDS** (arXiv:2511.20972, Nov 2025 вАФ a dialogue agent that *responds by singing*; this is its fully-client, no-AI, kid-sized embodiment), **Pauline Oliveros, *Deep Listening*** (the listen-then-answer tradition), and the classic **call-and-response / "Simon"** memory game. Born from RESEARCH ¬І302. Shipped as the winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `299-kids-clap-band` (clap вЖТ onset-detection layered groove, Steve Reich *Clapping Music*) + `300-kids-blow-sail` (blow вЖТ breath-envelope sailing, Wiener-entropy breath/tone discriminator) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS.md. **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вАФ unverified surface: whether YIN pitch-tracking on a real 4-year-old's wobbly voice lands cleanly on the Dorian grid, and whether the sing-back-and-grow loop reads as a "song we're building together" vs. a list of echoes.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/298-kids-echo-friend/README.md`

### 303-kids-wind-harp
**Status**: `demoable` ¬Ј **Cycle shipped**: 304 ¬Ј **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

Open `/dream/303-kids-wind-harp`. **For kids (4+).** Hit the big amber **"Tilt to play вЦЄ"** button, then **tilt your phone or iPad** вАФ gravity swings a row of seven glowing strings like a wind-harp, and any string that swings far enough **plucks itself and sings**. The child plays music by *tipping the world*, hands-free; no tapping, no reading, no timer, no fail.

The lab's **first tilt-input piece since the loved `83-kids-tilt-rain`**, and a deliberate pull on three loved prototypes (`83-kids-tilt-rain`вЭ§пЄП tilt ¬Ј `105-pluck-field`вЭ§пЄП Karplus pluck ¬Ј `140-kids-string-bridge`вЭ§пЄП playable strings). It couples two genuinely **fresh-for-this-lab techniques**: (1) **Verlet-integration string physics** вАФ each of 7 strings is a 14-node mass-chain solved with Verlet + distance-constraint relaxation (3 passes/frame) on a **fixed 120 Hz step**; the device-tilt `deviceorientation` ќ≤/ќ≥ becomes a **gravity vector**, so tipping the tablet changes which way "down" is and the hanging strings swing and settle with believable physical motion; (2) **Karplus-Strong plucked-string synthesis** (Karplus & Strong, 1983) вАФ when a string's midpoint swing crosses a threshold it plucks: a noise burst rendered offline through a tuned lowpass-fed delay line into an `AudioBuffer`, bigger swing вЖТ louder/brighter/longer, per-string refractory so it never machine-guns, stereo-panned lowвЖТhigh. A third subsystem is the hand-written **raw WebGL2** ribbon renderer (matte alpha-over string curves expanded along their normals on a soft breathing dark gradient, a glow flash per pluck вАФ **no additive bloom, no three.js**). The 7 strings are tuned to **D-Dorian** (D E F G A B C вАФ explicitly NOT C-major-pentatonic), with an ambient D+A drone underneath and a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter so simultaneous plucks can never blast.

Degrades at every step so it's **fully playable on a desktop with no sensors**: iOS 13+ `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` is called inside the Start gesture (denial вЖТ readable `text-rose-300` note + fallback); **pointer-drag** tilts the gravity vector on desktop; and a **2 s auto-sway** kicks in when no tilt events arrive so the harp **plays itself** and a 4-year-old (or Karel at 06:30 on his phone) always sees causeвЖТeffect. No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: the **Aeolian harp** (a stringed instrument played by moving air rather than fingers вАФ here the child's tilt is the wind); the **Karplus-Strong** plucked-string algorithm (1983). Chains from RESEARCH ¬І304 (*Rhythm in the Air*, arXiv:2511.00793, Nov 2025 вАФ embodied motion вЖТ scale-constrained musical output; tilt is the body, D-Dorian is the scale constraint). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `299-kids-clap-band` (clap вЖТ HFC-onset-detection layered groove, Steve Reich *Clapping Music*) + `300-kids-blow-sail` (blow вЖТ Wiener-entropy breath-envelope sailing) build-reviewed and re-banked in IDEAS.md. **Ambition 2/5**: never-used-technique(#1, Verlet rope physics) + named-reference(#3). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вАФ unverified surface: the deviceorientationвЖТgravity *feel* and pluck threshold on a real tilting hand, the iOS permission branches, and the rendered look of the ribbon strips (no sensors/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/303-kids-wind-harp/README.md`

### 306-kids-rain-shaker
**Status**: `demoable` ¬Ј **Cycle shipped**: 306 ¬Ј **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

Open `/dream/306-kids-rain-shaker`. **For kids (4+).** Tap the big amber **"Shake to play"** button, then **shake the phone or iPad like a rainstick or maraca** вАФ gentle shakes make a soft trickle of beads, bigger shakes tumble a warm rain down the screen, and each strong shake-peak strikes a **D-Dorian** bell. The child plays by *moving the whole device*, hands-free; no tapping, no reading, no timer, no fail. An always-on pad keeps it alive, and a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter means even the most vigorous shaking can never blast.

The lab's **first `devicemotion` / accelerometer instrument** in 300+ prototypes вАФ and deliberately a *different sensor and axis* from the lab's prior motion pieces: `303-kids-wind-harp` and `290-kids-sound-safari` used `deviceorientation` (tilt ќ≤/ќ≥ and compass heading ќ± вАФ device *orientation*), whereas this reads `accelerationIncludingGravity` for shake *energy*. Three subsystems: (1) **`shake.ts`** вАФ per `devicemotion` event, read the acceleration vector, subtract a slow per-axis running average (a **high-pass** that removes the constant gravity component), take the magnitude, and feed a smoothed **shake-energy envelope** (fast attack, slow release); threshold crossings with a ~130 ms refractory emit discrete **hit** events while the continuous envelope drives bead density. Every input path (motion, pointer-shake, auto-demo) feeds the IDENTICAL `pushSample` pipeline. (2) **`rain-audio.ts`** вАФ a soft D-Dorian pad drone (detuned triangles, breathing lowpass) + a rainstick trickle of short filtered-noise bead grains whose spawn rate/brightness track the live energy + **FM bell chimes** struck on hit events (panned, velocity from hit strength, lowpassed so they never get piercing), all summed through a final `DynamicsCompressor` used as a limiter. (3) **`rain-gl.ts`** вАФ raw **WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL ES 3.00): a soft **darkвЖТdawn** gradient quad that warms with energy + a CPU-simulated pool of up to 600 falling **bead/rain particles** as soft point-sprites in **matte premultiplied alpha-over** (the lab's non-additive house style вАФ switched from the builder's additive blend during curation to honor the JURY's anti-glow ban) + warm glow blooms on each bell. DPR/resize aware.

Degrades for the 06:30 phone review: iOS 13+ `DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission()` is called inside the Start tap (denial вЖТ readable `text-rose-300` note + fallback); on a sensor-less device it falls back to **pointer/mouse-shake** (fast pointer movement synthesises an acceleration vector into the same detector) and a gentle **auto-demo** that rains and chimes by itself; no WebGL2 вЖТ `text-rose-300` notice, audio still runs. `AudioContext` is created inside the Start tap (iOS-safe). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + a motion sensor + WebGL2 are all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: the **rainstick** (Andean/Chilean cactus-spine instrument played by tilting and shaking) + the **maraca / shaker** percussion tradition; the embodied-motionвЖТsound mapping echoes movement-sonification research (**CHI 2026**, *Designing Interactive Movement Sonification*). Born from RESEARCH ¬І306. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `304-kids-clap-band` (clap вЖТ HFC-onset-detection layered groove, Steve Reich *Clapping Music*, D-Mixolydian) + `305-kids-blow-sail` (blow вЖТ Wiener-entropy breath-envelope sailing, C-Lydian) build-verified and re-banked in IDEAS.md. **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, devicemotion shake-energy) + named-reference(#3) + recent-research(#5, ¬І306). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вАФ unverified surface: the shake threshold/scale *feel* on a real shaking hand (one-number fixes if too touchy or too stiff), the auto-demo chime cadence, and WebGL2 point-size behaviour across GPUs (no phone sensor / GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/306-kids-rain-shaker/README.md`

### 371-kids-clap-along
**Status**: `demoable` ¬Ј **Cycle shipped**: 336 ¬Ј **Last touched**: 2026-06-07

Open `/dream/371-kids-clap-along`. **For kids (4+).** Tap the big amber **"Clap to play вЦЄ"** button, then **clap your real hands** вАФ a friendly creature claps a short rhythm, you clap it back, and **the phone HEARS your claps through the microphone** (no screen-tapping). On a good-enough match the creature delights and the shared rhythm **grows by one clap** вАФ Simon-style вАФ so the little song the two of you share gets longer and longer. No reading, no score, no fail; a missed clap-back just warmly replays the same pattern ("let's try together").

The lab's **first prototype that actually LISTENS for a real hand-clap.** A grep audit found the lab has shipped call-and-response/echo rhythm **five times** (`154-kids-clap-back`, `213-kids-echo-drum`, `209-kids-drum-tap`, `150-kids-beat-builder`, `218-kids-xylophone-drops`) вАФ and **every one is TOUCH** (the device plays a pattern, the child taps the screen back). None hear a real clap; onset detection itself was banked-but-never-shipped (`299`/`304-kids-clap-band`). 371 finally does it. Four subsystems: (1) **`onset.ts`** вАФ real-time **acoustic onset detection**: each animation frame it reads the mic's FFT from an `AnalyserNode` (`getFloatFrequencyData`, `fftSize` 1024, `smoothingTimeConstant` 0 so transients aren't blurred), computes a **spectral-flux √Ч high-frequency-content (HFC)** novelty (half-wave-rectified positive bin-to-bin energy increases, weighted toward high bins so a bright broadband clap pops while the steady drone/voice stays low), compares it to an **adaptive threshold** (slow running mean √Ч slack + floor, so it self-tunes to a loud kitchen or quiet bedroom), and applies a **~140 ms refractory window** so one clap вАФ even a wobbly toddler double-bounce вАФ counts once. (2) **`pattern.ts`** вАФ a **growing-memory call-and-response state machine** (`intro вЖТ calling вЖТ waiting вЖТ judging вЖТ celebrate`): the creature calls a rhythm, the child's detected onset times are greedily matched against the target beats within a generous **¬±0.34 s** tolerance, ~60% lands = a match вЖТ celebrate + **grow the pattern by one beat** (occasionally a syncopation); a miss costs nothing. The growing shared rhythm IS the song вАФ the **memory/consequence** the jury asked the kids lane for after `350-kids-bump-along` regressed to a memory-less poke-toy. (3) **`clap-audio.ts`** вАФ an always-on soft **D + A drone** + **D-Dorian** tuned clap voices (D E F G A B C вАФ explicitly NOT C-major-pentatonic), call panned/brightened one way and the child's answer the other (two voices), beats tuned up the scale by position so the growing rhythm reads as a little tune, a rising reward sparkle on each grow, all through a `DynamicsCompressor` hard limiter so it can never blast. (4) **`creature-gl.ts`** вАФ raw **WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL ES 3.00): one warm **breathing creature** (squashes when it claps, leans in to listen on the child's turn, eyes curve into happy arcs when delighted) above a **row of glowing clap beads** that grows with the song so the child can *see* it getting longer вАФ amber for the call, mint for the answer вАФ in **matte premultiplied alpha-over** (the lab's non-additive house style, no glow-stacking).

One unified pipeline, three doors: every input route is judged by the **identical** onset machine вАФ **mic** (real acoustic claps via `sampleMic()`), **pointer/tap clap** (mic denied вЖТ a tap plays a **real broadband noise burst** into the *same* analyser so it's detected exactly like a hand clap), and **auto-demo** (no claps for ~4.5 s вЖТ the creature calls AND claps its own answer back through the same `clap()вЖТanalyserвЖТdetector` path, so the piece **plays itself hands-free** for the 06:30 phone review). Degrades at every step: `AudioContext` + `getUserMedia({audio})` created **inside the Start tap** (iOS-safe); mic is analysis-only (never recorded/uploaded, analyser not routed to the speakers вЖТ no feedback); mic denied вЖТ readable `text-rose-300` notice + tap-to-clap fallback; no WebGL2 вЖТ `text-rose-300` notice, audio still runs. No API route, no `guard` needed (mic-analysis + WebGL2 all client-side; no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Drumball** (Audio Mostly 2024 вАФ a tangible call-and-response drumming system inspired by the West African djembe) ¬Ј **Steve Reich, *Clapping Music* (1972)** ¬Ј the African call-and-response / talking-drum tradition. Born from RESEARCH ¬І336 (lab-gap audit: 5√Ч clap-back, all touch; 0√Ч real mic clap). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `372-kids-blow-garden` (blow вЖТ Wiener-entropy breath-envelope growing garden) + `373-kids-firefly-path` (tap-to-lay-stones вЖТ firefly replays a growing D-Dorian path) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬І336. **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, acoustic clap onset detection) + вЙ•3-subsystems(#2) + named-reference(#3). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вАФ unverified surface: the onset thresholds/HFC weighting on a real mic (too-hot self-trigger vs. too-deaf soft claps; defaults lean forgiving), drone-vs-mic self-noise under real AGC, the ¬±0.34 s / ~60% match tolerance against a real toddler clap-back, and the WebGL2 creature/bead sizing (no mic / GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/371-kids-clap-along/README.md`

### 375-tempo-canon
**Status**: `demoable` ¬Ј **Cycle shipped**: 337 ¬Ј **Last touched**: 2026-06-07

Open `/dream/375-tempo-canon`. **The accompanist that follows YOU.** Press the big violet **"Play demo вЦЄ"** button (or play it yourself on the home-row keys / a Web MIDI keyboard): you play the melody of Beethoven's **Ode to Joy** (the right hand), and the system aligns your live performance to the reference score in real time and plays the **bass + chord accompaniment (the left hand) locked to your tempo** вАФ speeding up when you rush, slowing when you ritard. The signature is that you can *literally see the machine following you*: the **warping path** вАФ a glowing diagonal that bends steeper when you rush and flattens when you drag вАФ is the centerpiece visual.

The JURY's **#1 standing adult ask, finally taken**: a real-time score-follower / live-accompaniment tool. Score-following had **lost curation three times** (re-rejected cycle 335) for three specific reasons, and this build is a deliberate answer to each: (a) *"not lab-first вАФ `26-score-follow` exists"* вЖТ this is the live **accompanist** (it *plays the accompaniment in sync with your rubato*, a different deliverable from `26`'s bare cursor), and its **online-DTW warping-path render is genuinely lab-first** (grep: no prior prototype draws an alignment path); (b) *"needs mic INPUT, which the JURY bans"* вЖТ it is driven by **MIDI / computer-keyboard / a baked demo, NO microphone**, dodging the ban entirely; (c) *"real-instrument verifiability wall a sandbox can't cross вАФ the thing that killed it 3√Ч"* вЖТ solved exactly the way `358-beat-mirror` solved it, with a **built-in known performance** (see below) that self-proves the whole pipeline with zero hardware. The JURY's own provocation #1 prescribed precisely this ("solve it the way 358 did, with a known internal answer").

Four subsystems: (1) **`dtw.ts`** вАФ **online / streaming Dynamic Time Warping** (Simon Dixon's MATCH lineage, 2005): instead of a full N√ЧM offline matrix it grows the alignment path one note at a time inside a **bounded search window** (`radius 5`) ahead of the last committed column, via the classic 3-predecessor recurrence `D(i,j)=cost(i,j)+min(D(i-1,j),D(i,j-1),D(i-1,j-1))`, keeping only the current/previous frontier rows; commits are **forward-only** (position never jumps backward) so the accompaniment can't stutter. **Local tempo = the slope of the warping path** (steeper than 45¬∞ = rushing, shallower = dragging). (2) **input layer** вАФ one `handlePlayedNote` pipe fed by three doors: the baked **demo**, **computer keyboard** (home-row `a s d f g h j k` вЖТ D-major scale, labels shown), and **Web MIDI** (`navigator.requestMIDIAccess()`, graceful status if absent вАФ never throws). (3) **`audio.ts`** вАФ Web Audio synthesis only: a detuned-oscillator piano voice with a fast-attack/expo-decay env; an optional soft **melody echo** + the star **accompaniment** (functional bass + chord triad in D major, I/V/vi/IV) whose chord decay scales with the slope-derived tempo, so the left hand rings longer when you drag. (4) **`gl.ts`** вАФ raw **WebGL2** with custom shaders plotting reference-score-time (X) √Ч your-performance-time (Y): the glowing **bending warping path**, the faint **45¬∞ in-step guide**, the translucent **bounded search window** band, a pulsing **current-cell cursor**, and expanding **accompaniment fire-rings**, one per chord.

The headline verifiability feature is the **built-in known performance** (`makePerformance()` in `score.ts`): a baked Ode-to-Joy with expressive rubato вАФ a steady intro, an **accelerando** through the middle, a **ritardando** into the final held note, plus deterministic per-note timing jitter вАФ emitted note-by-note on wall-clock timers. The follower never sees the score positions; it only receives pitches over time and must align them. So pressing one button proves the whole pipeline **with no microphone, no MIDI device, no camera**: you watch the warping path bend (steepening in the accelerando, flattening in the ritard), the BPM readout swing, and the accompaniment lock on. Degrades cleanly: no WebGL2 вЖТ readable `text-rose-300` notice, follower + audio still run. No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + MIDI + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Matchmaker вАФ *An Open-source Library for Real-time Piano Score Following and Systematic Evaluation*** (arXiv:2510.10087, Oct 2025; systematically compares online-DTW vs HMM followers) ¬Ј **Simon Dixon, *Live Tracking of Musical Performances Using On-Line Time Warping* / MATCH** (2005). Born from RESEARCH ¬І337. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one concept, three follower algorithms); siblings `374-the-accompanist` (HMM/forward note-event follower + confidence meter, score-river viz) + `376-the-prompter` (cue/anchor FSM robust to wrong notes, build-time-proven 8/8 cues) build-verified and banked in IDEAS ¬І337 as the winner's **next-cycle deepenings**. **Ambition 3/5**: вЙ•3-subsystems(#2, input + online-DTW follower + Web-Audio elastic accompaniment + WebGL2 warping-path) + named-reference(#3, Matchmaker/Dixon) + multi-cycle-commitment(#4, "The Accompanist" thread вАФ cycle-1 ships alignment+warping-path+self-verifying demo; cycle-2 folds in the HMM/confidence follower behind a DTW/HMM toggle + ACCompanion-style expressive dynamics following; cycle-3 maps Karel's own *Welcome Home* melody as the score). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вАФ unverified surface: whether a real fumbly performance keeps the forward-only DTW aligned (a long string of wrong notes could outrun the window-5 search before re-locking), the slopeвЖТtempo blend feel on real rubato, the warping-path legibility at a 06:30 phone glance, and real Web MIDI on hardware (no MIDI/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/375-tempo-canon/README.md`

### 380-expressive-accompanist
**Status**: `demoable` ¬Ј **Cycle shipped**: 339 ¬Ј **Last touched**: 2026-06-07

Open `/dream/380-expressive-accompanist`. **The accompanist that follows your EXPRESSION, not just your tempo.** Press the big violet **"Play demo вЦЄ"** (or play the home-row keys / a Web MIDI keyboard): you play the melody (right hand) of **Pachelbel's Canon in D**, and the system plays the **bass + chord accompaniment (left hand)** that follows you in **three dimensions at once** вАФ it speeds/slows with your *tempo*, swells/fades with your *dynamics* (play soft вЖТ it answers soft), and detaches/sustains with your *articulation* (tap staccato вЖТ its chords go short and crisp; hold legato вЖТ they ring). Cycle 1 of this thread (`375-tempo-canon`) followed only your tempo; this is the deepening that makes the machine feel like a duet partner instead of a backing track.

**Cycle 2 of the lab's "Accompanist" multi-cycle thread**, and the most direct realization of **The ACCompanion**'s central thesis (Cancino-Chac√≥n/Peter/Widmer, IJCAI 2023): *tempo alone sounds mechanical вАФ following dynamics and articulation is what makes an accompanist human.* The lab-first axis is **expressive-parameter coupling**: a grep of every prior README shows no prototype that *follows a soloist's dynamics or articulation* (375 follows only tempo). Three subsystems past the input layer: (1) **`dtw.ts`** вАФ online/streaming **DTW** (Simon Dixon's MATCH, 2005): a bounded forward-only warping path (radius вЙИ5, 3-predecessor recurrence `D(i,j)=cost+min(D(i-1,j),D(i,j-1),D(i-1,j-1))`, frontier rows only) gives *which* score position you're at and a local-tempo slope вАФ extended in cycle 2 with two EMA trackers: `smoothedVelocity` (ќ± 0.35) and `smoothedArticulation` = note-held-duration √Ј inter-onset-interval (ќ± 0.30; ~1 = legato, ~0 = staccato). (2) **`audio.ts`** вАФ Web Audio synthesis only: a detuned-oscillator piano melody echo + the star **accompaniment** (functional bass + chord triads, Pachelbel ground bass I-V-vi-III-IV-I-IV-V) whose **loudness scales with the followed dynamics**, **decay length scales with the followed articulation**, and **chord placement is driven by the DTW tempo**, all summed through a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter so it can never blast. (3) **`gl.ts`** вАФ raw **WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL ES 3.00): the cycle-1 warping path, now wearing an **expression ribbon** вАФ a halo layer whose **point-sprite size encodes live dynamics** (fat glow = loud) and whose **screen-space dashing (`u_dash` uniform) encodes articulation** (solid = legato, dotted = staccato), over a solid cyan-white alignment core; plus a translucent search-window band, a 45¬∞ in-step guide, a pulsing committed-cell cursor, and expanding **fire-rings** on each chord whose size = that chord's loudness. DPR/resize aware.

One unified `handlePlayedNote(pitch, velocity, durationMs)` pipe, three doors: the baked **demo** (default; auto-plays ~2 s after load so the 06:30 phone reviewer sees + hears all three couplings hands-free), **computer keyboard** (home-row `a s d f g h j k` вЖТ a D-major scale, labels shown; hold for legato, tap for staccato), and **Web MIDI** (`navigator.requestMIDIAccess()`, graceful status if absent вАФ never throws; real velocity drives dynamics). The headline verifiability move (the thing that killed score-following 3√Ч before `375` solved it the `358-beat-mirror` way): the baked **"known performance"** (`makePerformance()` in `score.ts`) bakes deliberately wide, *legible* swings in all three axes вАФ a steady intro вЖТ **accelerando** вЖТ big **ritardando**; a **crescendo** (ppвЖТff) then **diminuendo** (ffвЖТmp); a **legato** first half (ratio ~0.85) then a **staccato** second half (ratio ~0.25). The follower never sees score positions вАФ only `(pitch, velocity, durationMs, dt)` tuples вАФ so pressing one button proves the whole tempo+dynamics+articulation pipeline **with no MIDI, no mic, no camera**: the ribbon visibly swells then thins, dashes then re-solidifies, and the accompaniment audibly swells/fades and detaches/sustains to match. Degrades cleanly: no WebGL2 вЖТ readable `text-rose-300` notice, follower + audio still run; no Web MIDI вЖТ status line, keyboard + demo still work; `AudioContext` created inside the Play tap (autoplay-safe, silent catch). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + MIDI + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **The ACCompanion** вАФ Cancino-Chac√≥n, Peter & Widmer, *Combining Reactivity, Robustness, and Musical Expressivity in an Automatic Piano Accompanist* (IJCAI 2023, arXiv:2304.12939; the expressive-coupling north star) ¬Ј **Peransformer** вАФ score-aware-discriminator expressive performance rendering (arXiv:2510.10175, Oct 2025) ¬Ј **Simon Dixon, MATCH** online-DTW (2005). Born from RESEARCH ¬І339. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one concept вАФ *an accompanist that follows your expression* вАФ three technical attacks); siblings `381-resilient-accompanist` (dual DTWвЗДHMM follower with confidence-gated handoff that survives wrong/skipped notes) + `382-anticipating-accompanist` (Kalman-style predictive tempo model that schedules the accompaniment *onto your predicted next onset* so it plays *with* you, not behind) build-verified and banked in IDEAS ¬І339 as the winner's **cycle-3 deepenings** (fold robustness + anticipation into the expressive follower). **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, expressive-parameter following вАФ dynamics + articulation, lab-first) + named-reference(#3, ACCompanion/Peransformer/Dixon) + multi-cycle-commitment(#4, "The Accompanist" thread cycle 2; cycle-3 folds in 381's robustness + 382's anticipation; cycle-4 maps Karel's own *Welcome Home* melody as the score). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вАФ unverified surface: whether the EMA articulation ratio reads cleanly off real (vs baked) playing, the dynamics/decay coupling *feel* through the limiter when chords overlap, the `u_dash` staccato dashing + velocity-sized fire-rings render as intended across GPUs, and real Web MIDI velocity on hardware (no MIDI/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/380-expressive-accompanist/README.md`

### 384-kids-firefly-chorus
**Status**: `demoable` ¬Ј **Cycle shipped**: 340 ¬Ј **Last touched**: 2026-06-07 ¬Ј **For**: kids (4+)

Open `/dream/384-kids-firefly-chorus`. **Tip the world to gather scattered fireflies until they spontaneously blink вАФ and sing вАФ in unison.** A dark dusk meadow holds ~280 fireflies, each blinking out of phase and humming its own slightly-different soft note (a gentle twinkly chaos). **Tilt the device** (or drag a finger; or just watch) to drift fireflies together вАФ and when they cluster they begin to *nudge each other's blink timing* until a gathered group locks into a single pulsing blink and its scattered pitches collapse onto one shared D-Dorian chord. The child *feels chaos becoming harmony* by gathering them; clusters that have synced stay synced. No "wrong", no fail, no score вАФ a meadow that breathes into unison.

The lab-first axis is **spontaneous synchronization** itself: a grep of every prior README shows fireflies as a decorative motif (`122-kids-firefly-song`, `211-kids-firefly-web`, `188-kids-glow-bug`) but **no prototype implements coupled-oscillator phase-locking** вАФ the *emergence* of order from disorder has never been the mechanic. Three subsystems: (1) **`kuramoto.ts`** вАФ a genuine **Kuramoto model**: each firefly is a phase oscillator integrating `dќЄбµҐ/dt = ѕЙбµҐ + (K/NбµҐ)¬Јќ£в±Љ sin(ќЄв±Љ вИТ ќЄбµҐ)` summed over **only its neighbours** within a coupling radius (cheap **spatial-hash grid**, 3√Ч3 cell window, ~280 oscillators at 60fps), flashing when ќЄ crosses 2ѕА; **union-find** clusters the locked groups; it computes both a **per-firefly local order parameter** and the **global Kuramoto order parameter rвИИ[0,1]** (`r¬Јe^{iѕИ} = (1/N)¬Јќ£ e^{iќЄв±Љ}`), surfaced as a legible "togetherness" meter (rвЙИ0 chaos вЖТ rвЙИ1 unison) вАФ the synchronization made *visible*, in the legible/instructional register the jury asked the lab to feed. (2) **`audio.ts`** вАФ Web Audio only: an always-on soft D+A summer-night pad bed (never silent) + **D-Dorian** sine bells (D E F G A B C вАФ explicitly **not** C-major-pentatonic) that, as a neighbourhood's local r rises, blend toward the shared **D-F-A tonic triad**; a 14-voice pool + per-firefly refractory + probability gate so hundreds of flashes never machine-gun; a low-pass tone and a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter so it can never blast; a 12-min goodnight fade. (3) **`gl.ts`** вАФ raw **WebGL2** (GLSL ES 3.00): a dusk-indigo meadow gradient (warm vignette tracks global r) + instanced soft-glow firefly quads in **matte premultiplied alpha-over** (the lab's non-additive house style), DPR/resize aware.

One sim, three input doors, all converging on the same model: **device tilt** (`DeviceOrientationEvent`, iOS `requestPermission()` requested inside the Start tap) вЖТ a gravity vector that drifts the fireflies; a **pointer-drag** "breeze" fallback that drifts them identically when orientation is denied/absent; and an **auto-demo** вАФ after ~3s with no input a slow synthetic wandering breeze herds the meadow into one cluster, so the 06:30 phone reviewer *sees the blinks lock and the togetherness meter climb and hears the chord settle* without touching anything or needing a sensor. Degrades cleanly: orientation denied вЖТ readable `text-rose-300` notice + drag fallback + auto-demo still runs; no WebGL2 вЖТ `text-rose-300` notice (audio still runs); `AudioContext` created inside the Start tap (autoplay-safe). No API route, no `guard` needed (DeviceOrientation + WebGL2 + Web Audio all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Yoshiki Kuramoto (1975)** вАФ the coupled-phase-oscillator model ¬Ј **Christiaan Huygens (1665)** вАФ the original "sympathy of clocks" coupled-pendulum synchronization ¬Ј the spontaneous flash-synchrony of **SE-Asian *Pteroptyx* fireflies** ¬Ј live research front: **arXiv:2603.08352** ("Synchronization of higher-dimensional Kuramoto oscillators on networks," March 2026). Born from RESEARCH ¬І340. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire (three unrelated emergent-system sound worlds, each dodging the jury's banned tags вАФ touch / mic-voice / inline-SVG); siblings `383-kids-coral-garden` (shake вЖТ **Diffusion-Limited Aggregation** coral growth, WittenвАУSander 1981) + `385-kids-ant-garden` (wave at camera вЖТ **ant-colony stigmergy** pheromone-trail song, Deneubourg 1990 / Dorigo 1992) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS ¬І340 вАФ both grep-verified lab-first techniques. **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, coupled-oscillator synchronization) + named-reference(#3, Kuramoto/Huygens) + recent-research(#5, arXiv:2603.08352, Mar 2026). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вАФ unverified surface: the coupling constant K (3.4) + radius (0.13) feel on a real cluster (too strong вЖТ instant lock reads as no-emergence; too weak вЖТ never syncs), the tiltвЖТgravity mapping on a real tilting hand, whether the sync reads as *magic* vs. a glitch at a 06:30 phone glance, and the voice-pool load when many neighbourhoods flash at once (no sensor/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/384-kids-firefly-chorus/README.md`
